    19‚Üí# Product Requirements Document - Metrics
    20‚Üí
    21‚Üí**Author:** Dmytro
    22‚Üí**Date:** 2026-03-25
    23‚Üí
    24‚Üí## Executive Summary
    25‚Üí
    26‚ÜíMetrics is a single-page Bitcoin dashboard built on Astro JS that delivers real-time BTC price data through a polished, dark crypto interface. It solves a specific problem: checking the Bitcoin price today means navigating bloated exchange platforms or news aggregators packed with irrelevant coins, ads, and trading interfaces. Metrics provides the answer in one glance ‚Äî live price via WebSocket, 24-hour change, market cap, and a sparkline chart ‚Äî with zero accounts, zero configuration, and zero noise.
    27‚Üí
    28‚ÜíThe target user is a developer (initially Dmytro) who wants a fast, beautiful, always-open BTC tab. The product is designed around three pillars: **speed** (sub-2s page load, sub-1s price updates), **aesthetics** (dark crypto visual identity with glassmorphism, neon accents, and monospaced typography), and **focus** (one asset, one page, one purpose).
    29‚Üí
    30‚ÜíDeliberate constraint is the feature. Where CoinGecko, CoinMarketCap, and exchange UIs compete on breadth, Metrics competes on depth of simplicity. The core insight: the best dashboard is the one you don't have to think about. It's built for someone who wants to *know* the price, not *trade* it. Astro's Islands Architecture hydrates only the interactive components (price ticker, chart) while the shell stays static HTML ‚Äî a page that loads like a static site but updates like a live app.
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üí## Project Classification
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üí- **Project Type:** Web application (SPA-like with Astro Islands Architecture)
    35‚Üí- **Domain:** Fintech (cryptocurrency data visualization)
    36‚Üí- **Complexity:** Low ‚Äî read-only public API consumption, no user data, no transactions, no regulatory requirements
    37‚Üí- **Project Context:** Greenfield ‚Äî new product, no existing codebase
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üí## Success Criteria
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üí### User Success
    42‚Üí
    43‚Üí- **Instant awareness:** User opens a tab and knows the current BTC price within 1 second ‚Äî no scanning, no clicking, no waiting
    44‚Üí- **Pinned tab worthy:** The dashboard is reliable and beautiful enough to earn a permanent spot in the browser tab bar
    45‚Üí- **Zero learning curve:** No onboarding, no configuration, no accounts ‚Äî it just works on first visit
    46‚Üí
    47‚Üí### Business Success
    48‚Üí
    49‚Üí- **Personal utility:** Dmytro uses Metrics daily as his primary BTC price check, replacing CoinGecko/Google
    50‚Üí- **Craft quality:** The project demonstrates polished frontend engineering ‚Äî suitable for sharing as open-source or a portfolio piece
    51‚Üí- **Deployable:** Can be moved from local dev to public hosting (Vercel/Netlify) with minimal effort when ready
    52‚Üí
    53‚Üí### Technical Success
    54‚Üí
    55‚ÜíSpecific targets defined in Non-Functional Requirements (NFR1‚ÄìNFR17). Key benchmarks:
    56‚Üí
    57‚Üí- Lighthouse performance ‚â• 90, accessibility ‚â• 80
    58‚Üí- WebSocket connection uptime > 99% during active browser sessions
    59‚Üí- Total JS bundle (hydrated islands only) < 100KB gzipped
    60‚Üí- Chart library < 50KB gzipped
    61‚Üí
    62‚Üí## User Journeys
    63‚Üí
    64‚Üí### Journey 1: The Morning Price Check (Happy Path)
    65‚Üí
    66‚Üí**Dmytro, developer, morning coffee ritual.**
    67‚Üí
    68‚ÜíIt's 8:15 AM. Dmytro grabs his coffee and opens his laptop. Among his pinned browser tabs, Metrics is already there. He glances at it ‚Äî **$68,432.17**, green arrow, **+2.3% in 24h**. The sparkline shows a gentle upward trend since yesterday afternoon. Market cap sits at $1.34T. He knows everything he needs to know in under 2 seconds. He moves on to his work. No clicks, no scrolling, no cognitive load. The tab title even shows the price, so sometimes he doesn't even need to switch to it.
    69‚Üí
    70‚Üí**Capabilities revealed:** Real-time price display, 24h change indicator, sparkline chart, market cap, tab title with price, instant page load from pinned tab state.
    71‚Üí
    72‚Üí### Journey 2: The Volatile Day (Edge Case ‚Äî Reconnection & Stale Data)
    73‚Üí
    74‚Üí**Dmytro, mid-afternoon, market is moving fast.**
    75‚Üí
    76‚ÜíBitcoin just dropped 8% in an hour. Dmytro switches to his Metrics tab to watch it move. The price is ticking down live ‚Äî **$62,100... $61,980... $61,850**. Then his WiFi blips. A subtle indicator appears: **"Reconnecting..."** in muted text. Within seconds, the WebSocket reconnects via exponential backoff and the live feed resumes. He didn't have to refresh. If the connection had stayed down longer, the price would have shown a stale-data badge so he'd know not to trust it, and the app would have fallen back to REST polling for updates every 60 seconds.
    77‚Üí
    78‚Üí**Capabilities revealed:** WebSocket reconnection with exponential backoff, stale data visual indicator, REST polling fallback, high-frequency price update rendering without layout shift.
    79‚Üí
    80‚Üí### Journey 3: The Fresh Visit (First-Time Load)
    81‚Üí
    82‚Üí**Dmytro opens Metrics for the first time on a new machine.**
    83‚Üí
    84‚ÜíHe types the local dev URL into his browser. The page loads in under 1.5 seconds ‚Äî dark background fills the screen first (no flash of white), then the static shell renders immediately: labels, card outlines, layout. Within 500ms, the WebSocket connects and the price appears with a smooth fade-in. The sparkline draws itself with a quick animation. No loading spinners, no skeleton screens ‚Äî just a fast, elegant entrance. He bookmarks it, pins the tab, and it becomes part of his daily setup.
    85‚Üí
    86‚Üí**Capabilities revealed:** Fast initial page load (static-first Astro), WebSocket connection initialization, graceful data hydration without layout shift, dark theme renders immediately (no FOUC), smooth entry animations.
    87‚Üí
    88‚Üí### Journey 4: The Mobile Glance (Responsive Edge Case)
    89‚Üí
    90‚Üí**Dmytro, on his phone, quick check while out.**
    91‚Üí
    92‚ÜíHe opens Metrics on his phone browser. The layout stacks vertically ‚Äî price prominently on top, 24h change below, then the sparkline chart fills the width, market cap at the bottom. Everything is readable, touch-friendly, and the dark theme looks sharp on OLED. The WebSocket connects and the price starts updating live. Same experience, smaller screen.
    93‚Üí
    94‚Üí**Capabilities revealed:** Responsive design (desktop-first with mobile stacking), touch-friendly layout, OLED-friendly dark theme, full functionality on mobile browsers.
    95‚Üí
    96‚Üí### Journey Requirements Summary
    97‚Üí
    98‚Üí| Capability | J1 | J2 | J3 | J4 |
    99‚Üí|---|---|---|---|---|
   100‚Üí| Live BTC price via WebSocket | ‚úì | ‚úì | ‚úì | ‚úì |
   101‚Üí| 24h change (% and absolute) | ‚úì | ‚úì | ‚úì | ‚úì |
   102‚Üí| Market cap display | ‚úì | ‚úì | ‚úì | ‚úì |
   103‚Üí| 24h sparkline chart | ‚úì | ‚úì | ‚úì | ‚úì |
   104‚Üí| Tab title with price | ‚úì | | | |
   105‚Üí| WebSocket reconnection | | ‚úì | | |
   106‚Üí| Stale data indicator | | ‚úì | | |
   107‚Üí| REST polling fallback | | ‚úì | | |
   108‚Üí| Fast initial load (< 2s) | | | ‚úì | |
   109‚Üí| Graceful data hydration | | | ‚úì | |
   110‚Üí| No FOUC (dark theme first) | | | ‚úì | |
   111‚Üí| Entry animations | | | ‚úì | |
   112‚Üí| Responsive mobile layout | | | | ‚úì |
   113‚Üí
   114‚Üí## Technical Architecture
   115‚Üí
   116‚Üí### Astro Islands Architecture
   117‚Üí
   118‚ÜíSingle-page Astro JS application. The page shell is static HTML (MPA pattern) with two hydrated client islands: the WebSocket price ticker and the sparkline chart. This gives the performance benefits of a static site with the interactivity of a live dashboard.
   119‚Üí
   120‚Üí- **Rendering model:** Astro static-first with `client:load` directives for interactive islands (price ticker, chart component)
   121‚Üí- **Real-time data:** WebSocket connections managed within hydrated islands only ‚Äî no SSR or server-side WebSocket handling
   122‚Üí- **State management:** Minimal ‚Äî component-local state within each island. No global state store needed. Price data flows: WebSocket ‚Üí island component ‚Üí DOM
   123‚Üí- **Asset strategy:** Tailwind CSS compiled at build time; JetBrains Mono and Inter loaded via `@fontsource` packages or self-hosted for performance
   124‚Üí
   125‚Üí### Browser Support
   126‚Üí
   127‚Üí- Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge (latest 2 versions each)
   128‚Üí- No IE11 or legacy browser support
   129‚Üí- `backdrop-filter` (glassmorphism) supported across all targets ‚Äî no fallback needed
   130‚Üí
   131‚Üí### Accessibility
   132‚Üí
   133‚Üí- Semantic HTML structure (headings, landmarks, ARIA labels where meaningful)
   134‚Üí- Sufficient color contrast for price text against dark backgrounds
   135‚Üí- No formal WCAG compliance target ‚Äî best-effort with sensible defaults
   136‚Üí
   137‚Üí### Implementation Constraints
   138‚Üí
   139‚Üí- **No SEO requirements** ‚Äî personal dashboard, not indexed
   140‚Üí- **No SSR needed** ‚Äî fully static build with client-side hydration
   141‚Üí- **No routing** ‚Äî single page, no client-side router
   142‚Üí- **No backend** ‚Äî all data sourced from public WebSocket and REST APIs client-side
   143‚Üí- **Dev server:** Astro dev server for local development; production build outputs static files
   144‚Üí
   145‚Üí## Project Scoping & Phased Development
   146‚Üí
   147‚Üí### MVP Strategy
   148‚Üí
   149‚Üí**MVP Approach:** Experience MVP ‚Äî the minimum feature set that delivers the full "open tab, know the price" experience with visual polish. No feature stubs or placeholder UI. Everything shipped in v1 works completely and looks finished.
   150‚Üí
   151‚Üí**Resource Requirements:** Solo developer (Dmytro). Astro JS, Tailwind CSS, and TypeScript skills. No backend or DevOps needed for v1.
   152‚Üí
   153‚Üí**Core User Journeys Supported:** All four (J1‚ÄìJ4) ‚Äî full support.
   154‚Üí
   155‚Üí### MVP Feature Set (Phase 1)
   156‚Üí
   157‚Üí- Live BTC/USD price via Binance WebSocket with CoinCap fallback
   158‚Üí- 24h price change (percentage and absolute) via CoinGecko REST
   159‚Üí- Market cap display via CoinGecko REST (polled every 60s)
   160‚Üí- 24h sparkline chart using lightweight-charts (TradingView), hourly data, hover tooltips
   161‚Üí- Dark crypto aesthetic: near-black backgrounds, neon accents, glassmorphism cards, JetBrains Mono / Inter typography
   162‚Üí- Tab title showing current price
   163‚Üí- WebSocket reconnection with exponential backoff
   164‚Üí- Stale data indicator on disconnect
   165‚Üí- REST polling fallback when WebSocket is unavailable
   166‚Üí- Responsive layout (desktop-first, mobile stacking)
   167‚Üí- No FOUC ‚Äî dark theme renders immediately
   168‚Üí- Smooth entry animations for data hydration
   169‚Üí- Local development setup with Astro dev server
   170‚Üí
   171‚Üí### Key Technical Decisions
   172‚Üí
   173‚Üí- **Chart library:** lightweight-charts (TradingView) ‚Äî finance-native, ~40KB gzipped, fits bundle constraint
   174‚Üí- **WebSocket fallback strategy:** Try Binance first, fall back to CoinCap on failure. No auto-detection or geo-check ‚Äî simple sequential fallback acceptable for v1
   175‚Üí- **Binance 24h expiry:** Reconnection logic handles this as a normal disconnect/reconnect cycle
   176‚Üí
   177‚Üí### Phase 2 (Growth)
   178‚Üí
   179‚Üí- Static hosting deployment (Vercel/Netlify) with production build
   180‚Üí- Extended chart timeframes (7d, 30d)
   181‚Üí- Additional fiat currency options (EUR, GBP)
   182‚Üí- PWA support for mobile home screen install
   183‚Üí
   184‚Üí### Phase 3 (Expansion)
   185‚Üí
   186‚Üí- Multi-asset support (ETH, SOL, etc.)
   187‚Üí- Personal portfolio tracking
   188‚Üí- Reusable dark-theme Astro component library
   189‚Üí- Public open-source release
   190‚Üí
   191‚Üí### Risk Mitigation
   192‚Üí
   193‚Üí- *Binance WebSocket geo-blocked:* CoinCap fallback provides equivalent functionality. Acceptable latency trade-off for v1.
   194‚Üí- *CoinGecko rate limiting:* 60s polling interval stays well within 30 req/min free tier. If hit, degrade gracefully by showing last known data with timestamp.
   195‚Üí- *lightweight-charts Astro compatibility:* Library is framework-agnostic vanilla JS ‚Äî low integration risk with Astro client islands.
   196‚Üí- *Resource:* Solo developer project. Tight scope minimizes risk. No external dependencies requiring agreements.
   197‚Üí
   198‚Üí## Functional Requirements
   199‚Üí
   200‚Üí### Real-Time Price Display
   201‚Üí
   202‚Üí- FR1: User can view the current BTC/USD price, updated in real-time
   203‚Üí- FR2: User can see price updates reflected in the browser tab title
   204‚Üí- FR3: User can see a visual indication of price direction (up/down) on each update
   205‚Üí
   206‚Üí### Market Data
   207‚Üí
   208‚Üí- FR4: User can view the 24-hour price change as both a percentage and absolute value
   209‚Üí- FR5: User can distinguish positive and negative price changes via color coding
   210‚Üí- FR6: User can view the current BTC market capitalization
   211‚Üí- FR7: System refreshes supplementary market data (24h change, market cap) periodically without user action
   212‚Üí
   213‚Üí### Price Chart
   214‚Üí
   215‚Üí- FR8: User can view a sparkline chart showing BTC price over the last 24 hours
   216‚Üí- FR9: User can hover over the chart to see the price and time at a specific data point
   217‚Üí- FR10: Chart displays hourly data points for the 24-hour period
   218‚Üí
   219‚Üí### Connection Resilience
   220‚Üí
   221‚Üí- FR11: System connects to the primary WebSocket data source (Binance) on page load
   222‚Üí- FR12: System falls back to a secondary WebSocket source (CoinCap) if the primary fails
   223‚Üí- FR13: System automatically reconnects when a WebSocket connection drops
   224‚Üí- FR14: User can see a visual indicator when the data connection is lost or reconnecting
   225‚Üí- FR15: User can see a stale data indicator when displayed price may be outdated
   226‚Üí- FR16: System falls back to REST API polling when WebSocket is unavailable
   227‚Üí
   228‚Üí### Visual Presentation
   229‚Üí
   230‚Üí- FR17: User sees a dark-themed interface with crypto-aesthetic styling on page load
   231‚Üí- FR18: User experiences no flash of unstyled or light-themed content on initial load
   232‚Üí- FR19: User sees smooth entry animations when data first loads
   233‚Üí- FR20: User sees no layout shift as dynamic content populates
   234‚Üí
   235‚Üí### Responsive Experience
   236‚Üí
   237‚Üí- FR21: User can view all dashboard data on desktop screens with a horizontal layout
   238‚Üí- FR22: User can view all dashboard data on mobile screens with a vertically stacked layout
   239‚Üí- FR23: Dashboard is usable and readable across all supported modern browsers
   240‚Üí
   241‚Üí## Non-Functional Requirements
   242‚Üí
   243‚Üí### Performance
   244‚Üí
   245‚Üí- NFR1: Page load (DOMContentLoaded) completes in < 2 seconds on a 4G connection
   246‚Üí- NFR2: Largest Contentful Paint (LCP) occurs in < 1.5 seconds
   247‚Üí- NFR3: Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS) = 0 ‚Äî no visible layout shift during data hydration
   248‚Üí- NFR4: Price updates render to DOM within 1 second of WebSocket message receipt
   249‚Üí- NFR5: All animations run at 60 FPS with no dropped frames
   250‚Üí- NFR6: Lighthouse performance score ‚â• 90
   251‚Üí- NFR7: Total hydrated JavaScript bundle < 100KB gzipped
   252‚Üí- NFR8: Chart library (lightweight-charts) < 50KB gzipped
   253‚Üí
   254‚Üí### Integration
   255‚Üí
   256‚Üí- NFR9: Binance WebSocket connection establishes within 500ms of island hydration under normal network conditions
   257‚Üí- NFR10: WebSocket reconnection uses exponential backoff starting at 1 second, capping at 30 seconds
   258‚Üí- NFR11: CoinGecko REST API polling interval ‚â• 60 seconds to stay within free tier rate limits (30 req/min)
   259‚Üí- NFR12: System tolerates CoinGecko API unavailability by displaying last known data with a timestamp
   260‚Üí- NFR13: Binance 24-hour connection expiry handled transparently as a standard reconnect cycle
   261‚Üí- NFR14: Fallback from Binance to CoinCap completes without user intervention
   262‚Üí
   263‚Üí### Reliability
   264‚Üí
   265‚Üí- NFR15: Dashboard remains functional (displays last known data) when all external APIs are unreachable
   266‚Üí- NFR16: No JavaScript errors or white screens on WebSocket disconnection
   267‚Üí- NFR17: Browser tab remains stable during extended sessions (no memory leaks over 24+ hours)
   268‚Üí

    16‚Üí---
    17‚Üí
    18‚Üí# Architecture Decision Document
    19‚Üí
    20‚Üí_This document builds collaboratively through step-by-step discovery. Sections are appended as we work through each architectural decision together._
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí## Project Context Analysis
    23‚Üí
    24‚Üí### Requirements Overview
    25‚Üí
    26‚Üí**Functional Requirements:**
    27‚Üí23 FRs across 6 categories. The dominant category is Connection Resilience (FR11‚ÄìFR16) ‚Äî 6 requirements governing WebSocket lifecycle, multi-source fallback, and stale data handling. This is the most architecturally significant area despite the project's low overall complexity. The remaining FRs cover straightforward data display (price, change, market cap, chart) and visual presentation (dark theme, animations, responsive layout).
    28‚Üí
    29‚Üí**Non-Functional Requirements:**
    30‚Üí17 NFRs with specific quantified targets. Performance NFRs (1‚Äì8) define a tight bundle budget (<100KB JS, <50KB chart lib) and strict rendering metrics (CLS = 0, LCP <1.5s, 60 FPS). Integration NFRs (9‚Äì14) specify WebSocket connection behavior (500ms connect, exponential backoff 1s‚Äì30s, 60s REST polling). Reliability NFRs (15‚Äì17) require graceful degradation and no memory leaks over 24h+ sessions.
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üí**Scale & Complexity:**
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üí- Primary domain: Frontend web ‚Äî real-time data visualization
    35‚Üí- Complexity level: Low
    36‚Üí- Estimated architectural components: 5 UI components + 1 WebSocket service layer + 1 REST polling service + 1 shared theme constants module
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üí### Technical Constraints & Dependencies
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üí- **Framework locked:** Astro JS with Islands Architecture ‚Äî non-negotiable
    41‚Üí- **Styling locked:** Tailwind CSS with custom semantic tokens
    42‚Üí- **Chart library:** lightweight-charts (TradingView) ‚Äî chosen for finance-native API, ~40KB gzipped
    43‚Üí- **External API dependencies (no auth required):**
    44‚Üí  - Binance WebSocket (primary real-time) ‚Äî 24h connection expiry, potential geo-restrictions
    45‚Üí  - CoinCap WebSocket (fallback real-time) ‚Äî simpler, universal access
    46‚Üí  - CoinGecko REST (supplementary data) ‚Äî 30 req/min free tier, 60s polling interval
    47‚Üí- **No backend, no SSR, no routing, no SEO, no user data storage**
    48‚Üí- **Browser targets:** Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge (latest 2 versions) ‚Äî backdrop-filter supported across all
    49‚Üí
    50‚Üí### Cross-Cutting Concerns Identified
    51‚Üí
    52‚Üí1. **WebSocket connection state** ‚Äî Shared across Price Card, Status Indicator, and fallback logic. Needs a unified connection manager that multiple islands can observe.
    53‚Üí2. **Design token consistency** ‚Äî Tailwind config tokens must stay in sync with lightweight-charts JS API colors. Solved via shared `theme-constants.ts` file.
    54‚Üí3. **Stale data handling** ‚Äî Affects Price Card (badge display), Status Indicator (state text), and tab title (should it show stale price?). Needs a consistent staleness threshold (>30s).
    55‚Üí4. **Animation/motion standards** ‚Äî Consistent timing (300ms/400ms/500ms), easing curves, and `prefers-reduced-motion` support across all components.
    56‚Üí5. **Number formatting** ‚Äî USD formatting with comma separators, abbreviated market cap, consistent decimal places across all data displays.
    57‚Üí
    58‚Üí## Starter Template Evaluation
    59‚Üí
    60‚Üí### Primary Technology Domain
    61‚Üí
    62‚ÜíFrontend web ‚Äî single-page real-time dashboard built on Astro Islands Architecture.
    63‚Üí
    64‚Üí### Starter Options Considered
    65‚Üí
    66‚Üí| Option | Template | Fit | Verdict |
    67‚Üí|---|---|---|---|
    68‚Üí| A | `create astro -- --template minimal` | Perfect ‚Äî clean slate, zero cruft | **Selected** |
    69‚Üí| B | Astroplate / AstroWind / Astroship | Poor ‚Äî blog/content-site scaffolding to remove | Rejected |
    70‚Üí| C | Manual setup | No advantage over CLI wizard | Rejected |
    71‚Üí
    72‚Üí### Selected Starter: Astro Minimal + CLI Integrations
    73‚Üí
    74‚Üí**Rationale:** The project has exactly 1 page, 5 components, and no content management. Any opinionated starter adds complexity to remove. The minimal template + `astro add` commands give us the exact stack specified in the PRD with zero waste.
    75‚Üí
    76‚Üí**Initialization Commands:**
    77‚Üí
    78‚Üí```bash
    79‚Üínpm create astro@latest -- --template minimal --typescript strict
    80‚Üínpx astro add tailwind
    81‚Üínpm install lightweight-charts @fontsource/jetbrains-mono @fontsource/inter
    82‚Üínpm install -D eslint prettier eslint-plugin-astro prettier-plugin-astro
    83‚Üínpm install -D vitest
    84‚Üí```
    85‚Üí
    86‚Üí**Package manager:** npm (explicit choice ‚Äî simplest for solo developer, no pnpm/yarn complexity needed).
    87‚Üí
    88‚Üí### Architectural Decisions Provided by Starter
    89‚Üí
    90‚Üí**Language & Runtime:**
    91‚Üí- Astro 6.x (current stable 6.0.8) ‚Äî requires Node 22+
    92‚Üí- TypeScript in strict mode ‚Äî catches type errors early, appropriate for intermediate developer
    93‚Üí- Astro's built-in TypeScript support (no separate tsconfig needed beyond Astro defaults)
    94‚Üí- Note: Astro 6 is a recent stable release. Headline features (redesigned dev server, CSP APIs, live content collections) aren't needed for this project ‚Äî we're on Astro 6 because `create astro@latest` gives us Astro 6. Acceptable early-adopter risk for a personal project with no user data.
    95‚Üí
    96‚Üí**Styling Solution:**
    97‚Üí- Tailwind CSS v4 via `@tailwindcss/vite` plugin
    98‚Üí- CSS-first configuration: `@theme` directives in `src/styles/global.css` replace `tailwind.config.js` for token definition
    99‚Üí- Design tokens (`accent-primary`, `bg-base`, `text-muted`, etc.) defined as CSS custom properties via `@theme`
   100‚Üí- **Tailwind v4 ‚Üî lightweight-charts token strategy:** Colors needed by the lightweight-charts JS API cannot be read from CSS `@theme` at import time. For v1, duplicate color values in a `theme-constants.ts` file ‚Äî acceptable sync risk for a solo developer with 5 color values. Refactor to runtime `getComputedStyle()` reads if a theme switcher is ever added.
   101‚Üí
   102‚Üí**Build Tooling:**
   103‚Üí- Vite (bundled with Astro) ‚Äî HMR, tree-shaking, optimized production builds
   104‚Üí- Astro's static output mode (`output: 'static'`) ‚Äî generates pure HTML/CSS/JS files
   105‚Üí- Automatic CSS purging via Tailwind v4
   106‚Üí
   107‚Üí**Code Quality:**
   108‚Üí- ESLint with `eslint-plugin-astro` for Astro-aware linting
   109‚Üí- Prettier with `prettier-plugin-astro` for consistent formatting
   110‚Üí- Configured from first commit ‚Äî important if project becomes open-source in Phase 3
   111‚Üí
   112‚Üí**Testing Framework:**
   113‚Üí- Vitest included in dev dependencies from project init
   114‚Üí- Recommended test strategy for v1: unit tests for WebSocket manager and number formatting utilities, single Playwright smoke test for "page loads, price appears"
   115‚Üí- Note: if chart config uses runtime `getComputedStyle()` in future, CSS values won't be available in Node.js test environment ‚Äî the duplicated `theme-constants.ts` approach keeps test imports trivial
   116‚Üí
   117‚Üí**Code Organization:**
   118‚Üí- `src/pages/` ‚Äî single `index.astro` page
   119‚Üí- `src/components/` ‚Äî Astro components (islands)
   120‚Üí- `src/layouts/` ‚Äî page layout wrapper
   121‚Üí- `src/styles/` ‚Äî global CSS with Tailwind `@import` and `@theme` tokens
   122‚Üí- `src/lib/` ‚Äî shared utilities (`theme-constants.ts`, formatting helpers, WebSocket manager)
   123‚Üí
   124‚Üí**Development Experience:**
   125‚Üí- Astro dev server with HMR
   126‚Üí- TypeScript type checking
   127‚Üí- Tailwind CSS IntelliSense (VS Code extension)
   128‚Üí
   129‚Üí**Note:** Project initialization using these commands should be the first implementation story.
   130‚Üí
   131‚Üí## Core Architectural Decisions
   132‚Üí
   133‚Üí### Decision Priority Analysis
   134‚Üí
   135‚Üí**Critical Decisions (Block Implementation):**
   136‚Üí1. Inter-island state management ‚Äî Nano Stores
   137‚Üí2. Island UI framework ‚Äî Vanilla TypeScript (no framework)
   138‚Üí3. WebSocket manager architecture ‚Äî single manager, multiple consumers via stores
   139‚Üí
   140‚Üí**Important Decisions (Shape Architecture):**
   141‚Üí4. Message normalization ‚Äî common `PriceTick` interface across data sources
   142‚Üí5. Stale data threshold ‚Äî 30 seconds
   143‚Üí
   144‚Üí**Deferred Decisions (Post-MVP):**
   145‚Üí- Hosting provider selection (Phase 2)
   146‚Üí- CI/CD pipeline (Phase 2)
   147‚Üí- PWA configuration (Phase 2)
   148‚Üí- Component library extraction (Phase 3)
   149‚Üí
   150‚Üí### Data Architecture
   151‚Üí
   152‚ÜíNo database. No persistent storage. All data is ephemeral ‚Äî sourced from external APIs and held in memory via Nano Stores.
   153‚Üí
   154‚Üí**Data flow:**
   155‚Üí```
   156‚ÜíBinance WebSocket ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚Üí ConnectionManager ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚Üí Nano Store (priceStore) ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚Üí Price Card island
   157‚ÜíCoinCap WebSocket ‚îÄ‚îò  (fallback)           Nano Store (statusStore) ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚Üí Status Indicator island
   158‚Üí                                            Nano Store (priceStore) ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚Üí Tab title updater
   159‚Üí
   160‚ÜíCoinGecko REST ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚Üí MarketDataService ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚Üí Nano Store (marketStore) ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚Üí Market Cap Card island
   161‚Üí                     (60s polling)         Nano Store (chartStore)  ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚Üí Chart Card island
   162‚Üí```
   163‚Üí
   164‚Üí**Data interfaces:**
   165‚Üí- `PriceTick`: `{ price: number, timestamp: number, direction: 'up' | 'down' | 'neutral' }` ‚Äî normalized from both Binance and CoinCap formats
   166‚Üí- `MarketData`: `{ marketCap: number, change24h: number, changePercent24h: number, high24h: number, low24h: number }` ‚Äî from CoinGecko REST
   167‚Üí- `ChartPoint`: `{ time: number, value: number }` ‚Äî hourly price points for lightweight-charts
   168‚Üí- `ConnectionStatus`: `'connecting' | 'live' | 'reconnecting' | 'stale' | 'fallback'`
   169‚Üí
   170‚Üí### Authentication & Security
   171‚Üí
   172‚ÜíNot applicable. No user accounts, no API keys, no sensitive data. All external APIs are public and keyless.
   173‚Üí
   174‚Üí**Client-side security considerations:**
   175‚Üí- No user input to sanitize (read-only dashboard)
   176‚Üí- WebSocket connections use `wss://` (TLS encrypted)
   177‚Üí- No localStorage/sessionStorage usage ‚Äî no data persistence to protect
   178‚Üí- Content Security Policy: consider adding CSP headers when deploying (Phase 2) to restrict WebSocket origins
   179‚Üí
   180‚Üí### API & Communication Patterns
   181‚Üí
   182‚Üí**WebSocket Manager (`src/lib/connection-manager.ts`):**
   183‚Üí- Singleton pattern ‚Äî one instance manages the entire WebSocket lifecycle
   184‚Üí- Fallback chain: Binance `wss://stream.binance.com:9443/ws/btcusdt@trade` ‚Üí CoinCap `wss://ws.coincap.io/prices?assets=bitcoin`
   185‚Üí- Reconnection: exponential backoff starting at 1s, doubling to max 30s cap
   186‚Üí- 24h expiry: Binance hard-expires connections at 24 hours ‚Äî handled as a standard reconnect cycle
   187‚Üí- Stale threshold: 30 seconds without a price update ‚Üí `ConnectionStatus` transitions to `'stale'`
   188‚Üí- Publishes normalized `PriceTick` to `priceStore` (Nano Store)
   189‚Üí- Publishes `ConnectionStatus` to `statusStore` (Nano Store)
   190‚Üí
   191‚Üí**REST Polling Service (`src/lib/market-data-service.ts`):**
   192‚Üí- Polls CoinGecko REST API every 60 seconds (within 30 req/min free tier)
   193‚Üí- Fetches: market cap, 24h change, 24h high/low, hourly historical data for chart
   194‚Üí- On API failure: retains last known data, does not clear stores
   195‚Üí- Publishes to `marketStore` and `chartStore` (Nano Stores)
   196‚Üí
   197‚Üí**Error Handling Standards:**
   198‚Üí- WebSocket errors ‚Üí silent reconnection via backoff, status indicator updates
   199‚Üí- REST errors ‚Üí retain stale data, log to console, no user-facing error UI
   200‚Üí- No thrown exceptions reaching the UI ‚Äî all error states expressed via store values
   201‚Üí- Console logging for debugging only ‚Äî no external error reporting service in v1
   202‚Üí
   203‚Üí### Frontend Architecture
   204‚Üí
   205‚Üí**State Management: Nano Stores**
   206‚Üí- Decision: Nano Stores (~1KB) as the reactive state layer shared across Astro islands
   207‚Üí- Rationale: Astro's officially recommended approach for inter-island communication. Reactive, tiny, framework-agnostic.
   208‚Üí- Stores:
   209‚Üí  - `priceStore` ‚Äî current `PriceTick` (written by ConnectionManager)
   210‚Üí  - `statusStore` ‚Äî current `ConnectionStatus` (written by ConnectionManager)
   211‚Üí  - `marketStore` ‚Äî current `MarketData` (written by MarketDataService)
   212‚Üí  - `chartStore` ‚Äî array of `ChartPoint` (written by MarketDataService)
   213‚Üí
   214‚Üí**Island UI Framework: Vanilla TypeScript**
   215‚Üí- Decision: No UI framework. Islands use Astro `<script>` tags with `client:load` for direct DOM updates.
   216‚Üí- Rationale: Islands perform simple operations (update text, toggle classes, call lightweight-charts API). Framework overhead adds bundle weight without proportional value. Keeps total JS well within <100KB budget.
   217‚Üí
   218‚Üí**Component Architecture:**
   219‚Üí- 5 Astro components as defined in UX spec: Page Shell (static), Price Card (island), Market Cap Card (island), Chart Card (island), Status Indicator (island)
   220‚Üí- Each island subscribes to relevant Nano Stores and updates DOM directly
   221‚Üí- No component nesting beyond page ‚Üí island ‚Äî flat hierarchy
   222‚Üí
   223‚Üí**Bundle Optimization:**
   224‚Üí- Astro static output ‚Äî only island JS ships to client
   225‚Üí- Nano Stores: ~1KB gzipped
   226‚Üí- lightweight-charts: ~40KB gzipped
   227‚Üí- @fontsource fonts: self-hosted, subset to Latin characters
   228‚Üí- Estimated total hydrated JS: ~50-60KB gzipped (well within 100KB budget)
   229‚Üí
   230‚Üí### Infrastructure & Deployment
   231‚Üí
   232‚Üí**v1: Local Development Only**
   233‚Üí- `npm run dev` ‚Äî Astro dev server with HMR
   234‚Üí- No deployment pipeline, no hosting configuration
   235‚Üí- Environment configuration: API endpoints as hardcoded constants in `src/lib/` (all public, no secrets)
   236‚Üí
   237‚Üí**Phase 2 Deployment Path (documented, not implemented):**
   238‚Üí- Target: Vercel or Netlify (static hosting)
   239‚Üí- Build: `npm run build` ‚Üí `dist/` directory with static HTML/CSS/JS
   240‚Üí- No server-side requirements ‚Äî pure static deployment
   241‚Üí- Consider moving API endpoints to `.env` if multiple environments needed
   242‚Üí
   243‚Üí### Decision Impact Analysis
   244‚Üí
   245‚Üí**Implementation Sequence:**
   246‚Üí1. Project initialization (starter commands)
   247‚Üí2. Tailwind `@theme` token configuration + `theme-constants.ts`
   248‚Üí3. Nano Stores setup (4 stores with TypeScript interfaces)
   249‚Üí4. ConnectionManager (WebSocket lifecycle + fallback chain)
   250‚Üí5. MarketDataService (REST polling)
   251‚Üí6. Page Shell (static layout grid)
   252‚Üí7. Price Card island (subscribes to priceStore + statusStore)
   253‚Üí8. Status Indicator island (subscribes to statusStore)
   254‚Üí9. Market Cap Card island (subscribes to marketStore)
   255‚Üí10. Chart Card island (subscribes to chartStore, configures lightweight-charts)
   256‚Üí
   257‚Üí**Cross-Component Dependencies:**
   258‚Üí- ConnectionManager ‚Üí priceStore, statusStore (writes)
   259‚Üí- MarketDataService ‚Üí marketStore, chartStore (writes)
   260‚Üí- Price Card ‚Üí priceStore, statusStore (reads)
   261‚Üí- Market Cap Card ‚Üí marketStore (reads)
   262‚Üí- Chart Card ‚Üí chartStore (reads)
   263‚Üí- Status Indicator ‚Üí statusStore (reads)
   264‚Üí- theme-constants.ts ‚Üí Chart Card (color configuration)

   266‚Üí## Implementation Patterns & Consistency Rules
   267‚Üí
   268‚Üí### Critical Conflict Points Identified
   269‚Üí
   270‚Üí12 areas where AI agents could make different choices, organized into 5 categories. All patterns below are mandatory for implementation consistency.
   271‚Üí
   272‚Üí### Naming Patterns
   273‚Üí
   274‚Üí**File Naming:**
   275‚Üí- Astro components: PascalCase ‚Äî `PriceCard.astro`, `ChartCard.astro`, `StatusIndicator.astro`, `PageShell.astro`
   276‚Üí- TypeScript modules: kebab-case ‚Äî `connection-manager.ts`, `market-data-service.ts`, `theme-constants.ts`
   277‚Üí- Store files: kebab-case ‚Äî `price-store.ts`, `status-store.ts`, `market-store.ts`, `chart-store.ts`
   278‚Üí- Test files: co-located with source, `.test.ts` suffix ‚Äî `connection-manager.test.ts`, `format-utils.test.ts`
   279‚Üí- CSS: single global file ‚Äî `src/styles/global.css`
   280‚Üí
   281‚Üí**Code Naming:**
   282‚Üí- Variables and functions: camelCase ‚Äî `priceStore`, `formatCurrency()`, `handlePriceTick()`
   283‚Üí- Types and interfaces: PascalCase ‚Äî `PriceTick`, `MarketData`, `ConnectionStatus`
   284‚Üí- Constants: UPPER_SNAKE_CASE ‚Äî `STALE_THRESHOLD_MS`, `WS_BINANCE_URL`, `REST_POLL_INTERVAL_MS`
   285‚Üí- Store names: camelCase with `Store` suffix ‚Äî `priceStore`, `statusStore`, `marketStore`, `chartStore`
   286‚Üí- Event handlers: `handle` + noun + verb pattern ‚Äî `handlePriceUpdate()`, `handleConnectionDrop()`
   287‚Üí
   288‚Üí**CSS/Tailwind Naming:**
   289‚Üí- Semantic token names in `@theme`: kebab-case with category prefix ‚Äî `--color-accent-primary`, `--color-bg-base`, `--color-text-muted`
   290‚Üí- `@apply` compositions (max 3): `.card-glass`, `.price-text`, `.status-badge`
   291‚Üí- Astro scoped style keyframe names: kebab-case ‚Äî `@keyframes price-flash`, `@keyframes fade-in`
   292‚Üí
   293‚Üí### Structure Patterns
   294‚Üí
   295‚Üí**Project Organization:**
   296‚Üí```
   297‚Üísrc/
   298‚Üí‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ pages/

   300‚Üí‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ layouts/

   302‚Üí‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ components/

   304‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ MarketCapCard.astro      # Hydrated island
   305‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ ChartCard.astro          # Hydrated island
   306‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ StatusIndicator.astro    # Hydrated island
   307‚Üí‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ lib/
   308‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ stores/

   310‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ status-store.ts
   311‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ market-store.ts
   312‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ chart-store.ts
   313‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ services/
   314‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ connection-manager.ts
   315‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ market-data-service.ts
   316‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ utils/

   318‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ theme-constants.ts       # Shared color values for lightweight-charts

   322‚Üí
   323‚Üí**Rules:**
   324‚Üí- Components in `src/components/` ‚Äî flat, no subdirectories (only 4 components)
   325‚Üí- Business logic in `src/lib/services/` ‚Äî never in components
   326‚Üí- Stores in `src/lib/stores/` ‚Äî one file per store
   327‚Üí- Utility functions in `src/lib/utils/` ‚Äî pure functions only, no side effects
   328‚Üí- Tests co-located: `src/lib/services/connection-manager.test.ts` next to source file
   329‚Üí
   330‚Üí### Store Patterns
   331‚Üí
   332‚Üí**Nano Store conventions:**
   333‚Üí
   334‚Üí```typescript
   335‚Üí// Store definition pattern (every store file follows this)
   336‚Üíimport { atom } from 'nanostores';
   337‚Üíimport type { PriceTick } from '../types';
   338‚Üí
   339‚Üíexport const priceStore = atom<PriceTick | null>(null);
   340‚Üí```
   341‚Üí
   342‚Üí**Rules:**
   343‚Üí- All stores initialize to `null` (not undefined, not default objects)
   344‚Üí- Only services write to stores ‚Äî never components
   345‚Üí- Components subscribe in `<script>` blocks using `store.subscribe()`
   346‚Üí- One store per concern ‚Äî never combine unrelated data in a single store
   347‚Üí- Type every store with explicit generic: `atom<PriceTick | null>`
   348‚Üí
   349‚Üí### Format Patterns
   350‚Üí
   351‚Üí**Number Formatting (all handled by `format-utils.ts`):**
   352‚Üí- Price: `$68,432.17` ‚Äî USD, comma separators, always 2 decimal places
   353‚Üí- Percentage: `+2.3%` or `-1.8%` ‚Äî sign always shown, 1 decimal place
   354‚Üí- Absolute change: `+$1,534.22` or `-$892.40` ‚Äî sign + USD, 2 decimal places
   355‚Üí- Market cap: `$1.34T` or `$892.45B` ‚Äî abbreviated with 2 decimal places
   356‚Üí- 24h range: `$66.9k ‚Äî $68.9k` ‚Äî abbreviated with 1 decimal place
   357‚Üí- Chart tooltip: `$68,432.17 at 14:30` ‚Äî full price + 24h time (no date, no seconds)
   358‚Üí
   359‚Üí**Rules:**
   360‚Üí- All formatting goes through `format-utils.ts` helper functions ‚Äî never inline formatting in components
   361‚Üí- Use `Intl.NumberFormat` as the base ‚Äî no manual string building
   362‚Üí- Direction indicators always pair symbol with color: `‚Üë` green, `‚Üì` red, `‚Üí` white (neutral)
   363‚Üí- Never use color alone to communicate direction
   364‚Üí
   365‚Üí### Communication Patterns
   366‚Üí
   367‚Üí**Store update flow (unidirectional):**
   368‚Üí```
   369‚ÜíExternal API ‚Üí Service ‚Üí Store ‚Üí Component ‚Üí DOM
   370‚Üí```
   371‚Üí
   372‚Üí**Rules:**
   373‚Üí- Services are the only writers to stores
   374‚Üí- Components are read-only subscribers ‚Äî never call `store.set()` from a component
   375‚Üí- No component-to-component communication ‚Äî always go through stores
   376‚Üí- No direct DOM manipulation outside of store subscription callbacks in island `<script>` blocks
   377‚Üí
   378‚Üí**WebSocket message handling:**
   379‚Üí- Raw messages are parsed and normalized in `connection-manager.ts` ‚Äî components never see raw WebSocket data
   380‚Üí- Normalization happens before store write ‚Äî stores only contain clean `PriceTick` objects
   381‚Üí- Binance format: `{ p: "68432.17", ... }` ‚Üí `{ price: 68432.17, timestamp: Date.now(), direction: 'up' }`
   382‚Üí- CoinCap format: `{ bitcoin: "68432.17" }` ‚Üí same normalized `PriceTick`
   383‚Üí
   384‚Üí### Process Patterns
   385‚Üí
   386‚Üí**Error Handling:**
   387‚Üí- WebSocket errors: catch silently, update `statusStore` to `'reconnecting'`, trigger backoff ‚Äî no console.error for expected disconnects
   388‚Üí- REST errors: catch silently, retain last store values, `console.warn()` with endpoint and status code
   389‚Üí- Parsing errors (malformed API response): `console.error()` with raw payload, skip the update, do not crash the island
   390‚Üí- Never throw from a store subscription callback ‚Äî wrap in try/catch
   391‚Üí- Never show error UI to the user ‚Äî the UX spec explicitly avoids error states. Use stale indicators instead.
   392‚Üí
   393‚Üí**Initialization sequence:**
   394‚Üí- Services initialize in `Layout.astro` via a single `<script>` block that imports and starts `ConnectionManager` and `MarketDataService`
   395‚Üí- Islands hydrate via `client:load` and immediately subscribe to relevant stores
   396‚Üí- Order doesn't matter ‚Äî stores start as `null`, components render empty state, data appears when stores update
   397‚Üí
   398‚Üí**Animation implementation:**
   399‚Üí- All durations as constants in a shared location (CSS custom properties or `theme-constants.ts`)
   400‚Üí- Price flash: CSS transition on `color` property, triggered by toggling a data attribute (`data-flash="up"`)
   401‚Üí- Entry fade-in: CSS transition on `opacity`, triggered by removing a `data-loading` attribute
   402‚Üí- Chart draw: lightweight-charts built-in animation API
   403‚Üí- Always check `prefers-reduced-motion` ‚Äî if enabled, set all durations to 0ms
   404‚Üí
   405‚Üí### Enforcement Guidelines
   406‚Üí
   407‚Üí**All AI Agents MUST:**
   408‚Üí1. Follow the file naming and project structure patterns exactly ‚Äî no alternative organizations
   409‚Üí2. Use `format-utils.ts` for all number display ‚Äî no inline `toFixed()` or manual formatting
   410‚Üí3. Write to stores only from services, read from stores only in components ‚Äî no exceptions
   411‚Üí4. Normalize all external API data before writing to stores ‚Äî components never parse raw responses
   412‚Üí5. Co-locate test files with source files using `.test.ts` suffix
   413‚Üí
   414‚Üí**Anti-Patterns (never do these):**
   415‚Üí- Creating a `utils/index.ts` barrel file ‚Äî import directly from specific modules
   416‚Üí- Adding a UI framework "just for one component" ‚Äî all islands use vanilla TS
   417‚Üí- Storing derived data in stores ‚Äî compute in components (e.g., don't store formatted strings)
   418‚Üí- Using `setInterval` for REST polling ‚Äî use recursive `setTimeout` to prevent drift on slow responses
   419‚Üí- Adding loading spinners or skeleton screens ‚Äî the empty card with labels IS the loading state
   420‚Üí

   482‚Üí- `src/layouts/Layout.astro` ‚Äî page shell (sets `<html>` bg color, loads fonts, initializes services via `<script>`, defines CSS grid)
   483‚Üí
   484‚Üí**Types:**
   485‚Üí- `src/lib/types.ts` ‚Äî all shared TypeScript interfaces: `PriceTick`, `MarketData`, `ChartPoint`, `ConnectionStatus`
   486‚Üí
   487‚Üí### Architectural Boundaries
   488‚Üí
   489‚Üí**External API Boundaries:**
   490‚Üí
   491‚Üí| Boundary | Service | Protocol | Direction |
   492‚Üí|---|---|---|---|
   493‚Üí| Binance | `connection-manager.ts` | WebSocket (`wss://`) | Inbound stream |
   494‚Üí| CoinCap | `connection-manager.ts` | WebSocket (`wss://`) | Inbound stream (fallback) |
   495‚Üí| CoinGecko | `market-data-service.ts` | REST (`https://`) | Outbound polling (60s) |
   496‚Üí
   497‚ÜíAll external communication is isolated in `src/lib/services/`. No component ever makes a network request directly.
   498‚Üí
   499‚Üí**Component Boundaries:**
   500‚Üí
   501‚Üí```
   502‚ÜíLayout.astro (static shell)
   503‚Üí‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ PriceCard.astro (island, client:load)
   504‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ reads: priceStore, statusStore
   505‚Üí‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ MarketCapCard.astro (island, client:load)
   506‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ reads: marketStore
   507‚Üí‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ ChartCard.astro (island, client:load)
   508‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ reads: chartStore
   509‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ imports: theme-constants.ts, lightweight-charts
   510‚Üí‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ StatusIndicator.astro (island, client:load)
   511‚Üí    ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ reads: statusStore
   512‚Üí```
   513‚Üí
   514‚Üí**Data Boundary:**
   515‚Üí- No persistent storage ‚Äî all data is in-memory Nano Stores
   516‚Üí- Stores are the single source of truth between services and components
   517‚Üí- Services write, components read ‚Äî never the reverse

   541‚Üí### Integration Points
   542‚Üí
   543‚Üí**Internal Communication:**
   544‚ÜíAll inter-component communication flows through Nano Stores. No direct imports between components. No DOM events for data passing. The store is the only integration point.
   545‚Üí
   546‚Üí**External Integrations:**
   547‚Üí
   548‚Üí| Integration | Endpoint | Rate | Error Strategy |
   549‚Üí|---|---|---|---|
   550‚Üí| Binance WS | `wss://stream.binance.com:9443/ws/btcusdt@trade` | Continuous | Fallback to CoinCap |
   551‚Üí| CoinCap WS | `wss://ws.coincap.io/prices?assets=bitcoin` | Continuous | Fallback to REST polling |
   552‚Üí| CoinGecko REST | `https://api.coingecko.com/api/v3/...` | Every 60s | Retain last known data |

   556‚Üí[Binance WS] ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚Üí connection-manager.ts ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚Üí normalize ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚Üí priceStore ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚Üí PriceCard (DOM update)
   557‚Üí                                                                      ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚Üí Layout (tab title)
   558‚Üí                                        ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚Üí statusStore ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚Üí StatusIndicator (DOM update)
   559‚Üí                                                         ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚Üí PriceCard (stale badge)
   560‚Üí
   561‚Üí[CoinGecko REST] ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚Üí market-data-service.ts ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚Üí marketStore ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚Üí MarketCapCard (DOM update)
   562‚Üí                                             ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚Üí chartStore  ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚Üí ChartCard (chart update)
   563‚Üí```

   565‚Üí### Development Workflow

   567‚Üí**Dev Server:** `npm run dev` ‚Äî Astro dev server on `localhost:4321`, HMR for all files

   569‚Üí**E2E Test:** `npx playwright test` ‚Äî single smoke test: page loads, price element appears
   570‚Üí**Build:** `npm run build` ‚Äî outputs static files to `dist/`

   574‚Üí
   575‚Üí### Coherence Validation ‚úÖ
   576‚Üí
   577‚Üí**Decision Compatibility:**
   578‚ÜíAll technology choices are compatible and version-verified:
   579‚Üí- Astro 6.x + Tailwind CSS v4 (`@tailwindcss/vite`) ‚Äî officially supported since Astro 5.2+
   580‚Üí- Nano Stores + Astro vanilla TS islands ‚Äî Astro's recommended inter-island communication
   581‚Üí- lightweight-charts + vanilla TS ‚Äî framework-agnostic imperative API, no wrapper needed
   582‚Üí- Vitest + Astro ‚Äî recommended by Astro docs
   583‚Üí- TypeScript strict mode ‚Äî no conflicts with any dependency
   584‚Üí
   585‚ÜíNo contradictory decisions found.

   590‚Üí- Error handling approach (silent recovery, stale indicators) consistent with UX spec's "never show error UI" principle
   591‚Üí- Store patterns (null init, typed generics, service-only writes) applied uniformly
   592‚Üí
   593‚Üí**Structure Alignment:**
   594‚Üí- Every architectural decision maps to a specific file in the project tree
   595‚Üí- Boundaries are clean: services, stores, components, and utils each have dedicated directories

   622‚Üí
   623‚Üí### Gap Analysis Results
   624‚Üí
   625‚Üí**Gap Found: NFR17 ‚Äî Memory Leak Prevention (24h+ Sessions)**
   626‚Üí
   627‚ÜíPriority: Important ‚Äî addressed below.
   628‚Üí
   629‚ÜíThe architecture did not explicitly specify memory management for long-running sessions. Three leak vectors identified and resolved:
   630‚Üí
   631‚Üí**1. WebSocket listener accumulation:**
   632‚Üí- Risk: Each reconnect cycle could add new `onmessage`/`onerror` listeners without removing old ones
   633‚Üí- Rule: ConnectionManager MUST call `ws.close()` and null the reference before creating a new WebSocket instance. Never add listeners to a WebSocket that's being replaced.
   634‚Üí
   635‚Üí**2. Chart data array growth:**
   636‚Üí- Risk: `chartStore` array grows unbounded as new hourly data points arrive
   637‚Üí- Rule: Cap `chartStore` at 24 entries (24 hours of hourly data). When appending a new point, shift the oldest. Never let the array exceed the 24h window.
   638‚Üí
   639‚Üí**3. Timer reference leaks:**
   640‚Üí- Risk: `setTimeout` references from REST polling or reconnection backoff could accumulate if not properly cleared
   641‚Üí- Rule: Store all timer IDs. Clear previous timer before scheduling a new one. Use a single `AbortController` per service for coordinated cleanup if the page is ever torn down.
   642‚Üí
   643‚Üí**No other gaps found.** All critical, important, and nice-to-have areas are covered.

     9‚Üí# Metrics - Epic Breakdown
    10‚Üí
    11‚Üí## Overview
    12‚Üí
    13‚ÜíThis document provides the complete epic and story breakdown for Metrics, decomposing the requirements from the PRD, UX Design if it exists, and Architecture requirements into implementable stories.
    14‚Üí
    15‚Üí## Requirements Inventory
    16‚Üí
    17‚Üí### Functional Requirements
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí- FR1: User can view the current BTC/USD price, updated in real-time
    20‚Üí- FR2: User can see price updates reflected in the browser tab title
    21‚Üí- FR3: User can see a visual indication of price direction (up/down) on each update
    22‚Üí- FR4: User can view the 24-hour price change as both a percentage and absolute value
    23‚Üí- FR5: User can distinguish positive and negative price changes via color coding
    24‚Üí- FR6: User can view the current BTC market capitalization
    25‚Üí- FR7: System refreshes supplementary market data (24h change, market cap) periodically without user action
    26‚Üí- FR8: User can view a sparkline chart showing BTC price over the last 24 hours
    27‚Üí- FR9: User can hover over the chart to see the price and time at a specific data point
    28‚Üí- FR10: Chart displays hourly data points for the 24-hour period
    29‚Üí- FR11: System connects to the primary WebSocket data source (Binance) on page load
    30‚Üí- FR12: System falls back to a secondary WebSocket source (CoinCap) if the primary fails
    31‚Üí- FR13: System automatically reconnects when a WebSocket connection drops
    32‚Üí- FR14: User can see a visual indicator when the data connection is lost or reconnecting
    33‚Üí- FR15: User can see a stale data indicator when displayed price may be outdated
    34‚Üí- FR16: System falls back to REST API polling when WebSocket is unavailable
    35‚Üí- FR17: User sees a dark-themed interface with crypto-aesthetic styling on page load
    36‚Üí- FR18: User experiences no flash of unstyled or light-themed content on initial load
    37‚Üí- FR19: User sees smooth entry animations when data first loads
    38‚Üí- FR20: User sees no layout shift as dynamic content populates
    39‚Üí- FR21: User can view all dashboard data on desktop screens with a horizontal layout
    40‚Üí- FR22: User can view all dashboard data on mobile screens with a vertically stacked layout
    41‚Üí- FR23: Dashboard is usable and readable across all supported modern browsers
    42‚Üí
    43‚Üí### NonFunctional Requirements
    44‚Üí
    45‚Üí- NFR1: Page load (DOMContentLoaded) completes in < 2 seconds on a 4G connection
    46‚Üí- NFR2: Largest Contentful Paint (LCP) occurs in < 1.5 seconds
    47‚Üí- NFR3: Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS) = 0 ‚Äî no visible layout shift during data hydration
    48‚Üí- NFR4: Price updates render to DOM within 1 second of WebSocket message receipt
    49‚Üí- NFR5: All animations run at 60 FPS with no dropped frames
    50‚Üí- NFR6: Lighthouse performance score ‚â• 90
    51‚Üí- NFR7: Total hydrated JavaScript bundle < 100KB gzipped
    52‚Üí- NFR8: Chart library (lightweight-charts) < 50KB gzipped
    53‚Üí- NFR9: Binance WebSocket connection establishes within 500ms of island hydration under normal network conditions
    54‚Üí- NFR10: WebSocket reconnection uses exponential backoff starting at 1 second, capping at 30 seconds
    55‚Üí- NFR11: CoinGecko REST API polling interval ‚â• 60 seconds to stay within free tier rate limits (30 req/min)
    56‚Üí- NFR12: System tolerates CoinGecko API unavailability by displaying last known data with a timestamp
    57‚Üí- NFR13: Binance 24-hour connection expiry handled transparently as a standard reconnect cycle
    58‚Üí- NFR14: Fallback from Binance to CoinCap completes without user intervention
    59‚Üí- NFR15: Dashboard remains functional (displays last known data) when all external APIs are unreachable
    60‚Üí- NFR16: No JavaScript errors or white screens on WebSocket disconnection
    61‚Üí- NFR17: Browser tab remains stable during extended sessions (no memory leaks over 24+ hours)
    62‚Üí
    63‚Üí### Additional Requirements
    64‚Üí
    65‚Üí- Starter template: `npm create astro@latest -- --template minimal --typescript strict` ‚Äî specified in Architecture as Epic 1 Story 1
    66‚Üí- Tailwind CSS v4 via `@tailwindcss/vite` plugin with CSS-first `@theme` configuration (no `tailwind.config.js`)
    67‚Üí- Nano Stores (~1KB) as the reactive state layer shared across Astro islands (Astro's officially recommended approach)
    68‚Üí- Vanilla TypeScript islands ‚Äî no UI framework; islands use Astro `<script>` tags with `client:load` for direct DOM updates
    69‚Üí- WebSocket ConnectionManager as a singleton managing the entire WebSocket lifecycle with fallback chain
    70‚Üí- `theme-constants.ts` for lightweight-charts JS API color sync with Tailwind `@theme` tokens (duplicated values, acceptable for v1)
    71‚Üí- Shared TypeScript interfaces in `src/lib/types.ts`: `PriceTick`, `MarketData`, `ChartPoint`, `ConnectionStatus`
    72‚Üí- Unidirectional data flow: External API ‚Üí Service ‚Üí Store ‚Üí Component ‚Üí DOM
    73‚Üí- Memory leak prevention: listener cleanup on reconnect, chart data capped at 24 entries, timer reference management with `AbortController`
    74‚Üí- ESLint (`eslint-plugin-astro`) + Prettier (`prettier-plugin-astro`) + Vitest from first commit
    75‚Üí- Playwright for e2e smoke test ("page loads, price appears")
    76‚Üí- Package manager: npm (explicit choice)
    77‚Üí- Node 22+ required (Astro 6.x)
    78‚Üí- Co-located test files with `.test.ts` suffix
    79‚Üí- No barrel files (`utils/index.ts`) ‚Äî import directly from specific modules
    80‚Üí- Recursive `setTimeout` for REST polling (not `setInterval`) to prevent drift on slow responses
    81‚Üí
    82‚Üí### UX Design Requirements
    83‚Üí
    84‚Üí- UX-DR1: Implement design token system ‚Äî semantic color tokens (`bg-base` #0a0a0f, `bg-card` #12121a, `bg-card-hover` #1a1a2e, `accent-primary` #00f5ff cyan, `accent-secondary` #8b5cf6 purple, `accent-glow` rgba(0,245,255,0.15), `price-up` #22c55e, `price-down` #ef4444, `price-flash-up` #00f5ff, `price-flash-down` #ef4444, `text-primary` #f0f0f0, `text-secondary` #a0a0b0, `text-muted` #606070, `status-live` #22c55e, `status-reconnecting` #f59e0b, `status-stale` #ef4444) via Tailwind `@theme` directives in `src/styles/global.css`
    85‚Üí- UX-DR2: Implement typography system ‚Äî JetBrains Mono (`font-price`) for all numerical data, Inter (`font-label`) for labels/status text. Type scale: price 48px/36px weight 600, values 20-24px weight 500, card labels 12px Inter weight 400 `text-secondary`, status 11px Inter weight 400 `text-muted`, chart tooltip 13px JetBrains Mono. Line heights: 1.1 prices, 1.4 labels, 1.3 status
    86‚Üí- UX-DR3: Implement glassmorphism card pattern (`.card-glass`) ‚Äî `bg-card` background + `backdrop-blur(12px)` + 1px border `rgba(255,255,255,0.08)` + 16px border-radius + optional `accent-secondary` glow (`box-shadow: 0 0 20px rgba(139,92,246,0.1)`)
    87‚Üí- UX-DR4: Implement Price Card component ‚Äî label "Bitcoin BTC/USD", live price at 48px desktop/36px mobile, change row with arrow symbol (‚Üë/‚Üì) + percentage (+2.3%) + absolute (+$1,534.22) in `price-up`/`price-down` colors. States: Live (color flash 400ms), Stale (badge), Loading (label visible, data fades in 300ms)
    88‚Üí- UX-DR5: Implement Market Cap Card component ‚Äî label "Market Cap", value abbreviated ($1.34T) at 24px weight 500, secondary "24h Range" label with range value at 14px. Silent 60s refresh, no flash animation
    89‚Üí- UX-DR6: Implement Chart Card component ‚Äî lightweight-charts with line color `accent-primary` (#00f5ff), area gradient (rgba(0,245,255,0.1) top, transparent bottom), grid lines rgba(255,255,255,0.03), crosshair rgba(255,255,255,0.3), transparent background. Hover crosshair with price+time tooltip (13px JetBrains Mono). Draw animation 500ms. Full width spanning both grid columns on desktop
    90‚Üí- UX-DR7: Implement Status Indicator component ‚Äî 6px colored dot + state text at 11px `text-muted`, centered bottom of viewport. States: Live (green dot + "Live"), Reconnecting (amber pulsing dot 1.5s cycle + "Reconnecting..."), Stale (red static dot + "Connection lost"), Fallback (green dot + "Live ‚Äî CoinCap"). State transitions fade 300ms
    91‚Üí- UX-DR8: Implement Page Shell ‚Äî full-viewport `bg-base` (#0a0a0f) background, CSS Grid `1.5fr 1fr` desktop / single column mobile, 640px breakpoint, max-width 720px container, vertical centering on desktop, top-aligned on mobile. Spacing: 32px page padding desktop, 16px mobile, 24px card gaps
    92‚Üí- UX-DR9: Implement animation system ‚Äî data fade-in 300ms ease-out, chart draw 500ms ease-out, price tick flash 400ms ease-out, state transitions 300ms ease-in-out, reconnecting pulse 1.5s ease-in-out, card hover glow 300ms ease. No animation exceeds 500ms. No bounce/spring/overshoot. All respect `prefers-reduced-motion` (0ms durations). Price flash throttled to max 1 per 200ms
    93‚Üí- UX-DR10: Implement FOUC prevention ‚Äî set `bg-base` (#0a0a0f) on `<html>` element directly, dark theme as first paint. Empty glassmorphism cards with labels as the loading state (no spinners, no skeletons). Reserved space for all dynamic content (CLS = 0)
    94‚Üí- UX-DR11: Implement responsive design ‚Äî desktop-first with single 640px breakpoint (`sm:` Tailwind prefix). Price 48px‚Üí36px, grid collapses to single column. No `vh` units on mobile (use `dvh` or `min-h-screen`). Chart auto-resizes via `ResizeObserver`. No features hidden on mobile. CSS-only responsive logic
    95‚Üí- UX-DR12: Implement accessibility ‚Äî `<section aria-label="Bitcoin price">` with `<output aria-live="polite">` for price, `<section aria-label="Market data">` for market cap, `<section aria-label="24 hour price chart">` with `aria-hidden="true"` on chart canvas, `<div role="status" aria-live="polite">` for Status Indicator, `<html lang="en">`. Direction arrows (‚Üë/‚Üì) alongside color coding. All text meets AA contrast minimum

   141‚Üí## Epic 1: Project Foundation & Dashboard Shell
   142‚Üí
   143‚ÜíThe user sees a polished, responsive dark crypto dashboard that loads instantly ‚Äî the visual identity and layout are complete, ready for live data.
   144‚Üí
   145‚Üí### Story 1.1: Project Initialization & Tooling Setup
   146‚Üí
   147‚ÜíAs a developer,
   148‚ÜíI want a fully scaffolded Astro project with TypeScript, Tailwind, and code quality tooling,
   149‚ÜíSo that I can start building components with a consistent, well-configured dev environment from the first commit.
   150‚Üí
   151‚Üí**Acceptance Criteria:**
   152‚Üí
   153‚Üí**Given** a clean project directory
   154‚Üí**When** the initialization commands are run
   155‚Üí**Then** Astro 6.x is installed with the minimal template and TypeScript strict mode
   156‚Üí**And** Tailwind CSS v4 is configured via `@tailwindcss/vite` plugin in `astro.config.mjs`
   157‚Üí**And** `lightweight-charts`, `@fontsource/jetbrains-mono`, `@fontsource/inter`, and `nanostores` are installed
   158‚Üí**And** ESLint with `eslint-plugin-astro`, Prettier with `prettier-plugin-astro`, and Vitest are configured
   159‚Üí**And** the project structure follows Architecture spec: `src/pages/`, `src/layouts/`, `src/components/`, `src/lib/stores/`, `src/lib/services/`, `src/lib/utils/`, `src/styles/`
   160‚Üí**And** `src/lib/types.ts` defines `PriceTick`, `MarketData`, `ChartPoint`, and `ConnectionStatus` interfaces
   161‚Üí**And** `npm run dev` starts the Astro dev server successfully
   162‚Üí**And** `npm run build` produces a static output in `dist/`
   163‚Üí
   164‚Üí### Story 1.2: Design Token System & Typography
   165‚Üí
   166‚ÜíAs a developer,
   167‚ÜíI want a complete design token system with semantic colors, typography, and shared theme constants,
   168‚ÜíSo that all components use a consistent dark crypto visual identity and the chart library stays in sync.
   169‚Üí
   170‚Üí**Acceptance Criteria:**

   172‚Üí**Given** the Astro project from Story 1.1
   173‚Üí**When** `src/styles/global.css` is created with Tailwind `@theme` directives
   174‚Üí**Then** all semantic color tokens are defined: `bg-base` (#0a0a0f), `bg-card` (#12121a), `bg-card-hover` (#1a1a2e), `accent-primary` (#00f5ff), `accent-secondary` (#8b5cf6), `accent-glow` (rgba(0,245,255,0.15)), `price-up` (#22c55e), `price-down` (#ef4444), `price-flash-up` (#00f5ff), `price-flash-down` (#ef4444), `text-primary` (#f0f0f0), `text-secondary` (#a0a0b0), `text-muted` (#606070), `status-live` (#22c55e), `status-reconnecting` (#f59e0b), `status-stale` (#ef4444)
   175‚Üí**And** JetBrains Mono is configured as `font-price` and Inter as `font-label` via `@fontsource` imports
   176‚Üí**And** the type scale is defined: price 48px weight 600, values 20-24px weight 500, labels 12px weight 400, status 11px weight 400
   177‚Üí**And** `src/lib/theme-constants.ts` exports duplicated color values for lightweight-charts JS API configuration
   178‚Üí**And** `.card-glass`, `.price-text`, and `.status-badge` `@apply` compositions are defined (max 3)
   179‚Üí**And** CSS custom properties for runtime animation values (`--price-flash-up`, `--price-flash-down`) are defined in `:root`
   180‚Üí**And** tokens are usable via Tailwind utility classes in any component

   228‚Üí### Story 2.1: Nano Stores & Number Formatting Utilities
   229‚Üí
   230‚ÜíAs a developer,
   231‚ÜíI want reactive stores and consistent number formatting helpers in place,
   232‚ÜíSo that all components share a single source of truth for data and display values identically.
   233‚Üí
   234‚Üí**Acceptance Criteria:**
   235‚Üí
   236‚Üí**Given** the project structure from Epic 1
   237‚Üí**When** the store and utility files are created
   238‚Üí**Then** `src/lib/stores/price-store.ts` exports `priceStore` as `atom<PriceTick | null>(null)`
   239‚Üí**And** `src/lib/stores/status-store.ts` exports `statusStore` as `atom<ConnectionStatus>('connecting')`
   240‚Üí**And** `src/lib/stores/market-store.ts` exports `marketStore` as `atom<MarketData | null>(null)`
   241‚Üí**And** `src/lib/stores/chart-store.ts` exports `chartStore` as `atom<ChartPoint[]>([])`
   242‚Üí**And** every store is typed with explicit generics and initializes to `null` or empty (never undefined)
   243‚Üí**And** `src/lib/utils/format-utils.ts` exports: `formatPrice()` ‚Üí `$68,432.17`, `formatPercent()` ‚Üí `+2.3%` or `-1.8%`, `formatAbsoluteChange()` ‚Üí `+$1,534.22`, `formatMarketCap()` ‚Üí `$1.34T`, `formatRange()` ‚Üí `$66.9k ‚Äî $68.9k`, `formatChartTooltip()` ‚Üí `$68,432.17 at 14:30`
   244‚Üí**And** all formatters use `Intl.NumberFormat` as the base ‚Äî no manual string building
   245‚Üí**And** `format-utils.test.ts` covers all formatters with positive, negative, zero, and edge cases
   246‚Üí**And** tests pass via `npm run test`
   247‚Üí
   248‚Üí### Story 2.2: WebSocket Connection Manager (Binance)
   249‚Üí
   250‚ÜíAs a user,
   251‚ÜíI want the dashboard to connect to a live Bitcoin data source automatically on page load,
   252‚ÜíSo that I see real-time price updates without any action.
   253‚Üí
   254‚Üí**Acceptance Criteria:**
   255‚Üí
   256‚Üí**Given** the dashboard page loads and islands hydrate
   257‚Üí**When** the ConnectionManager initializes
   258‚Üí**Then** it connects to Binance WebSocket at `wss://stream.binance.com:9443/ws/btcusdt@trade` (FR11)
   259‚Üí**And** raw Binance messages (`{ p: "68432.17", ... }`) are normalized to `PriceTick` format: `{ price: number, timestamp: number, direction: 'up' | 'down' | 'neutral' }`
   260‚Üí**And** direction is computed by comparing to the previous price
   261‚Üí**And** normalized `PriceTick` is written to `priceStore`
   262‚Üí**And** `statusStore` is updated to `'live'` on successful connection
   263‚Üí**And** `statusStore` is set to `'connecting'` during initial connection attempt
   264‚Üí**And** the ConnectionManager is a singleton ‚Äî only one WebSocket connection exists at any time
   265‚Üí**And** `connection-manager.ts` never exposes raw WebSocket data to consumers
   266‚Üí**And** old WebSocket listeners are cleaned up before creating new connections (memory leak prevention)
   267‚Üí**And** services initialize via a `<script>` block in `Layout.astro`
   268‚Üí**And** `connection-manager.test.ts` covers message normalization and direction computation
   269‚Üí

   306‚Üí## Epic 3: Market Context & Price Chart
   307‚Üí
   308‚ÜíThe user gets the full market picture ‚Äî market cap, 24h range, and a 24h sparkline chart with hover tooltips ‚Äî completing the dashboard's information offering.
   309‚Üí
   310‚Üí### Story 3.1: Market Data Service (CoinGecko REST Polling)
   311‚Üí
   312‚ÜíAs a user,
   313‚ÜíI want the dashboard to fetch market data automatically in the background,
   314‚ÜíSo that I see up-to-date market cap, 24h change, and chart data without any action.
   315‚Üí
   316‚Üí**Acceptance Criteria:**
   317‚Üí
   318‚Üí**Given** the dashboard page loads and services initialize
   319‚Üí**When** the MarketDataService starts
   320‚Üí**Then** it fetches market cap, 24h change (percentage + absolute), 24h high/low, and hourly historical price data from CoinGecko REST API
   321‚Üí**And** `marketStore` is populated with a `MarketData` object: `{ marketCap, change24h, changePercent24h, high24h, low24h }`
   322‚Üí**And** `chartStore` is populated with an array of `ChartPoint` objects: `{ time: number, value: number }` for hourly data
   323‚Üí**And** `chartStore` is capped at 24 entries maximum ‚Äî oldest point shifted when appending new data
   324‚Üí**And** polling uses recursive `setTimeout` (not `setInterval`) with a 60-second interval (NFR11)
   325‚Üí**And** on CoinGecko API failure, last known store values are retained ‚Äî stores are never cleared (NFR12)
   326‚Üí**And** API errors are logged via `console.warn()` with endpoint and status code ‚Äî no user-facing error UI
   327‚Üí**And** timer references are stored and cleared before scheduling new timers (memory leak prevention)
   328‚Üí**And** `market-data-service.test.ts` covers data mapping, store updates, and error retention behavior

   373‚Üí## Epic 4: Connection Resilience & Reliability
   374‚Üí
   375‚ÜíThe dashboard never breaks ‚Äî WebSocket failures are handled silently with fallback sources, automatic reconnection, and clear stale data indication. The user can trust the data is always there.
   376‚Üí
   377‚Üí### Story 4.1: WebSocket Reconnection with Exponential Backoff
   378‚Üí
   379‚ÜíAs a user,
   380‚ÜíI want the dashboard to automatically recover from connection drops without my intervention,
   381‚ÜíSo that the live price feed resumes seamlessly after network interruptions.
   382‚Üí
   383‚Üí**Acceptance Criteria:**
   384‚Üí
   385‚Üí**Given** the ConnectionManager has an active Binance WebSocket connection
   386‚Üí**When** the WebSocket connection drops (error, close, or network failure)
   387‚Üí**Then** `statusStore` transitions to `'reconnecting'` (FR14)
   388‚Üí**And** reconnection attempts begin with exponential backoff: 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s, 30s cap (NFR10)
   389‚Üí**And** on successful reconnection, `statusStore` transitions back to `'live'`
   390‚Üí**And** the previous WebSocket instance is fully closed (`ws.close()`) and its reference nulled before creating a new connection (memory leak prevention, NFR17)
   391‚Üí**And** no JavaScript errors or white screens occur during disconnection or reconnection (NFR16)
   392‚Üí**And** Binance 24-hour connection expiry is handled as a standard disconnect/reconnect cycle ‚Äî no special logic (NFR13)
   393‚Üí**And** the last known price remains displayed in the Price Card during reconnection (NFR15)
   394‚Üí**And** `connection-manager.test.ts` covers backoff timing, listener cleanup, and reconnection state transitions
   395‚Üí
   396‚Üí### Story 4.2: CoinCap Fallback & REST Polling Fallback
   397‚Üí
   398‚ÜíAs a user,
   399‚ÜíI want the dashboard to try alternative data sources if the primary one fails,
   400‚ÜíSo that I always see price data regardless of which service is available.
   401‚Üí
   402‚Üí**Acceptance Criteria:**
   403‚Üí
   404‚Üí**Given** the ConnectionManager fails to connect or reconnect to Binance
   405‚Üí**When** Binance WebSocket is unavailable after reconnection attempts
   406‚Üí**Then** the ConnectionManager attempts to connect to CoinCap WebSocket at `wss://ws.coincap.io/prices?assets=bitcoin` (FR12)
   407‚Üí**And** CoinCap messages (`{ bitcoin: "68432.17" }`) are normalized to the same `PriceTick` format
   408‚Üí**And** on successful CoinCap connection, `statusStore` transitions to `'fallback'`
   409‚Üí**And** fallback from Binance to CoinCap completes without user intervention (NFR14)
   410‚Üí**And** if both Binance and CoinCap WebSockets fail, the system falls back to REST polling via the MarketDataService for price data (FR16)
   411‚Üí**And** during REST-only mode, `statusStore` reflects `'stale'` if no update received within 30 seconds
   412‚Üí**And** the dashboard remains functional displaying last known data when all external APIs are unreachable (NFR15)
   413‚Üí**And** when a higher-priority source becomes available again, the ConnectionManager reconnects to it
   414‚Üí**And** `connection-manager.test.ts` covers the full fallback chain: Binance ‚Üí CoinCap ‚Üí REST
   415‚Üí
   416‚Üí### Story 4.3: Status Indicator & Stale Data Badge
   417‚Üí
   418‚ÜíAs a user,
   419‚ÜíI want to see a subtle connection status indicator and know when displayed data may be outdated,
   420‚ÜíSo that I can trust the data when it's live and be aware when it's stale.
   421‚Üí
   422‚Üí**Acceptance Criteria:**
   423‚Üí
   424‚Üí**Given** the Status Indicator island is hydrated and subscribes to statusStore
   425‚Üí**When** statusStore changes value
   426‚Üí**Then** the indicator displays at the bottom center of the viewport as a 6px dot + text at 11px `text-muted` (UX-DR7)
   427‚Üí**And** Live state shows: green dot (`status-live`) + "Live" text ‚Äî barely visible, quiet good state
   428‚Üí**And** Reconnecting state shows: amber dot (`status-reconnecting`) + "Reconnecting..." text with dot pulsing animation (1.5s ease-in-out cycle, opacity 0.4‚Äì1.0) (FR14)
   429‚Üí**And** Stale state shows: red dot (`status-stale`) + "Connection lost" text, static (no animation)
   430‚Üí**And** Fallback state shows: green dot (`status-live`) + "Live ‚Äî CoinCap" text
   431‚Üí**And** state transitions fade over 300ms ease-in-out ‚Äî no abrupt switches
   432‚Üí**And** when statusStore is `'stale'` (no price update for >30 seconds), a "Stale" badge appears on the Price Card in `status-stale` color (FR15)
   433‚Üí**And** the stale badge does not hide or replace the price value ‚Äî it's an addition
   434‚Üí**And** when connection restores and a new price tick arrives, the stale badge fades out over 300ms
   435‚Üí**And** all animations respect `prefers-reduced-motion` (instant transitions, no pulsing)
   436‚Üí**And** the Status Indicator uses `<div role="status" aria-live="polite">` so screen readers announce state changes
   437‚Üí

# UX Design Specification - Metrics

**Author:** Dmytro
**Date:** 2026-03-25

---

## Executive Summary

### Project Vision

Single-page, real-time Bitcoin dashboard designed around three UX pillars: speed (information available in < 1 second of glancing), aesthetics (dark crypto visual identity that feels premium and alive), and focus (one asset, one page, zero cognitive overhead). The product competes on depth of simplicity ‚Äî every design decision serves the "open tab, know the price, move on" experience.

### Target Users

Primary user: Dmytro, a developer who uses Metrics as a pinned browser tab for daily BTC price checking. Desktop-first with occasional mobile glances. Expects zero configuration, zero onboarding, and instant information delivery. Tech-savvy but values beauty and polish ‚Äî the dashboard should feel crafted, not cobbled.

### Key Design Challenges

- **Information hierarchy:** Four data elements (price, 24h change, market cap, chart) on a single screen require clear visual priority. Live price dominates; supporting data is secondary but instantly parseable.
- **Dark theme readability:** Near-black backgrounds (#0a0a0f) with neon accents demand careful contrast management. Price figures must be high-contrast without causing eye strain during extended pinned-tab viewing.
- **State transitions:** WebSocket connection states (connecting, live, reconnecting, stale) must be communicated subtly without disrupting the primary price display. No layout shift during any state change.

### Design Opportunities

- **Sub-second glanceability:** Nail the information hierarchy so the price registers in peripheral vision. Tab title with live price extends this to no-focus-needed awareness.
...
