input.query: Binance WebSocket stream.binance.com:9443 vs data-stream.binance.vision endpoint 2026

content.content:
120‚Üí4. **Personality earns ownership.** The neon-on-dark aesthetic, the monospaced prices, the glassmorphism cards ‚Äî these aren't just style choices, they're identity. The product should feel like *yours*, not like a generic dashboard with a dark mode toggle.
   121‚Üí
   122‚Üí## UX Pattern Analysis & Inspiration
   123‚Üí
   124‚Üí### Inspiring Products Analysis
   125‚Üí
   126‚Üí**1. TradingView (chart widget, not the full platform)**
   127‚Üí
   128‚ÜíWhat they nail: The lightweight-charts library (which we're using) comes from TradingView. Their embedded chart widgets are a masterclass in information density on dark backgrounds. Price is always the hero element. The chart feels alive ‚Äî crosshair tracking, smooth transitions between timeframes, and a color system that makes green/red changes instantly parseable. Their dark theme is the industry standard that every crypto dashboard references.
   129‚Üí
   130‚Üí**Lesson for Metrics:** The chart interaction model (hover crosshair with price/time tooltip) is proven and familiar. Don't reinvent it ‚Äî adopt it directly from lightweight-charts defaults.
   131‚Üí
   132‚Üí**2. Is It Down Right Now (isitdownrightnow.com) / Downdetector**
   133‚Üí
   134‚ÜíWhat they nail: Single-purpose, single-question tools. You go there with one question ("Is X down?"), you get the answer immediately, you leave. Zero navigation, zero onboarding, zero accounts. The UX is the answer. This is the closest interaction model to what Metrics does ‚Äî except Metrics does it with visual polish.
   135‚Üí
   136‚Üí**Lesson for Metrics:** The "answer is the page" pattern. No hero section, no welcome message, no instructions. The data IS the interface. Load ‚Üí answer ‚Üí done.
   137‚Üí
   138‚Üí**3. Stripe Dashboard (dark mode)**
   139‚Üí
   140‚ÜíWhat they nail: Premium dark UI with clear information hierarchy. Numbers are the loudest elements ‚Äî large, monospaced, high-contrast white on near-black. Supporting text is muted gray. Cards with subtle borders and shadows create visual grouping without cluttering. State indicators (live, test mode) are minimal colored badges. The whole thing feels expensive and considered.
   141‚Üí
   142‚Üí**Lesson for Metrics:** The hierarchy pattern ‚Äî large monospaced numbers dominate, labels are quiet, cards group without competing. And the way Stripe handles state badges (small, colored, unobtrusive) maps directly to our reconnecting/stale data indicators.
   143‚Üí
   144‚Üí### Transferable UX Patterns
   145‚Üí
   146‚Üí**Information Hierarchy:**
   147‚Üí- Large monospaced price as the dominant visual element (TradingView, Stripe)
   148‚Üí- Muted secondary labels that don't compete with data (Stripe)
   149‚Üí- Color-coded directional indicators ‚Äî green up, red down ‚Äî no labels needed (TradingView)
   150‚Üí
   151‚Üí**Data Liveness:**
   152‚Üí- Price tick animation on update ‚Äî brief color flash or number transition (TradingView)
   153‚Üí- Chart crosshair with tooltip on hover for detail-on-demand (TradingView lightweight-charts)
   154‚Üí- No explicit "last updated" timestamp for live data ‚Äî liveness is communicated through motion, not text
   155‚Üí
   156‚Üí**Single-Purpose Layout:**
   157‚Üí- Data IS the interface ‚Äî no navigation, no sidebar, no header chrome (Is It Down)
   158‚Üí- Answer visible within 0 scrolls ‚Äî everything above the fold on all devices (Is It Down)
   159‚Üí- The page is the product ‚Äî no routes, no states, no modes
   160‚Üí
   161‚Üí**Dark Theme Execution:**
   162‚Üí- Near-black backgrounds with subtle card elevation via border or shadow, not background contrast (Stripe)
   163‚Üí- Glassmorphism for primary data cards, solid backgrounds for secondary elements
   164‚Üí- Neon accents used sparingly ‚Äî for interactive elements and status indicators, not decoration
   165‚Üí
   166‚Üí### Anti-Patterns to Avoid
   167‚Üí
   168‚Üí- **CoinGecko/CoinMarketCap:** Dozens of coins, ads, nav bars, footer links, trending lists, news feeds. The opposite of focus. Everything competes for attention.
   169‚Üí- **Loading spinners:** Products that show a spinner before data appears create anxiety. Metrics should show the static shell immediately and fade data in ‚Äî never a blank screen with a spinner.
   170‚Üí- **"Last updated: 3 seconds ago" timestamps:** These create doubt ("is 3 seconds old?"). Live motion (ticking price) communicates freshness better than any timestamp.
   171‚Üí- **Toast notifications for connection events:** "Connected!" / "Disconnected!" toasts are noisy and interruptive. State should be communicated through subtle, persistent indicators ‚Äî not ephemeral popups.
   172‚Üí- **Skeleton screens for simple data:** Skeleton loaders are for complex layouts with unknown content. For four known data elements, reserved space with a quick fade-in is cleaner and faster-feeling.
   173‚Üí
   174‚Üí### Design Inspiration Strategy
   175‚Üí
   176‚Üí**Adopt directly:**
   177‚Üí- lightweight-charts hover crosshair with price/time tooltip (proven, familiar)
   178‚Üí- Large monospaced price as visual anchor (TradingView/Stripe pattern)
   179‚Üí- Green/red color coding for price direction (universal convention)
   180‚Üí- "Answer is the page" single-screen layout (Is It Down pattern)
   181‚Üí
   182‚Üí**Adapt for Metrics:**
   183‚Üí- Stripe's card hierarchy ‚Üí glassmorphism cards with neon accent borders instead of subtle gray borders
   184‚Üí- Stripe's state badges ‚Üí minimal connection status indicator (colored dot + text, not a banner)
   185‚Üí- TradingView's price tick animation ‚Üí brief color flash on price change (cyan flash up, red flash down) that fades to white
   186‚Üí
   187‚Üí**Avoid:**
   188‚Üí- Any navigation elements, sidebars, or header chrome
   189‚Üí- Loading spinners or skeleton screens
   190‚Üí- Toast notifications for system events
   191‚Üí- Explicit "last updated" timestamps for live data
   192‚Üí- Any multi-page patterns or routing
   193‚Üí
   194‚Üí## Design System Foundation
   195‚Üí
   196‚Üí### Design System Choice
   197‚Üí
   198‚Üí**Tailwind CSS utility framework** with a custom design token layer. No component library ‚Äî the product has only 4-5 visual elements (price display, change indicator, market cap, chart card, status indicator), making a full component library unnecessary overhead. Tailwind provides the utility backbone; custom tokens define the crypto aesthetic.
   199‚Üí
   200‚Üí### Rationale for Selection
   201‚Üí
   202‚Üí- **Visual uniqueness required:** The dark crypto aesthetic (glassmorphism, neon accents, monospace prices) is too specific for any off-the-shelf component library. Custom styling is mandatory.
   203‚Üí- **Minimal component surface:** A single page with ~5 visual elements doesn't justify the bundle cost or learning curve of MUI, Chakra, or Ant Design.
   204‚Üí- **Already specified in PRD:** Tailwind CSS was chosen during product planning ‚Äî this decision maintains consistency.
   205‚Üí- **Solo developer efficiency:** Tailwind's utility classes allow rapid iteration without context-switching between CSS files and components. Custom tokens ensure consistency without a design system overhead.
   206‚Üí- **Bundle performance:** Tailwind's purge removes unused styles. No component library JS to ship. Aligns with the < 100KB gzipped JS budget.
   207‚Üí
   208‚Üí### Implementation Approach
   209‚Üí
   210‚Üí**Tailwind Config as Design System:**
   211‚Üí
   212‚ÜíThe `tailwind.config.js` file serves as the single source of truth for all design tokens, using `theme.extend` (not override) to preserve Tailwind defaults:
   213‚Üí
   214‚Üí- **Colors (semantic naming):** `accent-primary` (cyan), `accent-secondary` (purple), `accent-tertiary` (green), `price-up`, `price-down`, `bg-base`, `bg-card`, `text-primary`, `text-muted`, `text-label` ‚Äî never raw color names like `cyan-400`
   215‚Üí- **Typography:** JetBrains Mono for price figures, Inter for labels ‚Äî configured as named font families (`font-price`, `font-label`) with appropriate size/weight scales
   216‚Üí- **Effects:** Glassmorphism utilities (backdrop-blur, border opacity), glow effects for neon accents, transition presets for animations
   217‚Üí- **Spacing:** Custom spacing scale if Tailwind defaults don't match the desired density
   218‚Üí
   219‚Üí**CSS Custom Properties from Day One:**
   220‚Üí
   221‚ÜíCSS variables alongside Tailwind tokens for any value that animates or transitions at runtime:
   222‚Üí- `--price-flash-up` / `--price-flash-down` ‚Äî for price tick color flash animations (can't transition between Tailwind utility classes)
   223‚Üí- `--connection-status` ‚Äî for status indicator color transitions
   224‚Üí- Defined in `:root` and synchronized with Tailwind token values
   225‚Üí
   226‚Üí**Shared Constants File:**
   227‚Üí
   228‚ÜíA `theme-constants.ts` file that exports color values used by both Tailwind config and lightweight-charts API config. The chart library applies its own inline styles (line color, crosshair, grid lines) via its JS API ‚Äî these must mirror the Tailwind tokens but can't reference them directly. A shared constants file prevents drift.
   229‚Üí
   230‚Üí### Customization Strategy
   231‚Üí
   232‚Üí- **Semantic token naming** throughout ‚Äî tokens communicate intent (`accent-primary`, `text-muted`) not implementation (`cyan-400`, `gray-500`). If the accent color changes from cyan to green, no token names read wrong.
   233‚Üí- **Reusable utility compositions** via `@apply` for 3 repeated patterns maximum: `.card-glass` (glassmorphism card), `.price-text` (large monospaced price styling), `.status-badge` (connection state indicator). No more ‚Äî overusing `@apply` defeats utility-first and complicates debugging.
   234‚Üí- **Astro scoped styles** for animation keyframes co-located with each island component, while Tailwind handles layout and token-based styling
   235‚Üí- **No component abstraction layer** for v1 ‚Äî with only ~5 visual elements, inline Tailwind classes are sufficient. Component extraction happens in Phase 3 (reusable library) if needed.
   236‚Üí
   237‚Üí## Defining Experience
   238‚Üí
   239‚Üí### The Core Interaction
   240‚Üí
   241‚Üí**"Glance at a tab and know the Bitcoin price."**
   242‚Üí
   243‚ÜíThe defining experience is passive information absorption. The user does nothing ‚Äî the product delivers. Like a wall clock on the wall: you don't operate it, you don't configure it, you just look at it and you know. If Metrics nails this one interaction ‚Äî instant, reliable, beautiful awareness ‚Äî everything else follows.
   244‚Üí
   245‚Üí### User Mental Model
   246‚Üí
   247‚Üí**Current mental model (what Metrics replaces):**
   248‚Üí- "I need to check the Bitcoin price" ‚Üí open Google/CoinGecko ‚Üí scan through noise ‚Üí find the number ‚Üí close or navigate away
   249‚Üí- Active retrieval: go somewhere, look something up, extract the answer, leave
   250‚Üí
   251‚Üí**New mental model (what Metrics creates):**
   252‚Üí- "I wonder what Bitcoin is at" ‚Üí glance at pinned tab ‚Üí know
   253‚Üí- Passive awareness: the answer is already there, always current, always visible
   254‚Üí- Analogous to a wall clock ‚Äî you don't "use" it, you just look at it
   255‚Üí
   256‚Üí**Mental model shift:** From "go find information" to "information is already here." This is the core UX insight. The product doesn't feel like a tool you operate ‚Äî it feels like a window you look through.
   257‚Üí
   258‚Üí### Success Criteria for Core Experience
   259‚Üí
   260‚Üí- **Sub-second comprehension:** The price registers within one visual scan ‚Äî no reading, no parsing, no searching the page
   261‚Üí- **Zero-action awareness:** The user performs no clicks, no scrolls, no hovers to get the primary information
   262‚Üí- **Persistent reliability:** Every time the user glances at the tab, the data is there and current. 100% consistency builds the "wall clock" trust
   263‚Üí- **Tab-level awareness:** The browser tab title shows the price ‚Äî awareness without even switching to the tab
   264‚Üí- **Beautiful by default:** The visual quality reinforces "I built something good" on every glance
   265‚Üí
   266‚Üí### Pattern Analysis
   267‚Üí
   268‚Üí**Established patterns only ‚Äî no novel interaction design required.**
   269‚Üí

Based on the search results, here's the comparison between the two Binance WebSocket endpoints:

## Key Differences

**stream.binance.com:9443:**
The base endpoint is wss://stream.binance.com:9443 or wss://stream.binance.com:443. This endpoint supports both market data and user data streams. However, receiving user data streams on wss://stream.binance.com:9443 using a listenKey is now deprecated and will be removed from Binance's systems at a later date, with users directed to use the WebSocket API instead.

**data-stream.binance.vision:**
The base endpoint wss://data-stream.binance.vision can be subscribed to receive only market data messages, but user data stream is NOT available from this URL.

## Important Details

- A single connection to stream.binance.com is only valid for 24 hours
- WebSocket connections have a limit of 5 incoming messages per second
- To receive timestamp information in microseconds instead of milliseconds, add the parameter timeUnit=MICROSECOND or timeUnit=microsecond in the URL

In summary, use `stream.binance.com:9443` for market data streams (and user data via the deprecated method being phased out), and use `data-stream.binance.vision` as an alternative for market data only.

   798‚Üí### Animation Patterns
   799‚Üí
   800‚Üí**Timing Standards:**
   801‚Üí
   802‚Üí| Animation | Duration | Easing | Trigger |
   803‚Üí|---|---|---|---|
   804‚Üí| Data fade-in (initial load) | 300ms | ease-out | First data received |
   805‚Üí| Chart draw (initial load) | 500ms | ease-out | Historical data loaded |
   806‚Üí| Price tick flash | 400ms | ease-out | Each WebSocket price update |
   807‚Üí| State indicator transition | 300ms | ease-in-out | Connection state change |
   808‚Üí| Reconnecting dot pulse | 1500ms | ease-in-out | While reconnecting (loops) |
   809‚Üí| Card hover glow | 300ms | ease | Mouse enter/leave |
   810‚Üí
   811‚Üí**Rules:**
   812‚Üí- **No animation exceeds 500ms.** Everything feels snappy, never sluggish.
   813‚Üí- **ease-out for entrances** (data appearing) ‚Äî fast start, gentle landing.
   814‚Üí- **ease-in-out for state changes** (connection indicators) ‚Äî smooth both ways.
   815‚Üí- **ease for hover effects** ‚Äî natural, reversible.
   816‚Üí- **No bounce, no spring, no overshoot.** The aesthetic is calm and precise, not playful.
   817‚Üí- **All animations respect `prefers-reduced-motion`.** When enabled: instant transitions (0ms), no flashing, no pulsing. Chart draws instantly without animation.
   818‚Üí
   819‚Üí**Price Tick Flash Mechanic:**
   820‚Üí1. New price received via WebSocket
   821‚Üí2. Compare to previous price: higher ‚Üí `--price-flash-up` (cyan), lower ‚Üí `--price-flash-down` (red), equal ‚Üí no flash
   822‚Üí3. Price text color transitions to flash color instantly
   823‚Üí4. Price text color transitions back to `text-primary` (white) over 400ms ease-out
   824‚Üí5. Maximum flash frequency: throttle to 1 flash per 200ms to prevent seizure-inducing rapid flashing
   825‚Üí
   826‚Üí### Loading Patterns
   827‚Üí
   828‚Üí**Initial Page Load Sequence:**
   829‚Üí1. `bg-base` background renders immediately (set on `<html>` element ‚Äî no FOUC)
   830‚Üí2. Static shell visible: empty glassmorphism cards with labels, no data values
   831‚Üí3. Card value areas are reserved (fixed height) to prevent layout shift
   832‚Üí4. Data fades in per-card as each data source responds:
   833‚Üí   - Price Card: on first WebSocket tick
   834‚Üí   - Market Cap Card: on first REST response
   835‚Üí   - Chart Card: on historical data load (draws with animation)
   836‚Üí5. Each card's data appears independently ‚Äî no "wait for all" blocking
   837‚Üí
   838‚Üí**Rules:**
   839‚Üí- **Never show a loading spinner.** The static shell IS the loading state.
   840‚Üí- **Never show skeleton screens.** Reserved space with labels is sufficient for 5 known elements.
   841‚Üí- **Never block one card waiting for another.** Price might appear before chart ‚Äî that's fine.
   842‚Üí- **Never show a blank/white screen.** Dark background is the first thing that renders.
   843‚Üí- **CLS = 0.** All dynamic content has pre-reserved space. No element ever shifts position after initial render.
   844‚Üí
   845‚Üí**Data Refresh (steady state):**
   846‚Üí- REST data refreshes every 60s ‚Äî values update in place with no animation (silent refresh)
   847‚Üí- WebSocket ticks update price continuously ‚Äî each tick triggers the flash mechanic
   848‚Üí- Chart updates by appending new data points ‚Äî no full redraw, no flicker
   849‚Üí
   850‚Üí## Responsive Design & Accessibility
   851‚Üí
   852‚Üí### Responsive Strategy
   853‚Üí
   854‚Üí**Approach:** Desktop-first with a single mobile breakpoint. The product has one page with one layout ‚Äî complexity is minimal. No tablet-specific adaptation needed; tablets get the desktop layout.
   855‚Üí
   856‚Üí**Desktop (‚â• 640px):**
   857‚Üí- Two-column CSS Grid (`1.5fr 1fr`), max-width 720px, centered
   858‚Üí- Content vertically centered on viewport ‚Äî the dashboard floats in the center of the screen
   859‚Üí- Price at 48px, full card sizing

860‚Üí- Chart card spans full grid width for maximum sparkline readability 861‚Üí- 32px page padding, 24px card gaps 862‚Üí 863‚Üí**Mobile (< 640px):** 864‚Üí- Single-column stack, full-width cards 865‚Üí- Price scales to 36px ‚Äî still dominant but fits mobile viewport 866‚Üí- Chart card fills viewport width minus padding 867‚Üí- 16px page padding, 24px card gaps 868‚Üí- Top-aligned (not vertically centered ‚Äî mobile viewports are too short) 869‚Üí 870‚Üí**No intermediate breakpoints.** Two layouts are sufficient for a single-page dashboard. The 640px breakpoint aligns with Tailwind's `sm:` prefix. 871‚Üí 872‚Üí### Breakpoint Strategy 873‚Üí 874‚Üí| Breakpoint | Layout | Price Size | Grid | Centering | 875‚Üí|---|---|---|---|---| 876‚Üí| < 640px (mobile) | Single column | 36px | `grid-cols-1` | Top-aligned | 877‚Üí| ‚â• 640px (desktop) | Two column | 48px | `grid-cols-[1.5fr_1fr]` | Vertically centered | 878‚Üí 879‚Üí**Implementation:** 880‚Üí- Desktop-first CSS with `@media (max-width: 639px)` for mobile overrides 881‚Üí- OR Tailwind responsive: base styles = desktop, override with `max-sm:` for mobile 882‚Üí- No JavaScript-based responsive logic ‚Äî CSS only 883‚Üí- Chart component (lightweight-charts) auto-resizes via `ResizeObserver` ‚Äî no manual resize handling 884‚Üí 885‚Üí### Accessibility Strategy 886‚Üí 887‚Üí**Level:** Best-effort ‚Äî no formal WCAG compliance target. Sensible defaults that happen to meet AA in most cases. 888‚Üí 889‚Üí**What we're doing (already designed into the system):** 890‚Üí- **Color contrast:** All text exceeds AA minimums (verified in Visual Design Foundation). Price text at 15.2:1 exceeds AAA. 891‚Üí- **Color independence:** Direction indicators use arrows (‚Üë/‚Üì) alongside green/red ‚Äî never color-only. 892‚Üí- **Semantic HTML:** Each card is a `
` with `aria-label`. Status indicator uses `role="status"` with `aria-live="polite"`. 893‚Üí- **Motion safety:** All animations respect `prefers-reduced-motion`. Flash throttled to max 1 per 200ms. 894‚Üí- **Monospaced stability:** JetBrains Mono prevents digit-width changes on price updates ‚Äî no layout jitter that could confuse screen magnifier users. 895‚Üí 896‚Üí**What we're not doing (out of scope for v1):** 897‚Üí- No formal WCAG audit or certification 898‚Üí- No keyboard navigation optimization (no interactive elements except chart hover) 899‚Üí- No skip links (single-page, no navigation to skip) 900‚Üí- No high contrast mode override (the dark theme is already high contrast) 901‚Üí- No screen reader optimization for chart data (chart is decorative; price data is in the Price Card) 902‚Üí 903‚Üí**Accessibility decisions that matter most for this product:** 904‚Üí1. The live price must be accessible to screen readers ‚Äî `` element with `aria-live` ensures updates are announced 905‚Üí2. Connection state changes must be announced ‚Äî `role="status"` on Status Indicator handles this 906‚Üí3. Chart tooltip is enhancement-only ‚Äî all critical data is available without hovering 907‚Üí 908‚Üí### Testing Strategy 909‚Üí 910‚Üí**Responsive Testing:** 911‚Üí- Chrome DevTools device emulation for mobile breakpoint 912‚Üí- Real device testing on iPhone (Safari) and Android (Chrome) ‚Äî the two primary mobile browsers 913‚Üí- Verify chart resizes correctly on viewport change 914‚Üí- Verify no horizontal scroll at any viewport width 915‚Üí 916‚Üí**Visual Testing:** 917‚Üí- Verify no FOUC on initial load (dark background renders first) 918‚Üí- Verify CLS = 0 ‚Äî no layout shift during data hydration 919‚Üí- Verify animations run at 60 FPS (Chrome DevTools Performance panel) 920‚Üí- Verify `prefers-reduced-motion` disables all animations 921‚Üí 922‚Üí**Accessibility Spot Checks:** 923‚Üí- Run Lighthouse accessibility audit ‚Äî target ‚â• 80 score 924‚Üí- Verify color contrast ratios match design spec using browser DevTools 925‚Üí- Test with VoiceOver (macOS) ‚Äî confirm price updates are announced 926‚Üí- Verify arrow indicators are present alongside color for direction 927‚Üí 928‚Üí**Browser Matrix:** 929‚Üí 930‚Üí| Browser | Desktop | Mobile | 931‚Üí|---|---|---| 932‚Üí| Chrome | ‚úì Test | ‚úì Test (Android) | 933‚Üí| Firefox | ‚úì Test | ‚Äî | 934‚Üí| Safari | ‚úì Test | ‚úì Test (iOS) | 935‚Üí| Edge | ‚úì Test | ‚Äî | 936‚Üí 937‚Üí### Implementation Guidelines 938‚Üí 939‚Üí**Responsive:** 940‚Üí- Use Tailwind responsive utilities ‚Äî `sm:` prefix for desktop overrides (or `max-sm:` for mobile overrides from desktop-first base) 941‚Üí- Font sizes: `text-4xl` (48px) desktop, `max-sm:text-3xl` (36px) mobile for price 942‚Üí- Grid: `grid-cols-[1.5fr_1fr] max-sm:grid-cols-1` 943‚Üí- No `vh` units for container height on mobile (iOS Safari viewport issues) ‚Äî use `min-h-screen` or `dvh` unit 944‚Üí 945‚Üí**Accessibility:** 946‚Üí- Price Card: `

input.content:
# Story 2.2: WebSocket Connection Manager (Binance)

Status: ready-for-dev

## Story

As a user,
I want the dashboard to connect to a live Bitcoin data source automatically on page load,
So that I see real-time price updates without any action.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **Given** the dashboard page loads and islands hydrate **When** the ConnectionManager initializes **Then** it connects to Binance WebSocket at `wss://stream.binance.com:9443/ws/btcusdt@trade` (FR11)
2. **And** raw Binance messages (`{ p: "68432.17", ... }`) are normalized to `PriceTick` format: `{ price: number, timestamp: number, direction: 'up' | 'down' | 'neutral' }`
3. **And** direction is computed by comparing to the previous price
4. **And** normalized `PriceTick` is written to `priceStore`
5. **And** `statusStore` is updated to `'live'` on successful connection
6. **And** `statusStore` is set to `'connecting'` during initial connection attempt
7. **And** the ConnectionManager is a singleton ‚Äî only one WebSocket connection exists at any time
8. **And** `connection-manager.ts` never exposes raw WebSocket data to consumers
9. **And** old WebSocket listeners are cleaned up before creating new connections (memory leak prevention)
10. **And** services initialize via a `<script>` block in `Layout.astro`
11. **And** `connection-manager.test.ts` covers message normalization and direction computation

## Tasks / Subtasks

- [ ] Task 1: Create `src/lib/services/connection-manager.ts` (AC: #1, #5, #6, #7, #8, #9)
  - [ ] Define `WS_BINANCE_URL` constant: `wss://stream.binance.com:9443/ws/btcusdt@trade`
  - [ ] Implement `BinanceTradeMessage` interface for raw incoming messages
  - [ ] Implement singleton `ConnectionManager` with private constructor and `getInstance()` static method
  - [ ] Implement `connect()` method: sets `statusStore` to `'connecting'`, creates WebSocket, attaches listeners
  - [ ] Implement `onopen` handler: sets `statusStore` to `'live'`
  - [ ] Implement `onmessage` handler: parses JSON, calls `normalizeBinanceMessage()`, writes to `priceStore`
  - [ ] Implement `onerror` handler: `console.warn()` only, no throw (error handling for this story is minimal ‚Äî reconnection is Story 4.1)
  - [ ] Implement `onclose` handler: `console.warn()` only (reconnection is Story 4.1)
  - [ ] Implement `cleanup()` private method: close existing WebSocket, null the reference, remove listeners before creating new connections
  - [ ] Implement `disconnect()` public method: calls `cleanup()` for external teardown
- [ ] Task 2: Implement message normalization (AC: #2, #3, #8)
  - [ ] Create `normalizeBinanceMessage(raw: BinanceTradeMessage, previousPrice: number | null): PriceTick`
  - [ ] Parse `p` (price string) to `number` via `parseFloat()`
  - [ ] Use `T` (trade time) as `timestamp` (milliseconds)
  - [ ] Compute `direction`: `'up'` if price > previousPrice, `'down'` if price < previousPrice, `'neutral'` if equal or no previous price
  - [ ] Store `previousPrice` as private instance variable for direction comparison
- [ ] Task 3: Create `src/lib/services/connection-manager.test.ts` (AC: #11)
  - [ ] Test: `normalizeBinanceMessage` produces correct `PriceTick` from raw Binance message
  - [ ] Test: direction is `'neutral'` when no previous price exists
  - [ ] Test: direction is `'up'` when current price > previous price
  - [ ] Test: direction is `'down'` when current price < previous price
  - [ ] Test: direction is `'neutral'` when current price === previous price
  - [ ] Test: price string parsed correctly to number (e.g., `"68432.17"` ‚Üí `68432.17`)
  - [ ] Test: timestamp from `T` field is preserved
  - [ ] Test: singleton pattern returns same instance
- [ ] Task 4: Verify integration (AC: #4, #10)
  - [ ] Verify `priceStore.set()` is called with normalized `PriceTick` on each message
  - [ ] Verify `statusStore.set()` transitions: `'connecting'` ‚Üí `'live'`
  - [ ] NOTE: Do NOT add the `<script>` block to `Layout.astro` in this story ‚Äî Story 2.3 or later will wire up the initialization. AC #10 documents the intended initialization mechanism only.

## Dev Notes

### SCOPE BOUNDARY

**This story implements Binance WebSocket connection ONLY.**

- NO reconnection logic ‚Äî that is Story 4.1 (WebSocket Reconnection with Exponential Backoff)
- NO CoinCap fallback ‚Äî that is Story 4.2 (CoinCap Fallback & REST Polling Fallback)
- NO exponential backoff ‚Äî that is Story 4.1
- NO stale data detection ‚Äî that is Story 4.3
- NO `Layout.astro` script block wiring ‚Äî a later story (2.3+) will import and call `ConnectionManager.getInstance().connect()`

The ConnectionManager created here is designed to be **extended** by Stories 4.1 and 4.2 without breaking changes. Keep the singleton pattern clean so reconnection and fallback logic can be added later.

### File Locations

- **Source:** `src/lib/services/connection-manager.ts`
- **Test:** `src/lib/services/connection-manager.test.ts`

### Binance WebSocket API Reference

**Endpoint:** `wss://stream.binance.com:9443/ws/btcusdt@trade`

Alternative endpoint (market data only): `wss://data-stream.binance.vision/ws/btcusdt@trade`

**Connection limits:**
- A single connection is valid for 24 hours (NFR13 ‚Äî handled as standard reconnect in Story 4.1)
- 5 incoming messages per second limit
- All timestamps are in milliseconds by default

**Raw Binance Trade Message Schema:**

```typescript
// This is the raw shape received from Binance WebSocket
interface BinanceTradeMessage {
  e: string;    // Event type, always "trade"
  E: number;    // Event time (ms)
  s: string;    // Symbol, e.g. "BTCUSDT"
  t: number;    // Trade ID
  p: string;    // Price as string, e.g. "68432.17000000"
  q: string;    // Quantity as string
  b: number;    // Buyer order ID
  a: number;    // Seller order ID
  T: number;    // Trade time (ms)
  m: boolean;   // Is the buyer the market maker?
  M: boolean;   // Ignore
}
```

**Example raw message:**
```json
{
  "e": "trade",
  "E": 1672515782136,
  "s": "BTCUSDT",
  "t": 12345,
  "p": "68432.17000000",
  "q": "0.001",
  "b": 88,
  "a": 50,
  "T": 1672515782136,
  "m": true,
  "M": true
}
```

### Normalization Logic

```typescript
// Binance raw ‚Üí PriceTick normalized
// Only 3 fields needed from the raw message:
//   p (price string)  ‚Üí parseFloat ‚Üí PriceTick.price
//   T (trade time ms) ‚Üí PriceTick.timestamp
//   compare to previous price ‚Üí PriceTick.direction

function normalizeBinanceMessage(
  raw: BinanceTradeMessage,
  previousPrice: number | null
): PriceTick {
  const price = parseFloat(raw.p);
  const timestamp = raw.T;

  let direction: PriceTick['direction'] = 'neutral';
  if (previousPrice !== null) {
    if (price > previousPrice) direction = 'up';
    else if (price < previousPrice) direction = 'down';
    // equal ‚Üí stays 'neutral'
  }

  return { price, timestamp, direction };
}
```

### Singleton Pattern

```typescript
class ConnectionManager {
  private static instance: ConnectionManager | null = null;
  private ws: WebSocket | null = null;
  private previousPrice: number | null = null;

  private constructor() {}

  static getInstance(): ConnectionManager {
    if (!ConnectionManager.instance) {
      ConnectionManager.instance = new ConnectionManager();
    }
    return ConnectionManager.instance;
  }

  connect(): void {
    this.cleanup(); // Ensure no existing connection leaks
    statusStore.set('connecting');
    this.ws = new WebSocket(WS_BINANCE_URL);
    // ... attach listeners
  }

  private cleanup(): void {
    if (this.ws) {
      this.ws.onopen = null;
      this.ws.onmessage = null;
      this.ws.onerror = null;
      this.ws.onclose = null;
      this.ws.close();
      this.ws = null;
    }
  }

  disconnect(): void {
    this.cleanup();
  }
}
```

### Store Writes

This story writes to two stores (created by Story 2.1):

- **`priceStore`** (`atom<PriceTick | null>`) ‚Äî set with normalized `PriceTick` on every incoming trade message
- **`statusStore`** (`atom<ConnectionStatus>`) ‚Äî set to `'connecting'` when `connect()` is called, set to `'live'` on WebSocket `onopen`

Import paths:
```typescript
import { priceStore } from '../stores/price-store';
import { statusStore } from '../stores/status-store';
```

### Memory Leak Prevention

- Before creating a new WebSocket, always call `cleanup()` to:
  1. Set all event handlers (`onopen`, `onmessage`, `onerror`, `onclose`) to `null`
  2. Call `ws.close()` on the existing connection
  3. Set `this.ws = null` to release the reference
- Never add listeners to a WebSocket that is being replaced
- The singleton pattern ensures only one WebSocket exists at any time

### Error Handling (This Story)

- `onerror`: `console.warn('WebSocket error:', event)` ‚Äî no throw, no statusStore change (reconnection logic in Story 4.1 will handle this)
- `onclose`: `console.warn('WebSocket closed:', event.code, event.reason)` ‚Äî no reconnection attempt (Story 4.1)
- Malformed messages: wrap `JSON.parse()` in try/catch, `console.error()` with raw data, skip the update ‚Äî never crash the handler
- Never throw from WebSocket callbacks

### Testing Strategy

Export `normalizeBinanceMessage` as a named export for direct unit testing. The function is pure (no side effects) and easy to test in isolation.

For singleton and WebSocket behavior, use a mock WebSocket:

```typescript
// In test file, mock the global WebSocket
class MockWebSocket {
  onopen: ((event: Event) => void) | null = null;
  onmessage: ((event: MessageEvent) => void) | null = null;
  onerror: ((event: Event) => void) | null = null;
  onclose: ((event: CloseEvent) => void) | null = null;
  close = vi.fn();
  // Simulate events by calling handlers directly
}

vi.stubGlobal('WebSocket', vi.fn(() => new MockWebSocket()));
```

### Anti-Patterns (DO NOT)

- Do NOT use `addEventListener` for WebSocket events ‚Äî use `onmessage`/`onopen`/etc. property assignment (easier cleanup, prevents listener accumulation)
- Do NOT expose the raw `WebSocket` instance or raw Binance messages outside `connection-manager.ts`
- Do NOT use `setInterval` for anything in this module
- Do NOT implement reconnection logic ‚Äî that is Story 4.1
- Do NOT implement CoinCap fallback ‚Äî that is Story 4.2
- Do NOT add the `<script>` initialization block to `Layout.astro` ‚Äî a later story handles wiring
- Do NOT throw exceptions from WebSocket callbacks
- Do NOT store formatted strings in stores ‚Äî store raw numbers, format in components

### Dependency on Story 2.1

This story imports from `price-store.ts` and `status-store.ts`, which are created in Story 2.1 (Nano Stores & Number Formatting Utilities). Story 2.1 must be completed first.

### Previous Story Intelligence

From Story 1.1:
- Project uses Astro 6.0.8 with TypeScript strict mode
- Vitest configured with `include: ['src/**/*.test.ts']`
- ESLint flat config with `eslint-plugin-astro`
- Test files are co-located with source (`src/lib/services/connection-manager.test.ts`)
- No barrel files ‚Äî import directly from specific modules
- `PriceTick` and `ConnectionStatus` types defined in `src/lib/types.ts`

From Story 1.1 review findings (deferred items relevant to this story):
- `PriceTick.timestamp` has no unit documented ‚Äî this story uses milliseconds (matching Binance `T` field)
- `PriceTick.price` allows non-positive/non-finite values ‚Äî add a guard: skip `priceStore.set()` if `isNaN(price)` or `price <= 0`
- `PriceTick.direction` has no runtime validation ‚Äî the normalization function guarantees valid direction values

### Constants

```typescript
const WS_BINANCE_URL = 'wss://stream.binance.com:9443/ws/btcusdt@trade';
```

Use `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` per architecture naming conventions.

### References

- [Binance WebSocket Streams API](https://developers.binance.com/docs/binance-spot-api-docs/web-socket-streams) ‚Äî official endpoint docs, trade stream schema, 24h connection limit
- [Binance WebSocket Streams (GitHub)](https://github.com/binance/binance-spot-api-docs/blob/master/web-socket-streams.md) ‚Äî trade stream message format reference
- [Source: architecture.md#API & Communication Patterns] ‚Äî WebSocket Manager singleton, fallback chain, normalization rules
- [Source: architecture.md#Data Architecture] ‚Äî PriceTick interface, unidirectional data flow
- [Source: architecture.md#Process Patterns] ‚Äî error handling standards, initialization sequence
- [Source: architecture.md#Implementation Patterns] ‚Äî naming conventions, anti-patterns, memory leak prevention
- [Source: architecture.md#Gap Analysis Results] ‚Äî NFR17 memory leak prevention rules (listener cleanup, reference nulling)
- [Source: prd.md#FR11] ‚Äî Binance connection on page load
- [Source: prd.md#NFR9] ‚Äî 500ms connection establishment target
- [Source: prd.md#NFR13] ‚Äî 24h connection expiry (handled by Story 4.1)
- [Source: epics.md#Story 2.2] ‚Äî acceptance criteria
- [Source: epics.md#Story 4.1] ‚Äî reconnection with backoff (NOT this story)
- [Source: epics.md#Story 4.2] ‚Äî CoinCap fallback (NOT this story)

content.text:
Story 2.2 has been created and sprint status updated. Here is a summary of what was done:

**Created:** `/Users/dmytroshamsiiev/Projects/metrics/_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/2-2-websocket-connection-manager-binance.md`

**Updated:** `/Users/dmytroshamsiiev/Projects/metrics/_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/sprint-status.yaml` -- changed `2-2-websocket-connection-manager-binance` from `backlog` to `ready-for-dev`

**Key contents of the story file:**

- **Story and Acceptance Criteria** pulled directly from epics.md Story 2.2
- **4 tasks** with detailed subtasks: ConnectionManager singleton, message normalization, unit tests, integration verification
- **Scope boundary clearly defined**: Binance-only, NO reconnection (Story 4.1), NO CoinCap fallback (Story 4.2), NO Layout.astro wiring
- **Binance WebSocket API verified** via web research: endpoint `wss://stream.binance.com:9443/ws/btcusdt@trade` confirmed, full trade message schema documented with all 11 fields (`e`, `E`, `s`, `t`, `p`, `q`, `b`, `a`, `T`, `m`, `M`)
- **Normalization logic**: only 3 fields needed from raw message -- `p` (price string via parseFloat), `T` (trade time ms), and direction computed by comparing to previous price
- **Singleton pattern** with cleanup method for memory leak prevention (null handlers before close, null reference)
- **Store writes**: `priceStore.set()` with normalized PriceTick, `statusStore.set()` for connecting/live transitions
- **Testing strategy**: export `normalizeBinanceMessage` as pure function for direct testing, MockWebSocket for integration tests
- **Anti-patterns**: no addEventListener (use property assignment), no raw data exposure, no setInterval, no reconnection logic
- **Dependency**: Story 2.1 must be completed first (provides priceStore and statusStore)
- **Deferred item from 1.1 review addressed**: PriceTick.timestamp uses milliseconds matching Binance `T` field; guard against NaN/non-positive prices

Story 2.4 covers: updating browser tab title with formatted price on every priceStore tick. Key notes:
- Uses `document.title` in a priceStore.subscribe() callback
- Format: `$68,432.17 ‚Äî Metrics` using formatPrice() from format-utils
- When priceStore is null: title is just `Metrics`
- Location: script block in Layout.astro (or a small utility)
- Very small story ‚Äî simple subscribe + document.title update
- Include exact code example
- Anti-patterns: don't throttle separately from display, don't create a new component for this

**File B:** `_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/3-1-market-data-service-coingecko-rest-polling.md`
Story 3.1 covers: CoinGecko REST polling service. Key notes:
- File: src/lib/services/market-data-service.ts
- Test: src/lib/services/market-data-service.test.ts
- Endpoints: CoinGecko /simple/price and /coins/bitcoin/market_chart for hourly data
- Polling: recursive setTimeout (NOT setInterval), 60s interval (NFR11)
- Writes to: marketStore (MarketData) and chartStore (ChartPoint[])
- chartStore capped at 24 entries
- Error handling: retain last store values, console.warn, never clear stores
- Timer cleanup: store timer ID, clear before scheduling new
- AbortController for coordinated cleanup
- Include exact CoinGecko response format from web research

4. **Update sprint status** in `_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/sprint-status.yaml`:
   - `2-4-tab-title-price-updates: backlog` ‚Üí `ready-for-dev`
   - `3-1-market-data-service-coingecko-rest-polling: backlog` ‚Üí `ready-for-dev`
   - `epic-3: backlog` ‚Üí `epic-3: in-progress` (first story in Epic 3)

Use the standard story template with: Story, Acceptance Criteria, Tasks/Subtasks, Dev Notes (exhaustive), Dev Agent Record (empty sections). Be EXHAUSTIVE ‚Äî the dev agent only has the story file.

     8‚Üí
     9‚Üí# Metrics - Epic Breakdown
    10‚Üí
    11‚Üí## Overview
    12‚Üí
    13‚ÜíThis document provides the complete epic and story breakdown for Metrics, decomposing the requirements from the PRD, UX Design if it exists, and Architecture requirements into implementable stories.
    14‚Üí
    15‚Üí## Requirements Inventory
    16‚Üí
    17‚Üí### Functional Requirements
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí- FR1: User can view the current BTC/USD price, updated in real-time
    20‚Üí- FR2: User can see price updates reflected in the browser tab title
    21‚Üí- FR3: User can see a visual indication of price direction (up/down) on each update
    22‚Üí- FR4: User can view the 24-hour price change as both a percentage and absolute value
    23‚Üí- FR5: User can distinguish positive and negative price changes via color coding
    24‚Üí- FR6: User can view the current BTC market capitalization
    25‚Üí- FR7: System refreshes supplementary market data (24h change, market cap) periodically without user action
    26‚Üí- FR8: User can view a sparkline chart showing BTC price over the last 24 hours
    27‚Üí- FR9: User can hover over the chart to see the price and time at a specific data point
    28‚Üí- FR10: Chart displays hourly data points for the 24-hour period
    29‚Üí- FR11: System connects to the primary WebSocket data source (Binance) on page load
    30‚Üí- FR12: System falls back to a secondary WebSocket source (CoinCap) if the primary fails
    31‚Üí- FR13: System automatically reconnects when a WebSocket connection drops
    32‚Üí- FR14: User can see a visual indicator when the data connection is lost or reconnecting
    33‚Üí- FR15: User can see a stale data indicator when displayed price may be outdated
    34‚Üí- FR16: System falls back to REST API polling when WebSocket is unavailable
    35‚Üí- FR17: User sees a dark-themed interface with crypto-aesthetic styling on page load
    36‚Üí- FR18: User experiences no flash of unstyled or light-themed content on initial load
    37‚Üí- FR19: User sees smooth entry animations when data first loads
    38‚Üí- FR20: User sees no layout shift as dynamic content populates
    39‚Üí- FR21: User can view all dashboard data on desktop screens with a horizontal layout
    40‚Üí- FR22: User can view all dashboard data on mobile screens with a vertically stacked layout
    41‚Üí- FR23: Dashboard is usable and readable across all supported modern browsers
    42‚Üí
    43‚Üí### NonFunctional Requirements
    44‚Üí
    45‚Üí- NFR1: Page load (DOMContentLoaded) completes in < 2 seconds on a 4G connection
    46‚Üí- NFR2: Largest Contentful Paint (LCP) occurs in < 1.5 seconds
    47‚Üí- NFR3: Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS) = 0 ‚Äî no visible layout shift during data hydration
    48‚Üí- NFR4: Price updates render to DOM within 1 second of WebSocket message receipt
    49‚Üí- NFR5: All animations run at 60 FPS with no dropped frames
    50‚Üí- NFR6: Lighthouse performance score ‚â• 90
    51‚Üí- NFR7: Total hydrated JavaScript bundle < 100KB gzipped
    52‚Üí- NFR8: Chart library (lightweight-charts) < 50KB gzipped
    53‚Üí- NFR9: Binance WebSocket connection establishes within 500ms of island hydration under normal network conditions
    54‚Üí- NFR10: WebSocket reconnection uses exponential backoff starting at 1 second, capping at 30 seconds
    55‚Üí- NFR11: CoinGecko REST API polling interval ‚â• 60 seconds to stay within free tier rate limits (30 req/min)
    56‚Üí- NFR12: System tolerates CoinGecko API unavailability by displaying last known data with a timestamp
    57‚Üí- NFR13: Binance 24-hour connection expiry handled transparently as a standard reconnect cycle
    58‚Üí- NFR14: Fallback from Binance to CoinCap completes without user intervention
    59‚Üí- NFR15: Dashboard remains functional (displays last known data) when all external APIs are unreachable
    60‚Üí- NFR16: No JavaScript errors or white screens on WebSocket disconnection
    61‚Üí- NFR17: Browser tab remains stable during extended sessions (no memory leaks over 24+ hours)
    62‚Üí
    63‚Üí### Additional Requirements
    64‚Üí
    65‚Üí- Starter template: `npm create astro@latest -- --template minimal --typescript strict` ‚Äî specified in Architecture as Epic 1 Story 1
    66‚Üí- Tailwind CSS v4 via `@tailwindcss/vite` plugin with CSS-first `@theme` configuration (no `tailwind.config.js`)
    67‚Üí- Nano Stores (~1KB) as the reactive state layer shared across Astro islands (Astro's officially recommended approach)
    68‚Üí- Vanilla TypeScript islands ‚Äî no UI framework; islands use Astro `<script>` tags with `client:load` for direct DOM updates
    69‚Üí- WebSocket ConnectionManager as a singleton managing the entire WebSocket lifecycle with fallback chain
    70‚Üí- `theme-constants.ts` for lightweight-charts JS API color sync with Tailwind `@theme` tokens (duplicated values, acceptable for v1)
    71‚Üí- Shared TypeScript interfaces in `src/lib/types.ts`: `PriceTick`, `MarketData`, `ChartPoint`, `ConnectionStatus`
    72‚Üí- Unidirectional data flow: External API ‚Üí Service ‚Üí Store ‚Üí Component ‚Üí DOM
    73‚Üí- Memory leak prevention: listener cleanup on reconnect, chart data capped at 24 entries, timer reference management with `AbortController`
    74‚Üí- ESLint (`eslint-plugin-astro`) + Prettier (`prettier-plugin-astro`) + Vitest from first commit
    75‚Üí- Playwright for e2e smoke test ("page loads, price appears")
    76‚Üí- Package manager: npm (explicit choice)
    77‚Üí- Node 22+ required (Astro 6.x)
    78‚Üí- Co-located test files with `.test.ts` suffix
    79‚Üí- No barrel files (`utils/index.ts`) ‚Äî import directly from specific modules
    80‚Üí- Recursive `setTimeout` for REST polling (not `setInterval`) to prevent drift on slow responses
    81‚Üí
    82‚Üí### UX Design Requirements
    83‚Üí
    84‚Üí- UX-DR1: Implement design token system ‚Äî semantic color tokens (`bg-base` #0a0a0f, `bg-card` #12121a, `bg-card-hover` #1a1a2e, `accent-primary` #00f5ff cyan, `accent-secondary` #8b5cf6 purple, `accent-glow` rgba(0,245,255,0.15), `price-up` #22c55e, `price-down` #ef4444, `price-flash-up` #00f5ff, `price-flash-down` #ef4444, `text-primary` #f0f0f0, `text-secondary` #a0a0b0, `text-muted` #606070, `status-live` #22c55e, `status-reconnecting` #f59e0b, `status-stale` #ef4444) via Tailwind `@theme` directives in `src/styles/global.css`
    85‚Üí- UX-DR2: Implement typography system ‚Äî JetBrains Mono (`font-price`) for all numerical data, Inter (`font-label`) for labels/status text. Type scale: price 48px/36px weight 600, values 20-24px weight 500, card labels 12px Inter weight 400 `text-secondary`, status 11px Inter weight 400 `text-muted`, chart tooltip 13px JetBrains Mono. Line heights: 1.1 prices, 1.4 labels, 1.3 status
    86‚Üí- UX-DR3: Implement glassmorphism card pattern (`.card-glass`) ‚Äî `bg-card` background + `backdrop-blur(12px)` + 1px border `rgba(255,255,255,0.08)` + 16px border-radius + optional `accent-secondary` glow (`box-shadow: 0 0 20px rgba(139,92,246,0.1)`)
    87‚Üí- UX-DR4: Implement Price Card component ‚Äî label "Bitcoin BTC/USD", live price at 48px desktop/36px mobile, change row with arrow symbol (‚Üë/‚Üì) + percentage (+2.3%) + absolute (+$1,534.22) in `price-up`/`price-down` colors. States: Live (color flash 400ms), Stale (badge), Loading (label visible, data fades in 300ms)
    88‚Üí- UX-DR5: Implement Market Cap Card component ‚Äî label "Market Cap", value abbreviated ($1.34T) at 24px weight 500, secondary "24h Range" label with range value at 14px. Silent 60s refresh, no flash animation
    89‚Üí- UX-DR6: Implement Chart Card component ‚Äî lightweight-charts with line color `accent-primary` (#00f5ff), area gradient (rgba(0,245,255,0.1) top, transparent bottom), grid lines rgba(255,255,255,0.03), crosshair rgba(255,255,255,0.3), transparent background. Hover crosshair with price+time tooltip (13px JetBrains Mono). Draw animation 500ms. Full width spanning both grid columns on desktop
    90‚Üí- UX-DR7: Implement Status Indicator component ‚Äî 6px colored dot + state text at 11px `text-muted`, centered bottom of viewport. States: Live (green dot + "Live"), Reconnecting (amber pulsing dot 1.5s cycle + "Reconnecting..."), Stale (red static dot + "Connection lost"), Fallback (green dot + "Live ‚Äî CoinCap"). State transitions fade 300ms
    91‚Üí- UX-DR8: Implement Page Shell ‚Äî full-viewport `bg-base` (#0a0a0f) background, CSS Grid `1.5fr 1fr` desktop / single column mobile, 640px breakpoint, max-width 720px container, vertical centering on desktop, top-aligned on mobile. Spacing: 32px page padding desktop, 16px mobile, 24px card gaps
    92‚Üí- UX-DR9: Implement animation system ‚Äî data fade-in 300ms ease-out, chart draw 500ms ease-out, price tick flash 400ms ease-out, state transitions 300ms ease-in-out, reconnecting pulse 1.5s ease-in-out, card hover glow 300ms ease. No animation exceeds 500ms. No bounce/spring/overshoot. All respect `prefers-reduced-motion` (0ms durations). Price flash throttled to max 1 per 200ms
    93‚Üí- UX-DR10: Implement FOUC prevention ‚Äî set `bg-base` (#0a0a0f) on `<html>` element directly, dark theme as first paint. Empty glassmorphism cards with labels as the loading state (no spinners, no skeletons). Reserved space for all dynamic content (CLS = 0)
    94‚Üí- UX-DR11: Implement responsive design ‚Äî desktop-first with single 640px breakpoint (`sm:` Tailwind prefix). Price 48px‚Üí36px, grid collapses to single column. No `vh` units on mobile (use `dvh` or `min-h-screen`). Chart auto-resizes via `ResizeObserver`. No features hidden on mobile. CSS-only responsive logic
    95‚Üí- UX-DR12: Implement accessibility ‚Äî `<section aria-label="Bitcoin price">` with `<output aria-live="polite">` for price, `<section aria-label="Market data">` for market cap, `<section aria-label="24 hour price chart">` with `aria-hidden="true"` on chart canvas, `<div role="status" aria-live="polite">` for Status Indicator, `<html lang="en">`. Direction arrows (‚Üë/‚Üì) alongside color coding. All text meets AA contrast minimum
    96‚Üí
    97‚Üí### FR Coverage Map
    98‚Üí
    99‚Üí- FR1: Epic 2 ‚Äî Live BTC/USD price display
   100‚Üí- FR2: Epic 2 ‚Äî Price in browser tab title
   101‚Üí- FR3: Epic 2 ‚Äî Price direction indicator (up/down)
   102‚Üí- FR4: Epic 2 ‚Äî 24h change (percentage + absolute)
   103‚Üí- FR5: Epic 2 ‚Äî Color-coded positive/negative changes
   104‚Üí- FR6: Epic 3 ‚Äî Market cap display
   105‚Üí- FR7: Epic 3 ‚Äî Periodic market data refresh
   106‚Üí- FR8: Epic 3 ‚Äî 24h sparkline chart
   107‚Üí- FR9: Epic 3 ‚Äî Chart hover tooltip (price + time)
   108‚Üí- FR10: Epic 3 ‚Äî Hourly chart data points
   109‚Üí- FR11: Epic 2 ‚Äî Binance WebSocket connection on load
   110‚Üí- FR12: Epic 4 ‚Äî CoinCap fallback WebSocket
   111‚Üí- FR13: Epic 4 ‚Äî Auto-reconnection on drop
   112‚Üí- FR14: Epic 4 ‚Äî Reconnecting visual indicator
   113‚Üí- FR15: Epic 4 ‚Äî Stale data indicator
   114‚Üí- FR16: Epic 4 ‚Äî REST polling fallback
   115‚Üí- FR17: Epic 1 ‚Äî Dark-themed crypto aesthetic
   116‚Üí- FR18: Epic 1 ‚Äî No FOUC
   117‚Üí- FR19: Epic 2 ‚Äî Entry animations
   118‚Üí- FR20: Epic 1 ‚Äî No layout shift
   119‚Üí- FR21: Epic 1 ‚Äî Desktop horizontal layout
   120‚Üí- FR22: Epic 1 ‚Äî Mobile stacked layout
   121‚Üí- FR23: Epic 1 ‚Äî Cross-browser support
   122‚Üí
   123‚Üí## Epic List
   124‚Üí
   125‚Üí### Epic 1: Project Foundation & Dashboard Shell
   126‚ÜíThe user sees a polished, responsive dark crypto dashboard that loads instantly ‚Äî the visual identity and layout are complete, ready for live data.
   127‚Üí**FRs covered:** FR17, FR18, FR20, FR21, FR22, FR23
   128‚Üí
   129‚Üí### Epic 2: Live Bitcoin Price Display
   130‚ÜíThe user can glance at the dashboard (or tab title) and instantly know the current BTC price, its direction, and 24h change ‚Äî updated in real-time.
   131‚Üí**FRs covered:** FR1, FR2, FR3, FR4, FR5, FR11, FR19
   132‚Üí
   133‚Üí### Epic 3: Market Context & Price Chart
   134‚ÜíThe user gets the full market picture ‚Äî market cap, 24h range, and a 24h sparkline chart with hover tooltips ‚Äî completing the dashboard's information offering.

   136‚Üí
   137‚Üí### Epic 4: Connection Resilience & Reliability
   138‚ÜíThe dashboard never breaks ‚Äî WebSocket failures are handled silently with fallback sources, automatic reconnection, and clear stale data indication. The user can trust the data is always there.
   139‚Üí**FRs covered:** FR12, FR13, FR14, FR15, FR16
   140‚Üí
   141‚Üí## Epic 1: Project Foundation & Dashboard Shell
   142‚Üí
   143‚ÜíThe user sees a polished, responsive dark crypto dashboard that loads instantly ‚Äî the visual identity and layout are complete, ready for live data.
   144‚Üí
   145‚Üí### Story 1.1: Project Initialization & Tooling Setup
   146‚Üí
   147‚ÜíAs a developer,
   148‚ÜíI want a fully scaffolded Astro project with TypeScript, Tailwind, and code quality tooling,
   149‚ÜíSo that I can start building components with a consistent, well-configured dev environment from the first commit.
   150‚Üí
   151‚Üí**Acceptance Criteria:**
   152‚Üí
   153‚Üí**Given** a clean project directory
   154‚Üí**When** the initialization commands are run
   155‚Üí**Then** Astro 6.x is installed with the minimal template and TypeScript strict mode
   156‚Üí**And** Tailwind CSS v4 is configured via `@tailwindcss/vite` plugin in `astro.config.mjs`
   157‚Üí**And** `lightweight-charts`, `@fontsource/jetbrains-mono`, `@fontsource/inter`, and `nanostores` are installed
   158‚Üí**And** ESLint with `eslint-plugin-astro`, Prettier with `prettier-plugin-astro`, and Vitest are configured
   159‚Üí**And** the project structure follows Architecture spec: `src/pages/`, `src/layouts/`, `src/components/`, `src/lib/stores/`, `src/lib/services/`, `src/lib/utils/`, `src/styles/`
   160‚Üí**And** `src/lib/types.ts` defines `PriceTick`, `MarketData`, `ChartPoint`, and `ConnectionStatus` interfaces
   161‚Üí**And** `npm run dev` starts the Astro dev server successfully
   162‚Üí**And** `npm run build` produces a static output in `dist/`
   163‚Üí
   164‚Üí### Story 1.2: Design Token System & Typography
   165‚Üí
   166‚ÜíAs a developer,
   167‚ÜíI want a complete design token system with semantic colors, typography, and shared theme constants,
   168‚ÜíSo that all components use a consistent dark crypto visual identity and the chart library stays in sync.
   169‚Üí
   170‚Üí**Acceptance Criteria:**
   171‚Üí
   172‚Üí**Given** the Astro project from Story 1.1
   173‚Üí**When** `src/styles/global.css` is created with Tailwind `@theme` directives
   174‚Üí**Then** all semantic color tokens are defined: `bg-base` (#0a0a0f), `bg-card` (#12121a), `bg-card-hover` (#1a1a2e), `accent-primary` (#00f5ff), `accent-secondary` (#8b5cf6), `accent-glow` (rgba(0,245,255,0.15)), `price-up` (#22c55e), `price-down` (#ef4444), `price-flash-up` (#00f5ff), `price-flash-down` (#ef4444), `text-primary` (#f0f0f0), `text-secondary` (#a0a0b0), `text-muted` (#606070), `status-live` (#22c55e), `status-reconnecting` (#f59e0b), `status-stale` (#ef4444)
   175‚Üí**And** JetBrains Mono is configured as `font-price` and Inter as `font-label` via `@fontsource` imports
   176‚Üí**And** the type scale is defined: price 48px weight 600, values 20-24px weight 500, labels 12px weight 400, status 11px weight 400
   177‚Üí**And** `src/lib/theme-constants.ts` exports duplicated color values for lightweight-charts JS API configuration
   178‚Üí**And** `.card-glass`, `.price-text`, and `.status-badge` `@apply` compositions are defined (max 3)
   179‚Üí**And** CSS custom properties for runtime animation values (`--price-flash-up`, `--price-flash-down`) are defined in `:root`
   180‚Üí**And** tokens are usable via Tailwind utility classes in any component
   181‚Üí
   182‚Üí### Story 1.3: Page Shell with Responsive Layout
   183‚Üí
   184‚ÜíAs a user,
   185‚ÜíI want the dashboard to load instantly with a dark background and a responsive card grid layout,
   186‚ÜíSo that I never see a flash of white and the page looks polished on both desktop and mobile.
   187‚Üí
   188‚Üí**Acceptance Criteria:**
   189‚Üí
   190‚Üí**Given** a user navigates to the dashboard URL
   191‚Üí**When** the page loads
   192‚Üí**Then** `<html>` has `lang="en"` and `bg-base` (#0a0a0f) set directly to prevent FOUC (FR18)
   193‚Üí**And** `Layout.astro` renders a full-viewport dark background with a centered content container (max-width 720px)
   194‚Üí**And** the layout uses CSS Grid with `grid-template-columns: 1.5fr 1fr` and 24px gaps on desktop (‚â•640px) (FR21)
   195‚Üí**And** the layout collapses to a single-column stack with 16px horizontal padding on mobile (<640px) (FR22)
   196‚Üí**And** the container is vertically centered on desktop and top-aligned on mobile
   197‚Üí**And** no `vh` units are used on mobile ‚Äî `dvh` or `min-h-screen` is used instead
   198‚Üí**And** responsive behavior is CSS-only with no JavaScript (Tailwind `sm:` breakpoint)
   199‚Üí**And** the page renders correctly on Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge latest 2 versions (FR23)
   200‚Üí**And** `index.astro` imports `Layout.astro` and serves as the single page entry
   201‚Üí
   202‚Üí### Story 1.4: Empty Dashboard Cards
   203‚Üí
   204‚ÜíAs a user,
   205‚ÜíI want to see the dashboard layout with labeled card placeholders immediately on page load,
   206‚ÜíSo that the page feels complete and no layout shift occurs when data arrives later.
   207‚Üí
   208‚Üí**Acceptance Criteria:**
   209‚Üí
   210‚Üí**Given** a user loads the dashboard
   211‚Üí**When** the static shell renders (before any data)
   212‚Üí**Then** four glassmorphism cards are visible: Price Card, Market Cap Card, Chart Card, Status Indicator area
   213‚Üí**And** each card uses the `.card-glass` pattern: `bg-card` + `backdrop-blur(12px)` + 1px border rgba(255,255,255,0.08) + 16px border-radius
   214‚Üí**And** Price Card displays label "Bitcoin BTC/USD" in `font-label`, `text-secondary`, 12px
   215‚Üí**And** Market Cap Card displays label "Market Cap" in `font-label`, `text-secondary`, 12px
   216‚Üí**And** Chart Card displays label "24h Chart" in `font-label`, `text-secondary`, 12px and spans full grid width on desktop
   217‚Üí**And** each card has reserved space (fixed height) for its data values so CLS = 0 when data populates (FR20)
   218‚Üí**And** Price Card has `<section aria-label="Bitcoin price">` with `<output aria-live="polite">` placeholder
   219‚Üí**And** Market Cap Card has `<section aria-label="Market data">`
   220‚Üí**And** Chart Card has `<section aria-label="24 hour price chart">`
   221‚Üí**And** Status Indicator area has `<div role="status" aria-live="polite">` at the bottom of the viewport
   222‚Üí**And** the dark-themed crypto aesthetic is visually complete ‚Äî near-black background, glassmorphism cards, neon accents visible (FR17)
   223‚Üí
   224‚Üí## Epic 2: Live Bitcoin Price Display
   225‚Üí
   226‚ÜíThe user can glance at the dashboard (or tab title) and instantly know the current BTC price, its direction, and 24h change ‚Äî updated in real-time.
   227‚Üí
   228‚Üí### Story 2.1: Nano Stores & Number Formatting Utilities
   229‚Üí
   230‚ÜíAs a developer,
   231‚ÜíI want reactive stores and consistent number formatting helpers in place,
   232‚ÜíSo that all components share a single source of truth for data and display values identically.
   233‚Üí
   234‚Üí**Acceptance Criteria:**
   235‚Üí
   236‚Üí**Given** the project structure from Epic 1
   237‚Üí**When** the store and utility files are created
   238‚Üí**Then** `src/lib/stores/price-store.ts` exports `priceStore` as `atom<PriceTick | null>(null)`
   239‚Üí**And** `src/lib/stores/status-store.ts` exports `statusStore` as `atom<ConnectionStatus>('connecting')`
   240‚Üí**And** `src/lib/stores/market-store.ts` exports `marketStore` as `atom<MarketData | null>(null)`
   241‚Üí**And** `src/lib/stores/chart-store.ts` exports `chartStore` as `atom<ChartPoint[]>([])`
   242‚Üí**And** every store is typed with explicit generics and initializes to `null` or empty (never undefined)
   243‚Üí**And** `src/lib/utils/format-utils.ts` exports: `formatPrice()` ‚Üí `$68,432.17`, `formatPercent()` ‚Üí `+2.3%` or `-1.8%`, `formatAbsoluteChange()` ‚Üí `+$1,534.22`, `formatMarketCap()` ‚Üí `$1.34T`, `formatRange()` ‚Üí `$66.9k ‚Äî $68.9k`, `formatChartTooltip()` ‚Üí `$68,432.17 at 14:30`
   244‚Üí**And** all formatters use `Intl.NumberFormat` as the base ‚Äî no manual string building
   245‚Üí**And** `format-utils.test.ts` covers all formatters with positive, negative, zero, and edge cases
   246‚Üí**And** tests pass via `npm run test`
   247‚Üí
   248‚Üí### Story 2.2: WebSocket Connection Manager (Binance)
   249‚Üí
   250‚ÜíAs a user,
   251‚ÜíI want the dashboard to connect to a live Bitcoin data source automatically on page load,
   252‚ÜíSo that I see real-time price updates without any action.
   253‚Üí
   254‚Üí**Acceptance Criteria:**
   255‚Üí
   256‚Üí**Given** the dashboard page loads and islands hydrate
   257‚Üí**When** the ConnectionManager initializes
   258‚Üí**Then** it connects to Binance WebSocket at `wss://stream.binance.com:9443/ws/btcusdt@trade` (FR11)
   259‚Üí**And** raw Binance messages (`{ p: "68432.17", ... }`) are normalized to `PriceTick` format: `{ price: number, timestamp: number, direction: 'up' | 'down' | 'neutral' }`
   260‚Üí**And** direction is computed by comparing to the previous price
   261‚Üí**And** normalized `PriceTick` is written to `priceStore`
   262‚Üí**And** `statusStore` is updated to `'live'` on successful connection
   263‚Üí**And** `statusStore` is set to `'connecting'` during initial connection attempt
   264‚Üí**And** the ConnectionManager is a singleton ‚Äî only one WebSocket connection exists at any time
   265‚Üí**And** `connection-manager.ts` never exposes raw WebSocket data to consumers
   266‚Üí**And** old WebSocket listeners are cleaned up before creating new connections (memory leak prevention)
   267‚Üí**And** services initialize via a `<script>` block in `Layout.astro`
   268‚Üí**And** `connection-manager.test.ts` covers message normalization and direction computation
   269‚Üí
   270‚Üí### Story 2.3: Live Price Card with Direction Indicator
   271‚Üí
   272‚ÜíAs a user,
   273‚ÜíI want to see the current BTC price updating live with visual direction feedback,
   274‚ÜíSo that I can glance at the dashboard and instantly know the price and whether it's moving up or down.
   275‚Üí
   276‚Üí**Acceptance Criteria:**
   277‚Üí
   278‚Üí**Given** the Price Card island is hydrated and priceStore has data
   279‚Üí**When** a new PriceTick arrives in priceStore
   280‚Üí**Then** the price displays in `font-price` at 48px desktop / 36px mobile, weight 600, `text-primary` color (FR1)
   281‚Üí**And** a direction arrow is shown: `‚Üë` in `price-up` green for up, `‚Üì` in `price-down` red for down (FR3, FR5)
   282‚Üí**And** the price text flashes cyan (`price-flash-up`) on price increase or red (`price-flash-down`) on decrease, fading to white over 400ms ease-out (UX-DR9)
   283‚Üí**And** flash frequency is throttled to max 1 per 200ms to prevent rapid flashing
   284‚Üí**And** 24h change displays as percentage (`+2.3%`) and absolute value (`+$1,534.22`) using format-utils (FR4)
   285‚Üí**And** 24h change values use `price-up` green for positive and `price-down` red for negative (FR5)
   286‚Üí**And** all number formatting goes through `format-utils.ts` ‚Äî no inline formatting
   287‚Üí**And** on initial data load, the price fades in with a 300ms ease-out animation (FR19)
   288‚Üí**And** all animations respect `prefers-reduced-motion` (instant transitions when enabled)
   289‚Üí**And** the `<output aria-live="polite">` element updates so screen readers announce price changes
   290‚Üí
   291‚Üí### Story 2.4: Tab Title Price Updates
   292‚Üí
   293‚ÜíAs a user,
   294‚ÜíI want the browser tab title to show the current BTC price,
   295‚ÜíSo that I can see the price without switching to the Metrics tab.
   296‚Üí
   297‚Üí**Acceptance Criteria:**
   298‚Üí
   299‚Üí**Given** the dashboard is live and priceStore is receiving updates
   300‚Üí**When** a new PriceTick arrives
   301‚Üí**Then** the browser tab title updates to show the formatted price, e.g. `$68,432.17 ‚Äî Metrics` (FR2)
   302‚Üí**And** the tab title uses the same `formatPrice()` function from format-utils for consistency
   303‚Üí**And** the tab title updates on every price tick (not throttled separately from the display)
   304‚Üí**And** if priceStore is `null` (no data yet), the tab title shows `Metrics` without a price
   305‚Üí
   306‚Üí## Epic 3: Market Context & Price Chart
   307‚Üí
   308‚ÜíThe user gets the full market picture ‚Äî market cap, 24h range, and a 24h sparkline chart with hover tooltips ‚Äî completing the dashboard's information offering.
   309‚Üí
   310‚Üí### Story 3.1: Market Data Service (CoinGecko REST Polling)
   311‚Üí
   312‚ÜíAs a user,
   313‚ÜíI want the dashboard to fetch market data automatically in the background,
   314‚ÜíSo that I see up-to-date market cap, 24h change, and chart data without any action.
   315‚Üí
   316‚Üí**Acceptance Criteria:**
   317‚Üí
   318‚Üí**Given** the dashboard page loads and services initialize
   319‚Üí**When** the MarketDataService starts
   320‚Üí**Then** it fetches market cap, 24h change (percentage + absolute), 24h high/low, and hourly historical price data from CoinGecko REST API
   321‚Üí**And** `marketStore` is populated with a `MarketData` object: `{ marketCap, change24h, changePercent24h, high24h, low24h }`
   322‚Üí**And** `chartStore` is populated with an array of `ChartPoint` objects: `{ time: number, value: number }` for hourly data
   323‚Üí**And** `chartStore` is capped at 24 entries maximum ‚Äî oldest point shifted when appending new data
   324‚Üí**And** polling uses recursive `setTimeout` (not `setInterval`) with a 60-second interval (NFR11)
   325‚Üí**And** on CoinGecko API failure, last known store values are retained ‚Äî stores are never cleared (NFR12)
   326‚Üí**And** API errors are logged via `console.warn()` with endpoint and status code ‚Äî no user-facing error UI
   327‚Üí**And** timer references are stored and cleared before scheduling new timers (memory leak prevention)
   328‚Üí**And** `market-data-service.test.ts` covers data mapping, store updates, and error retention behavior
   329‚Üí
   330‚Üí### Story 3.2: Market Cap Card
   331‚Üí
   332‚ÜíAs a user,
   333‚ÜíI want to see the current Bitcoin market cap and 24h price range at a glance,
   334‚ÜíSo that I have broader market context alongside the live price.
   335‚Üí
   336‚Üí**Acceptance Criteria:**
   337‚Üí
   338‚Üí**Given** the Market Cap Card island is hydrated and marketStore has data
   339‚Üí**When** marketStore updates with new MarketData
   340‚Üí**Then** market cap displays as an abbreviated value (e.g. `$1.34T`) in `font-price`, `text-primary`, 24px weight 500 (FR6)
   341‚Üí**And** "Market Cap" label is shown in `font-label`, `text-secondary`, 12px
   342‚Üí**And** 24h range displays as `$66.9k ‚Äî $68.9k` in `font-price`, `text-primary`, 14px
   343‚Üí**And** "24h Range" secondary label is shown in `font-label`, `text-secondary`, 12px
   344‚Üí**And** all values use `formatMarketCap()` and `formatRange()` from format-utils ‚Äî no inline formatting
   345‚Üí**And** values update silently every 60s with no flash animation (FR7)
   346‚Üí**And** on initial data load, values fade in with 300ms ease-out animation
   347‚Üí**And** when marketStore is `null` (no data yet), labels are visible but value areas are empty (reserved space)
   348‚Üí
   349‚Üí### Story 3.3: 24h Sparkline Chart with Hover Tooltip
   350‚Üí
   351‚ÜíAs a user,
   352‚ÜíI want to see a 24-hour price trend chart that I can hover for details,
   353‚ÜíSo that I can quickly read the trend shape and check specific price points.
   354‚Üí
   355‚Üí**Acceptance Criteria:**
   356‚Üí
   357‚Üí**Given** the Chart Card island is hydrated and chartStore has hourly data
   358‚Üí**When** chartStore populates with ChartPoint array
   359‚Üí**Then** a lightweight-charts area series renders with line color `accent-primary` (#00f5ff) (FR8)
   360‚Üí**And** area fill uses gradient: rgba(0,245,255,0.1) top to transparent bottom
   361‚Üí**And** chart grid lines are rgba(255,255,255,0.03), crosshair rgba(255,255,255,0.3)
   362‚Üí**And** chart background is transparent (card provides the background)
   363‚Üí**And** all chart colors are sourced from `theme-constants.ts` ‚Äî no hardcoded values in component
   364‚Üí**And** chart displays hourly data points for the 24-hour period (FR10)
   365‚Üí**And** on hover, a crosshair appears with a tooltip showing price and time formatted as `$68,432.17 at 14:30` using `formatChartTooltip()` (FR9)
   366‚Üí**And** on initial data load, the chart draws left-to-right with a 500ms ease-out animation (UX-DR6)
   367‚Üí**And** the chart auto-resizes via lightweight-charts built-in resize handling on viewport change
   368‚Üí**And** the chart card spans full grid width (`grid-column: 1 / -1`) on desktop
   369‚Üí**And** chart canvas has `aria-hidden="true"` ‚Äî price data is accessible via Price Card
   370‚Üí**And** animation respects `prefers-reduced-motion` (instant draw when enabled)
   371‚Üí**And** when chartStore updates with new data, the chart appends points without full redraw or flicker
   372‚Üí
   373‚Üí## Epic 4: Connection Resilience & Reliability
   374‚Üí
   375‚ÜíThe dashboard never breaks ‚Äî WebSocket failures are handled silently with fallback sources, automatic reconnection, and clear stale data indication. The user can trust the data is always there.
   376‚Üí
   377‚Üí### Story 4.1: WebSocket Reconnection with Exponential Backoff
   378‚Üí
   379‚ÜíAs a user,
   380‚ÜíI want the dashboard to automatically recover from connection drops without my intervention,
   381‚ÜíSo that the live price feed resumes seamlessly after network interruptions.
   382‚Üí
   383‚Üí**Acceptance Criteria:**
   384‚Üí
   385‚Üí**Given** the ConnectionManager has an active Binance WebSocket connection
   386‚Üí**When** the WebSocket connection drops (error, close, or network failure)
   387‚Üí**Then** `statusStore` transitions to `'reconnecting'` (FR14)
   388‚Üí**And** reconnection attempts begin with exponential backoff: 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s, 30s cap (NFR10)
   389‚Üí**And** on successful reconnection, `statusStore` transitions back to `'live'`
   390‚Üí**And** the previous WebSocket instance is fully closed (`ws.close()`) and its reference nulled before creating a new connection (memory leak prevention, NFR17)
   391‚Üí**And** no JavaScript errors or white screens occur during disconnection or reconnection (NFR16)
   392‚Üí**And** Binance 24-hour connection expiry is handled as a standard disconnect/reconnect cycle ‚Äî no special logic (NFR13)
   393‚Üí**And** the last known price remains displayed in the Price Card during reconnection (NFR15)
   394‚Üí**And** `connection-manager.test.ts` covers backoff timing, listener cleanup, and reconnection state transitions
   395‚Üí
   396‚Üí### Story 4.2: CoinCap Fallback & REST Polling Fallback
   397‚Üí
   398‚ÜíAs a user,
   399‚ÜíI want the dashboard to try alternative data sources if the primary one fails,
   400‚ÜíSo that I always see price data regardless of which service is available.
   401‚Üí
   402‚Üí**Acceptance Criteria:**
   403‚Üí
   404‚Üí**Given** the ConnectionManager fails to connect or reconnect to Binance
   405‚Üí**When** Binance WebSocket is unavailable after reconnection attempts
   406‚Üí**Then** the ConnectionManager attempts to connect to CoinCap WebSocket at `wss://ws.coincap.io/prices?assets=bitcoin` (FR12)
   407‚Üí**And** CoinCap messages (`{ bitcoin: "68432.17" }`) are normalized to the same `PriceTick` format
   408‚Üí**And** on successful CoinCap connection, `statusStore` transitions to `'fallback'`
   409‚Üí**And** fallback from Binance to CoinCap completes without user intervention (NFR14)
   410‚Üí**And** if both Binance and CoinCap WebSockets fail, the system falls back to REST polling via the MarketDataService for price data (FR16)
   411‚Üí**And** during REST-only mode, `statusStore` reflects `'stale'` if no update received within 30 seconds
   412‚Üí**And** the dashboard remains functional displaying last known data when all external APIs are unreachable (NFR15)
   413‚Üí**And** when a higher-priority source becomes available again, the ConnectionManager reconnects to it
   414‚Üí**And** `connection-manager.test.ts` covers the full fallback chain: Binance ‚Üí CoinCap ‚Üí REST
   415‚Üí
   416‚Üí### Story 4.3: Status Indicator & Stale Data Badge
   417‚Üí
   418‚ÜíAs a user,
   419‚ÜíI want to see a subtle connection status indicator and know when displayed data may be outdated,
   420‚ÜíSo that I can trust the data when it's live and be aware when it's stale.
   421‚Üí
   422‚Üí**Acceptance Criteria:**
   423‚Üí
   424‚Üí**Given** the Status Indicator island is hydrated and subscribes to statusStore
   425‚Üí**When** statusStore changes value
   426‚Üí**Then** the indicator displays at the bottom center of the viewport as a 6px dot + text at 11px `text-muted` (UX-DR7)
   427‚Üí**And** Live state shows: green dot (`status-live`) + "Live" text ‚Äî barely visible, quiet good state
   428‚Üí**And** Reconnecting state shows: amber dot (`status-reconnecting`) + "Reconnecting..." text with dot pulsing animation (1.5s ease-in-out cycle, opacity 0.4‚Äì1.0) (FR14)
   429‚Üí**And** Stale state shows: red dot (`status-stale`) + "Connection lost" text, static (no animation)
   430‚Üí**And** Fallback state shows: green dot (`status-live`) + "Live ‚Äî CoinCap" text
   431‚Üí**And** state transitions fade over 300ms ease-in-out ‚Äî no abrupt switches
   432‚Üí**And** when statusStore is `'stale'` (no price update for >30 seconds), a "Stale" badge appears on the Price Card in `status-stale` color (FR15)
   433‚Üí**And** the stale badge does not hide or replace the price value ‚Äî it's an addition
   434‚Üí**And** when connection restores and a new price tick arrives, the stale badge fades out over 300ms
   435‚Üí**And** all animations respect `prefers-reduced-motion` (instant transitions, no pulsing)
   436‚Üí**And** the Status Indicator uses `<div role="status" aria-live="polite">` so screen readers announce state changes
   437‚Üí
