1‚Üí# Story 1.2: Design Token System & Typography
     2‚Üí
     3‚ÜíStatus: ready-for-dev
     4‚Üí
     5‚Üí## Story
     6‚Üí
     7‚ÜíAs a developer,
     8‚ÜíI want a complete design token system with semantic colors, typography, and shared theme constants,
     9‚Üíso that all components use a consistent dark crypto visual identity and the chart library stays in sync.
    10‚Üí
    11‚Üí## Acceptance Criteria
    12‚Üí
    13‚Üí1. **Semantic color tokens via `@theme`** ‚Äî `src/styles/global.css` defines all semantic color tokens using Tailwind v4 `@theme` directives: `bg-base` (#0a0a0f), `bg-card` (#12121a), `bg-card-hover` (#1a1a2e), `accent-primary` (#00f5ff), `accent-secondary` (#8b5cf6), `accent-glow` (rgba(0,245,255,0.15)), `price-up` (#22c55e), `price-down` (#ef4444), `price-flash-up` (#00f5ff), `price-flash-down` (#ef4444), `text-primary` (#f0f0f0), `text-secondary` (#a0a0b0), `text-muted` (#606070), `status-live` (#22c55e), `status-reconnecting` (#f59e0b), `status-stale` (#ef4444).
    14‚Üí2. **Font families configured** ‚Äî JetBrains Mono as `font-price` and Inter as `font-label` via `@fontsource` imports in `Layout.astro` and `@theme` `--font-*` variables.
    15‚Üí3. **Type scale defined** ‚Äî Price: 48px weight 600, values: 20-24px weight 500, labels: 12px weight 400, status: 11px weight 400, chart tooltip: 13px weight 400.
    16‚Üí4. **Theme constants file** ‚Äî `src/lib/theme-constants.ts` exports duplicated color values for lightweight-charts JS API configuration.
    17‚Üí5. **`@apply` compositions** ‚Äî `.card-glass`, `.price-text`, and `.status-badge` compositions defined in `global.css` (max 3).
    18‚Üí6. **CSS custom properties for animations** ‚Äî `:root` defines `--price-flash-up`, `--price-flash-down` runtime animation values.
    19‚Üí7. **Tokens usable via Tailwind utilities** ‚Äî All tokens work as Tailwind utility classes (e.g., `bg-bg-base`, `text-text-primary`, `font-price`).
    20‚Üí
    21‚Üí## Tasks / Subtasks
    22‚Üí
    23‚Üí- [ ] Task 1: Configure `@theme` color tokens in `global.css` (AC: #1, #7)
    24‚Üí  - [ ] Add `@theme` block with all `--color-*` semantic tokens after `@import "tailwindcss"`
    25‚Üí  - [ ] Verify tokens generate correct utility classes (e.g., `bg-bg-base`, `text-accent-primary`)
    26‚Üí- [ ] Task 2: Configure font families (AC: #2)
    27‚Üí  - [ ] Add `@fontsource/jetbrains-mono` and `@fontsource/inter` imports in `Layout.astro` frontmatter
    28‚Üí  - [ ] Add `--font-price` and `--font-label` in `@theme` block
    29‚Üí- [ ] Task 3: Define `@apply` compositions (AC: #5)
    30‚Üí  - [ ] Add `.card-glass` ‚Äî `bg-card` + backdrop-blur(12px) + 1px border rgba(255,255,255,0.08) + 16px border-radius + optional glow
    31‚Üí  - [ ] Add `.price-text` ‚Äî font-price + 48px + weight 600 + text-primary + line-height 1.1
    32‚Üí  - [ ] Add `.status-badge` ‚Äî font-label + 11px + weight 400 + text-muted + line-height 1.3
    33‚Üí- [ ] Task 4: Define CSS custom properties for animations (AC: #6)
    34‚Üí  - [ ] Add `:root` block with `--price-flash-up`, `--price-flash-down`, animation duration variables
    35‚Üí- [ ] Task 5: Create `theme-constants.ts` (AC: #4)
    36‚Üí  - [ ] Export all color values needed by lightweight-charts JS API
    37‚Üí  - [ ] Include chart-specific colors: line color, area gradient, grid lines, crosshair, background
    38‚Üí- [ ] Task 6: Write unit tests for theme-constants (AC: #4)
    39‚Üí  - [ ] Create `src/lib/theme-constants.test.ts` verifying all exports exist and have correct values
    40‚Üí- [ ] Task 7: Verify integration (AC: #7)
    41‚Üí  - [ ] `npm run build` succeeds with new tokens
    42‚Üí  - [ ] `npm run test` passes
    43‚Üí  - [ ] `npm run lint` passes
    44‚Üí
    45‚Üí## Dev Notes
    46‚Üí
    47‚Üí### Tailwind CSS v4 `@theme` Syntax (CRITICAL)
    48‚Üí
    49‚ÜíTailwind v4 uses CSS-first configuration. Tokens are defined via `@theme` directive in `global.css`, NOT in a `tailwind.config.js` file. The `@theme` block defines CSS custom properties that generate utility classes.
    50‚Üí
    51‚Üí**Color namespace:** `--color-<name>` generates utilities like `bg-<name>`, `text-<name>`, `border-<name>`.
    52‚Üí**Font namespace:** `--font-<name>` generates the `font-<name>` utility class.
    53‚Üí
    54‚Üí```css
    55‚Üí@import "tailwindcss";
    56‚Üí
    57‚Üí@theme {
    58‚Üí  --color-bg-base: #0a0a0f;
    59‚Üí  --color-bg-card: #12121a;
    60‚Üí  --font-price: "JetBrains Mono", monospace;
    61‚Üí  --font-label: "Inter", sans-serif;
    62‚Üí}
    63‚Üí```
    64‚Üí
    65‚ÜíThis generates: `bg-bg-base`, `text-bg-card`, `font-price`, `font-label` etc.
    66‚Üí
    67‚Üí**IMPORTANT:** Do NOT use `@theme inline` ‚Äî we want tokens as actual CSS custom properties for runtime access. The standard `@theme` directive already creates CSS variables.
    68‚Üí
    69‚Üí### Complete `global.css` Structure
    70‚Üí
    71‚Üí```css
    72‚Üí@import "tailwindcss";
    73‚Üí
    74‚Üí@theme {
    75‚Üí  /* Background colors */
    76‚Üí  --color-bg-base: #0a0a0f;
    77‚Üí  --color-bg-card: #12121a;
    78‚Üí  --color-bg-card-hover: #1a1a2e;
    79‚Üí
    80‚Üí  /* Accent colors */
    81‚Üí  --color-accent-primary: #00f5ff;
    82‚Üí  --color-accent-secondary: #8b5cf6;
    83‚Üí  --color-accent-glow: rgba(0, 245, 255, 0.15);
    84‚Üí
    85‚Üí  /* Price direction colors */
    86‚Üí  --color-price-up: #22c55e;
    87‚Üí  --color-price-down: #ef4444;
    88‚Üí  --color-price-flash-up: #00f5ff;
    89‚Üí  --color-price-flash-down: #ef4444;
    90‚Üí
    91‚Üí  /* Text colors */
    92‚Üí  --color-text-primary: #f0f0f0;
    93‚Üí  --color-text-secondary: #a0a0b0;
    94‚Üí  --color-text-muted: #606070;
    95‚Üí
    96‚Üí  /* Status colors */
    97‚Üí  --color-status-live: #22c55e;
    98‚Üí  --color-status-reconnecting: #f59e0b;
    99‚Üí  --color-status-stale: #ef4444;
   100‚Üí
   101‚Üí  /* Font families */
   102‚Üí  --font-price: "JetBrains Mono", monospace;
   103‚Üí  --font-label: "Inter", sans-serif;
   104‚Üí}
   105‚Üí
   106‚Üí/* Runtime animation values ‚Äî accessible via JS getComputedStyle */
   107‚Üí:root {
   108‚Üí  --price-flash-up: #00f5ff;
   109‚Üí  --price-flash-down: #ef4444;
   110‚Üí  --animation-fade-in: 300ms;
   111‚Üí  --animation-price-flash: 400ms;
   112‚Üí  --animation-chart-draw: 500ms;
   113‚Üí  --animation-state-transition: 300ms;
   114‚Üí  --animation-reconnect-pulse: 1.5s;
   115‚Üí}
   116‚Üí
   117‚Üí/* Utility compositions (MAX 3 ‚Äî architecture mandate) */
   118‚Üí.card-glass {
   119‚Üí  @apply bg-bg-card rounded-2xl;
   120‚Üí  backdrop-filter: blur(12px);
   121‚Üí  border: 1px solid rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.08);
   122‚Üí  box-shadow: 0 0 20px rgba(139, 92, 246, 0.1);
   123‚Üí}
   124‚Üí
   125‚Üí.price-text {
   126‚Üí  @apply font-price text-text-primary font-semibold leading-none;
   127‚Üí  font-size: 48px;
   128‚Üí  line-height: 1.1;
   129‚Üí}
   130‚Üí
   131‚Üí.status-badge {
   132‚Üí  @apply font-label text-text-muted font-normal;
   133‚Üí  font-size: 11px;
   134‚Üí  line-height: 1.3;
   135‚Üí}
   136‚Üí```
   137‚Üí
   138‚Üí### Font Loading in Layout.astro
   139‚Üí
   140‚ÜíAdd these imports in the `Layout.astro` frontmatter:
   141‚Üí
   142‚Üí```astro
   143‚Üí---
   144‚Üíimport '../styles/global.css';
   145‚Üíimport '@fontsource/jetbrains-mono/400.css';
   146‚Üíimport '@fontsource/jetbrains-mono/500.css';
   147‚Üíimport '@fontsource/jetbrains-mono/600.css';
   148‚Üíimport '@fontsource/inter/400.css';
   149‚Üí---
   150‚Üí```
   151‚Üí
   152‚ÜíOnly import the weights actually used:
   153‚Üí- JetBrains Mono: 400 (chart tooltip), 500 (values), 600 (price)
   154‚Üí- Inter: 400 (labels, status)
   155‚Üí
   156‚Üí### theme-constants.ts Structure
   157‚Üí
   158‚Üí```typescript
   159‚Üí// src/lib/theme-constants.ts
   160‚Üí// Duplicated color values for lightweight-charts JS API.
   161‚Üí// These MUST stay in sync with @theme tokens in global.css.
   162‚Üí// Acceptable duplication for v1 (5 chart-specific values).
   163‚Üí
   164‚Üíexport const CHART_COLORS = {
   165‚Üí  lineColor: '#00f5ff',          // accent-primary
   166‚Üí  areaTopColor: 'rgba(0, 245, 255, 0.1)',
   167‚Üí  areaBottomColor: 'transparent',
   168‚Üí  gridLinesColor: 'rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.03)',
   169‚Üí  crosshairColor: 'rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.3)',
   170‚Üí  backgroundColor: 'transparent', // card provides background
   171‚Üí  textColor: '#f0f0f0',          // text-primary
   172‚Üí} as const;
   173‚Üí
   174‚Üíexport const PRICE_COLORS = {
   175‚Üí  up: '#22c55e',       // price-up
   176‚Üí  down: '#ef4444',     // price-down
   177‚Üí  flashUp: '#00f5ff',  // price-flash-up (cyan)
   178‚Üí  flashDown: '#ef4444', // price-flash-down
   179‚Üí  neutral: '#f0f0f0',  // text-primary
   180‚Üí} as const;
   181‚Üí
   182‚Üíexport const STATUS_COLORS = {
   183‚Üí  live: '#22c55e',
   184‚Üí  reconnecting: '#f59e0b',
   185‚Üí  stale: '#ef4444',
   186‚Üí} as const;
   187‚Üí```
   188‚Üí
   189‚Üí### Type Scale Reference (from UX Spec)
   190‚Üí
   191‚Üí| Element | Font | Size | Weight | Color Token | Line Height |
   192‚Üí|---------|------|------|--------|-------------|-------------|
   193‚Üí| Live price (desktop) | font-price | 48px | 600 | text-primary | 1.1 |
   194‚Üí| Live price (mobile) | font-price | 36px | 600 | text-primary | 1.1 |
   195‚Üí| 24h change value | font-price | 20px | 500 | price-up/price-down | 1.1 |
   196‚Üí| Market cap value | font-price | 20px-24px | 500 | text-primary | 1.1 |
   197‚Üí| Card labels | font-label | 12px | 400 | text-secondary | 1.4 |
   198‚Üí| Status text | font-label | 11px | 400 | text-muted | 1.3 |
   199‚Üí| Chart tooltip | font-price | 13px | 400 | text-primary | 1.1 |
   200‚Üí
   201‚Üí### Anti-Patterns (DO NOT)
   202‚Üí
   203‚Üí- Do NOT create a `tailwind.config.js` ‚Äî Tailwind v4 uses CSS-first `@theme` directives only
   204‚Üí- Do NOT use `@theme inline` ‚Äî we need tokens as CSS custom properties for runtime access
   205‚Üí- Do NOT add more than 3 `@apply` compositions ‚Äî architecture mandates `.card-glass`, `.price-text`, `.status-badge` only
   206‚Üí- Do NOT use raw color names like `cyan-400` ‚Äî always use semantic tokens (`accent-primary`, `text-muted`)
   207‚Üí- Do NOT create barrel files (`index.ts`) ‚Äî import directly from `theme-constants.ts`
   208‚Üí- Do NOT add components, stores, services, or any UI ‚Äî this story is tokens and constants only
   209‚Üí- Do NOT modify `src/lib/types.ts` ‚Äî interfaces are already defined from Story 1.1
   210‚Üí- Do NOT use `theme.extend` pattern ‚Äî that's Tailwind v3. Use `@theme` block in CSS.
   211‚Üí
   212‚Üí### Previous Story Intelligence (Story 1.1)
   213‚Üí
   214‚Üí**What was established:**
   215‚Üí- `global.css` exists with only `@import "tailwindcss"` ‚Äî extend it, don't replace the import
   216‚Üí- `Layout.astro` exists with `import '../styles/global.css'` ‚Äî add font imports below it
   217‚Üí- `@fontsource/jetbrains-mono` and `@fontsource/inter` are already installed as production dependencies
   218‚Üí- `lightweight-charts` is installed (v5.1.0) ‚Äî `theme-constants.ts` values will be consumed by Story 3.3
   219‚Üí- Vitest is configured with `include: ['src/**/*.test.ts']`
   220‚Üí- ESLint flat config with `eslint-plugin-astro`
   221‚Üí- No `tailwind.config.js` exists (confirmed in Story 1.1 review)
   222‚Üí
   223‚Üí**Review feedback from 1.1:**
   224‚Üí- Layout.astro was patched to import `global.css` ‚Äî already in place
   225‚Üí- `astro.config.mjs` was patched to add explicit `output: 'static'` ‚Äî already in place
   226‚Üí
   227‚Üí### Files to Create/Modify
   228‚Üí
   229‚Üí| File | Action | Purpose |
   230‚Üí|------|--------|---------|
   231‚Üí| `src/styles/global.css` | MODIFY | Add `@theme` tokens, `:root` animation vars, `@apply` compositions |
   232‚Üí| `src/layouts/Layout.astro` | MODIFY | Add `@fontsource` weight imports |
   233‚Üí| `src/lib/theme-constants.ts` | CREATE | Lightweight-charts color constants |
   234‚Üí| `src/lib/theme-constants.test.ts` | CREATE | Unit tests for exported constants |
   235‚Üí
   236‚Üí### Project Structure Notes
   237‚Üí
   238‚Üí- All files follow architecture spec paths exactly [Source: architecture.md#Structure Patterns]
   239‚Üí- `theme-constants.ts` lives at `src/lib/theme-constants.ts` (not in `utils/`) per architecture spec
   240‚Üí- Test file co-located: `src/lib/theme-constants.test.ts`
   241‚Üí- No new directories needed ‚Äî all target directories exist from Story 1.1
   242‚Üí
   243‚Üí### References
   244‚Üí
   245‚Üí- [Source: architecture.md#Selected Starter] ‚Äî Tailwind v4 CSS-first config, `@theme` directives, `theme-constants.ts` strategy
   246‚Üí- [Source: architecture.md#Naming Patterns] ‚Äî CSS/Tailwind naming: `--color-accent-primary`, `--color-bg-base`, kebab-case
   247‚Üí- [Source: architecture.md#Naming Patterns] ‚Äî `@apply` compositions: `.card-glass`, `.price-text`, `.status-badge` (max 3)
   248‚Üí- [Source: architecture.md#Structure Patterns] ‚Äî `theme-constants.ts` at `src/lib/`, tests co-located
   249‚Üí- [Source: ux-design-specification.md#Color System] ‚Äî Complete color palette with hex values and contrast ratios
   250‚Üí- [Source: ux-design-specification.md#Typography System] ‚Äî Font families, type scale, weights, line heights
   251‚Üí- [Source: ux-design-specification.md#Spacing & Layout Foundation] ‚Äî Card glassmorphism pattern
   252‚Üí- [Source: epics.md#Story 1.2] ‚Äî Acceptance criteria, BDD scenarios
   253‚Üí- [Source: epics.md#UX-DR1] ‚Äî Full semantic color token list

1‚Üí# Story 1.4: Empty Dashboard Cards 2‚Üí 3‚ÜíStatus: ready-for-dev 4‚Üí 5‚Üí## Story 6‚Üí 7‚ÜíAs a user, 8‚ÜíI want to see the dashboard layout with labeled card placeholders immediately on page load, 9‚Üíso that the page feels complete and no layout shift occurs when data arrives later. 10‚Üí 11‚Üí## Acceptance Criteria 12‚Üí 13‚Üí1. **Four glassmorphism cards visible** ‚Äî Price Card, Market Cap Card, Chart Card, and Status Indicator area are rendered on the static shell before any data loads. 14‚Üí2. **`.card-glass` pattern on each card** ‚Äî Each card uses `bg-card` + `backdrop-blur(12px)` + 1px border `rgba(255,255,255,0.08)` + 16px border-radius (the `.card-glass` composition from Story 1.2). 15‚Üí3. **Price Card label** ‚Äî Displays "Bitcoin BTC/USD" in `font-label`, `text-secondary`, 12px. 16‚Üí4. **Market Cap Card label** ‚Äî Displays "Market Cap" in `font-label`, `text-secondary`, 12px. 17‚Üí5. **Chart Card label** ‚Äî Displays "24h Chart" in `font-label`, `text-secondary`, 12px and spans full grid width on desktop (`grid-column: 1 / -1`). 18‚Üí6. **Reserved space for CLS=0** ‚Äî Each card has a fixed height for its data area so that CLS = 0 when data populates later (FR20, NFR3). 19‚Üí7. **Price Card ARIA** ‚Äî `

Based on the search results, here's information about lightweight-charts v5 `createChart` options with `autoSize` and `ChartOptions`:

## `autoSize` Option

Setting this flag to true will make the chart watch the chart container's size and automatically resize the chart to fit its container whenever the size changes. This feature requires ResizeObserver class to be available in the global scope.

## Usage Example

You can use: `const chart = LightweightCharts.createChart(document.body, { autoSize: true, });`

## Important Details

The autoSize option and explicit sizes options width and height don't conflict with one another. If you specify autoSize flag, then width and height options will be ignored unless ResizeObserver has failed. If it fails then the values will be used as fallback.

The flag autoSize could also be set with and unset with applyOptions function.

## Resize Behavior

The API ignores the resize method if autoSize is active, and there is an API to check if active.

For the complete TypeScript interface definitions and all available `ChartOptions`, you can refer to the official Lightweight Charts API documentation at the interfaces like `TimeChartOptions` and `ChartOptionsBase` in the official docs.

Based on the search results for lightweight-charts v5 and ISeriesApi, here's what I found:

## Key Methods for Data Updates

The `update()` method adds new data items to the existing set (or updates the latest item if times of the passed/latest items are equal). The time of the new item must be greater or equal to the latest existing time point. If the new item's time is equal to the last existing item's time, then the existing item is replaced with the new one.

## setData Method

To set the data to a series, you should call the ISeriesApi.setData method, and if your data is updated in real-time, you can call the ISeriesApi.update method that allows you to update the last data item or add a new one.

## Historical Updates

The `update()` method has a `historicalUpdate` option that, if true, allows updating an existing data point that is not the latest bar, though updating older data using historicalUpdate will be slower than updating the most recent data point.

## Data Changed Event

You can subscribe to the data changed event, which is fired whenever the update or setData method is evoked on the series.

The search results point to the official Lightweight Charts documentation, which contains detailed API specifications and examples for working with these methods in v5.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about the CoinCap WebSocket API and bitcoin message format:

## Connection Details

The CoinCap WebSocket endpoint `wss://ws.coincap.io/prices?assets=bitcoin,ethereum` returns JSON with latest prices.

## Message Format

The message format is simple JSON with asset-price pairs. The messages are parsed as JSON objects where the keys are cryptocurrency tickers and the values are prices.

## Example Usage

A WebSocket connection to the coincap.io API endpoint receives real-time cryptocurrency price data. When messages arrive, they come in the format that can be parsed with `JSON.parse()`, containing a structure like:

```
{
  "bitcoin": "42500.50",
  "ethereum": "2400.25",
  ...
}
```

CoinCap allows streaming multiple assets in one URL query using the format `?assets=bitcoin,ethereum,litecoin`.

The WebSocket connection automatically sends price updates whenever market prices change, making it efficient for real-time price tracking applications without requiring repeated polling requests.

   470‚Üí## User Journey Flows
   471‚Üí
   472‚Üí### Flow 1: Initial Load
   473‚Üí
   474‚Üí```mermaid
   475‚ÜístateDiagram-v2
   476‚Üí    [*] --> URLEntered: User navigates to Metrics
   477‚Üí    URLEntered --> StaticShell: Astro serves static HTML
   478‚Üí    StaticShell --> DarkBackground: bg-base renders immediately (no FOUC)
   479‚Üí    DarkBackground --> CardSkeletons: Empty glassmorphism cards visible with labels
   480‚Üí    CardSkeletons --> IslandHydration: Astro hydrates client islands
   481‚Üí    IslandHydration --> WSConnect: WebSocket connects to Binance
   482‚Üí    IslandHydration --> RESTFetch: CoinGecko REST call (market cap, 24h data)
   483‚Üí    WSConnect --> WSSuccess: Connection established
   484‚Üí    WSConnect --> WSFail: Binance unavailable
   485‚Üí    WSFail --> CoinCapFallback: Try CoinCap WebSocket
   486‚Üí    CoinCapFallback --> WSSuccess: Fallback connected
   487‚Üí    CoinCapFallback --> RESTPolling: Both WebSockets failed
   488‚Üí    RESTPolling --> DataReady: REST polling active (60s interval)
   489‚Üí    WSSuccess --> DataReady: First price tick received
   490‚Üí    RESTFetch --> DataReady: Market cap + 24h data loaded
   491‚Üí    DataReady --> FadeIn: Price, change, cap fade in with animation
   492‚Üí    FadeIn --> ChartDraw: Sparkline draws with animation
   493‚Üí    ChartDraw --> LiveState: Dashboard fully operational
   494‚Üí    LiveState --> TabTitle: Tab title updates with price
   495‚Üí    LiveState --> [*]
   496‚Üí```
   497‚Üí
   498‚Üí**Timing targets:**
   499‚Üí- Static shell visible: < 200ms
   500‚Üí- Islands hydrated: < 800ms
   501‚Üí- First data visible: < 1.5s
   502‚Üí- Fully operational: < 2s
   503‚Üí
   504‚Üí**UX detail:** Empty cards render with labels ("Bitcoin BTC/USD", "Market Cap", "24h Chart") but no data values. This avoids layout shift ‚Äî the space is reserved. Data fades in with a 300ms ease-out transition. Chart draws left-to-right with a 500ms animation.
   505‚Üí
   506‚Üí### Flow 2: Live Operation (Steady State)
   507‚Üí
   508‚Üí```mermaid
   509‚ÜístateDiagram-v2
   510‚Üí    [*] --> Idle: Dashboard is live, user is elsewhere
   511‚Üí    Idle --> TabGlance: User sees price in tab title
   512‚Üí    TabGlance --> Aware: User knows price without switching
   513‚Üí    TabGlance --> FocusTab: User switches to Metrics tab
   514‚Üí    Idle --> FocusTab: User switches to Metrics tab
   515‚Üí    FocusTab --> PriceRead: Eyes land on price card (largest element)
   516‚Üí    PriceRead --> ChangeRead: Eyes scan to 24h change (green/red)
   517‚Üí    ChangeRead --> ChartScan: Eyes drop to sparkline (trend shape)
   518‚Üí    ChartScan --> Done: User has complete picture
   519‚Üí    PriceRead --> Done: User only needed the price
   520‚Üí    Done --> Idle: User switches away
   521‚Üí
   522‚Üí    state "Background Activity" as bg {
   523‚Üí        WSStream --> PriceTick: New trade received
   524‚Üí        PriceTick --> FlashColor: Brief cyan (up) or red (down) flash
   525‚Üí        FlashColor --> UpdateDOM: Price text updates
   526‚Üí        UpdateDOM --> UpdateTab: Tab title updates
   527‚Üí        UpdateTab --> WSStream: Wait for next tick
   528‚Üí        RESTTimer --> RESTCall: Every 60 seconds
   529‚Üí        RESTCall --> UpdateMcap: Market cap refreshes
   530‚Üí        UpdateMcap --> Update24h: 24h change refreshes
   531‚Üí        Update24h --> RESTTimer: Reset timer
   532‚Üí    }
   533‚Üí```
   534‚Üí
   535‚Üí**UX detail:** Price updates are near-continuous (Binance sends tick-by-tick trades). Each update triggers a brief color flash ‚Äî the price text transitions to `price-flash-up` (cyan) or `price-flash-down` (red) then fades back to `text-primary` (white) over 400ms. This creates the "alive" feeling without being distracting. Market cap and 24h change update silently every 60s ‚Äî no flash needed for these.
   536‚Üí
   537‚Üí### Flow 3: Connection Recovery
   538‚Üí
   539‚Üí```mermaid
   540‚ÜístateDiagram-v2
   541‚Üí    [*] --> Live: Dashboard operating normally
   542‚Üí    Live --> Disconnect: WebSocket connection drops
   543‚Üí    Disconnect --> ShowIndicator: "Reconnecting..." appears in text-muted
   544‚Üí    ShowIndicator --> Attempt1: Backoff: 1 second
   545‚Üí    Attempt1 --> Reconnected: Success
   546‚Üí    Attempt1 --> Attempt2: Fail ‚Üí backoff: 2 seconds
   547‚Üí    Attempt2 --> Reconnected: Success
   548‚Üí    Attempt2 --> Attempt3: Fail ‚Üí backoff: 4 seconds
   549‚Üí    Attempt3 --> Reconnected: Success
   550‚Üí    Attempt3 --> AttemptN: Fail ‚Üí continue doubling (max 30s)
   551‚Üí    AttemptN --> Reconnected: Success
   552‚Üí    AttemptN --> StaleState: Extended failure (> 30 seconds)
   553‚Üí    StaleState --> ShowStale: Stale data badge appears on price card
   554‚Üí    StaleState --> TryFallback: Switch to CoinCap / REST polling
   555‚Üí    TryFallback --> FallbackLive: Fallback connected
   556‚Üí    FallbackLive --> HideStale: Remove stale badge, show "Live" again
   557‚Üí    TryFallback --> StaleState: All sources failed
   558‚Üí    Reconnected --> HideIndicator: Remove "Reconnecting..." text
   559‚Üí    HideIndicator --> Live: Resume normal operation
   560‚Üí    HideStale --> Live: Resume normal operation
   561‚Üí
   562‚Üí    Live --> Expiry24h: Binance 24h connection expiry
   563‚Üí    Expiry24h --> Disconnect: Treated as normal disconnect
   564‚Üí```
   565‚Üí
   566‚Üí**UX detail:** The "Reconnecting..." text appears in the status area (bottom of viewport) using `text-muted` color ‚Äî barely visible, never alarming. If reconnection takes > 30 seconds, a small badge appears on the price card: "Stale" in `status-stale` color. The price value itself stays visible (last known value) ‚Äî never blank, never hidden. When connection restores, the indicator fades out over 300ms and the next price tick triggers a flash as usual ‚Äî confirming liveness.
   567‚Üí
   568‚Üí### Flow 4: Responsive Adaptation
   569‚Üí
   570‚Üí```mermaid
   571‚ÜístateDiagram-v2
   572‚Üí    [*] --> DetectViewport: Page loads or viewport resizes
   573‚Üí    DetectViewport --> Desktop: Width ‚â• 640px
   574‚Üí    DetectViewport --> Mobile: Width < 640px
   575‚Üí
   576‚Üí    Desktop --> TwoColGrid: grid-template-columns 1.5fr 1fr
   577‚Üí    TwoColGrid --> PriceLeft: Price card (48px price, change below)
   578‚Üí    TwoColGrid --> McapRight: Market cap card (right column)
   579‚Üí    TwoColGrid --> ChartFull: Chart card spans both columns
   580‚Üí    TwoColGrid --> CenterVert: Container centered vertically on viewport
   581‚Üí
   582‚Üí    Mobile --> SingleCol: Single column, full-width cards
   583‚Üí    SingleCol --> PriceTop: Price card (36px price, change below)
   584‚Üí    SingleCol --> McapMiddle: Market cap card
   585‚Üí    SingleCol --> ChartBottom: Chart card (full width)
   586‚Üí    SingleCol --> PadHoriz: 16px horizontal padding, 24px card gaps
   587‚Üí```
   588‚Üí
   589‚Üí**UX detail:** The transition between layouts uses CSS Grid + media query ‚Äî no JavaScript. On resize, cards reflow instantly. The price font scales from 48px (desktop) to 36px (mobile) via responsive utility. Chart card adjusts height automatically. All functionality is identical across viewports ‚Äî no features hidden on mobile.
   590‚Üí
   591‚Üí### Journey Patterns
   592‚Üí
   593‚Üí**State Communication Pattern:**
   594‚ÜíAll system states (live, reconnecting, stale) are communicated through a single status area at the bottom of the viewport. States escalate visually: live (green dot, nearly invisible) ‚Üí reconnecting (muted text, subtle) ‚Üí stale (colored badge on price card, more visible). The price display is never disrupted.
   595‚Üí
   596‚Üí**Data Update Pattern:**
   597‚ÜíTwo update cadences coexist: high-frequency (WebSocket price ticks, continuous) and low-frequency (REST market data, every 60s). High-frequency updates get visual feedback (color flash). Low-frequency updates are silent. This creates a natural "heartbeat" ‚Äî the price ticking confirms the system is alive.

input.content:
# Story 2.4: Tab Title Price Updates

Status: ready-for-dev

## Story

As a user,
I want the browser tab title to show the current BTC price,
So that I can see the price without switching to the Metrics tab.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **Given** the dashboard is live and priceStore is receiving updates, **When** a new PriceTick arrives, **Then** the browser tab title updates to show the formatted price, e.g. `$68,432.17 ‚Äî Metrics` (FR2).
2. The tab title uses the same `formatPrice()` function from `format-utils.ts` for consistency.
3. The tab title updates on every price tick (not throttled separately from the display).
4. If `priceStore` is `null` (no data yet), the tab title shows `Metrics` without a price.

## Tasks / Subtasks

- [ ] Task 1: Add tab title updater in Layout.astro `<script>` block (AC: #1, #2, #3, #4)
  - [ ] Import `priceStore` from `../lib/stores/price-store`
  - [ ] Import `formatPrice` from `../lib/utils/format-utils`
  - [ ] Subscribe to `priceStore` using `priceStore.subscribe()`
  - [ ] In the callback, if tick is `null`, set `document.title = 'Metrics'`
  - [ ] If tick is not `null`, set `document.title` to `` `${formatPrice(tick.price)} ‚Äî Metrics` ``
  - [ ] Verify the em dash ` ‚Äî ` (U+2014) is used as separator, not a hyphen

- [ ] Task 2: Verify initial state (AC: #4)
  - [ ] On page load before any data, `document.title` should be `Metrics` (set by the `<title>` tag in `Layout.astro` head)
  - [ ] Verify that the subscribe callback fires immediately with `null` and sets title to `Metrics`

- [ ] Task 3: Manual testing and verification (AC: #1, #2, #3)
  - [ ] Run `npm run dev` and verify tab title shows `Metrics` on initial load
  - [ ] Verify tab title updates with formatted price when priceStore receives data
  - [ ] Verify tab title format is exactly `$XX,XXX.XX ‚Äî Metrics`
  - [ ] Verify tab title updates on every price tick (no separate throttling)
  - [ ] `npm run build` succeeds
  - [ ] `npm run lint` passes

## Dev Notes

### SCOPE BOUNDARY

**This story is intentionally small.** It adds a single `priceStore.subscribe()` callback that updates `document.title`. No new files are created. No new components. No tests (the logic is a trivial one-liner that delegates to `formatPrice()`).

### Implementation Location

The tab title updater lives in the existing `<script>` block in `src/layouts/Layout.astro`. This is the same `<script>` block where service initialization happens (ConnectionManager, MarketDataService). The tab title subscription is added alongside (or after) the service initialization code.

**File:** `src/layouts/Layout.astro` (MODIFY ‚Äî add to existing `<script>` block)

### Exact Code

Add the following to the `<script>` block in `Layout.astro`:

```typescript
// Tab title price updates (Story 2.4)
import { priceStore } from '../lib/stores/price-store';
import { formatPrice } from '../lib/utils/format-utils';

priceStore.subscribe((tick) => {
  if (tick === null) {
    document.title = 'Metrics';
  } else {
    document.title = `${formatPrice(tick.price)} ‚Äî Metrics`;
  }
});
```

**That's the entire implementation.** No throttling, no debouncing, no new module.

### Data Flow

```
Binance WS ‚Üí ConnectionManager ‚Üí priceStore.set(PriceTick) ‚Üí subscribe callback ‚Üí document.title
```

This follows the mandatory unidirectional data flow: External API ‚Üí Service ‚Üí Store ‚Üí Consumer ‚Üí DOM.

### Why No Separate File or Module

The architecture spec (architecture.md#Data Architecture) shows the tab title updater as a direct consumer of `priceStore` within `Layout.astro`:

```
Nano Store (priceStore) ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚Üí Tab title updater
```

Creating a separate file (e.g., `src/lib/utils/tab-title.ts`) for a 7-line subscribe callback would be over-engineering. The `Layout.astro` script block is the correct location because:
1. It's where services are initialized (ConnectionManager, MarketDataService)
2. It runs once on page load
3. The subscription is global (not tied to a specific island)

### Why No Throttling

Per AC #3 and the epics.md spec: "the tab title updates on every price tick (not throttled separately from the display)." The tab title should reflect the same price shown in the Price Card at all times. Adding a separate throttle would cause the tab title to lag behind the displayed price, which is confusing.

### Why No Tests

The tab title updater is a trivial glue subscription:
- `formatPrice()` is already tested in `format-utils.test.ts` (Story 2.1)
- `priceStore.subscribe()` is a Nano Stores API ‚Äî already tested by the library
- `document.title` is a browser API

Testing this would require a DOM environment (jsdom/happy-dom) for `document.title`, which adds test infrastructure complexity disproportionate to the logic being tested. The manual testing in Task 3 is sufficient for this story.

### Title Format

- **With price:** `$68,432.17 ‚Äî Metrics`
  - Dollar sign, comma separators, always 2 decimal places (from `formatPrice()`)
  - Space + em dash (U+2014) + space as separator
  - App name "Metrics" after the separator
- **Without price (null):** `Metrics`
  - Just the app name, matching the `<title>` tag in the HTML head

### Integration with Existing Layout.astro

The current `Layout.astro` has a `<title>Metrics</title>` in the head. This sets the initial tab title before any JavaScript runs. The `priceStore.subscribe()` callback will fire immediately with `null` (the initial store value), setting `document.title = 'Metrics'` ‚Äî which matches the HTML `<title>` tag, so there's no visible change on initial load.

When `priceStore` receives its first `PriceTick` from the ConnectionManager, the subscribe callback fires again and updates the title to show the price.

### Nano Stores Subscribe Behavior

`priceStore.subscribe()` fires the callback **immediately** with the current value, then again on every subsequent change. This means:
1. On subscription creation ‚Üí callback fires with `null` ‚Üí title stays `Metrics`
2. On first price tick ‚Üí callback fires with `PriceTick` ‚Üí title becomes `$68,432.17 ‚Äî Metrics`
3. On every subsequent tick ‚Üí title updates

The subscription is never cleaned up because it runs for the entire page lifetime (the page is a single-page dashboard that never navigates away).

### Import Paths (Exact)

```typescript
import { priceStore } from '../lib/stores/price-store';
import { formatPrice } from '../lib/utils/format-utils';
```

These paths are relative from `src/layouts/Layout.astro` to `src/lib/stores/price-store.ts` and `src/lib/utils/format-utils.ts`.

### Anti-Patterns (DO NOT)

- **Do NOT create a new file** for this functionality. No `tab-title-updater.ts`, no `title-service.ts`. It's a subscribe callback in Layout.astro.
- **Do NOT create a new Astro component** for this. Tab title is not a visible DOM element ‚Äî it doesn't need an island.
- **Do NOT throttle or debounce** the tab title separately from the price display. Every price tick updates the title.
- **Do NOT use inline formatting** (`toFixed()`, manual string building). Always use `formatPrice()` from `format-utils.ts`.
- **Do NOT add `document.title` updates to PriceCard.astro** ‚Äî the tab title is a page-level concern, not a component concern. It belongs in Layout.astro.
- **Do NOT show stale indicators in the tab title** ‚Äî when price is stale, the tab title continues to show the last known price. The stale badge on the Price Card (Story 4.3) handles stale indication.
- **Do NOT use `priceStore.listen()`** ‚Äî use `priceStore.subscribe()` which fires immediately with the current value. `listen()` only fires on changes.

### Previous Story Intelligence

**Story 1.1 establishes:**
- `src/layouts/Layout.astro` with `<title>Metrics</title>` in the head
- Project uses Astro 6.0.8, TypeScript strict mode

**Story 2.1 establishes:**
- `src/lib/stores/price-store.ts`: `export const priceStore = atom<PriceTick | null>(null);`
- `src/lib/utils/format-utils.ts`: `formatPrice(price: number): string` ‚Üí `$68,432.17`

**Story 2.2 establishes:**
- `src/lib/services/connection-manager.ts`: singleton that writes normalized `PriceTick` to `priceStore`
- Services initialize via `<script>` block in `Layout.astro`

**Story 2.3 establishes:**
- PriceCard.astro subscribes to `priceStore` for live price display
- The Price Card shows the same `formatPrice()` output ‚Äî tab title must be consistent

### Dependencies

This story depends on:
- **Story 2.1** (Nano Stores & Format Utilities) ‚Äî provides `priceStore` and `formatPrice()`
- **Story 2.2** (ConnectionManager) ‚Äî writes to `priceStore` (without this, priceStore stays null and title stays "Metrics")

This story does NOT depend on:
- Story 2.3 (PriceCard) ‚Äî tab title is independent of the price card display
- Story 1.2/1.3/1.4 ‚Äî tab title doesn't need design tokens or layout

### Files to Create/Modify

| File | Action | Purpose |
|------|--------|---------|
| `src/layouts/Layout.astro` | MODIFY | Add priceStore subscription to `<script>` block for tab title updates |

No new files created. No files deleted.

### References

- [Source: epics.md#Story 2.4] ‚Äî Acceptance criteria
- [Source: architecture.md#Data Architecture] ‚Äî Data flow diagram showing `priceStore ‚Üí Tab title updater`
- [Source: architecture.md#Process Patterns] ‚Äî Initialization sequence: services initialize in Layout.astro `<script>` block
- [Source: architecture.md#Communication Patterns] ‚Äî Unidirectional flow, components/consumers read stores only
- [Source: architecture.md#Format Patterns] ‚Äî `formatPrice()` specification
- [Source: prd.md#FR2] ‚Äî "User can see price updates reflected in the browser tab title"
- [Source: ux-design-specification.md#Platform Strategy] ‚Äî "Tab title: Leveraged as a zero-focus information channel ‚Äî price visible without switching to the tab"
- [Source: 2-1-nano-stores-and-number-formatting-utilities.md] ‚Äî Store and formatter definitions
- [Source: 2-2-websocket-connection-manager-binance.md] ‚Äî ConnectionManager writes to priceStore

## Dev Agent Record

### Agent Model Used

### Debug Log References

### Completion Notes List

### File List

### Review Findings

### Change Log

# Story 3.2: Market Cap Card Status: ready-for-dev ## Story As a user, I want to see the current Bitcoin market cap and 24h price range at a glance, so that I have broader market context alongside the live price. ## Acceptance Criteria 1. **Market cap value displayed** ‚Äî Market cap displays as an abbreviated value (e.g. `$1.34T`) in `font-price`, `text-primary`, 24px weight 500 (FR6). 2. **"Market Cap" label shown** ‚Äî Label is in `font-label`, `text-secondary`, 12px. 3. **24h range displayed** ‚Äî Range displays as `$66.9K ‚Äî $68.9K` in `font-price`, `text-primary`, 14px. 4. **"24h Range" secondary label shown** ‚Äî Label is in `font-label`, `text-secondary`, 12px. 5. **Formatting via format-utils** ‚Äî All values use `formatMarketCap()` and `formatRange()` from `format-utils.ts` ‚Äî no inline formatting. 6. **Silent 60s refresh** ‚Äî Values update silently every 60s with no flash animation (FR7). 7. **Fade-in on initial data** ‚Äî On initial data load, values fade in with 300ms ease-out animation. 8. **Empty state when no data** ‚Äî When `marketStore` is `null` (no data yet), labels are visible but value areas are empty (reserved space from Story 1.4). 9. **Accessibility** ‚Äî `
` wrapping (UX-DR12). 10. **Reduced motion support** ‚Äî All animations respect `prefers-reduced-motion` (instant transitions when enabled). 11. **No store writes** ‚Äî Component never calls `marketStore.set()` ‚Äî read-only subscriber. 12. **No inline formatting** ‚Äî All number display goes through `format-utils.ts`. ## Tasks / Subtasks - [ ] Task 1: Add hydration script to `MarketCapCard.astro` (AC: #1‚Äì#6, #8, #11) - [ ] Add ` ``` ### Key Implementation Details **Store subscription pattern:** - Import `marketStore` directly (no barrel file) - Use `marketStore.subscribe()` for reactive updates - Guard for `null` ‚Äî the store initializes as `null` and only gets data when `MarketDataService` completes its first REST fetch - Component is read-only ‚Äî never calls `marketStore.set()` **Fade-in mechanism:** - Value containers start with `data-loading` attribute and `opacity: 0` - On first non-null data from `marketStore`, add `loaded` class to trigger CSS transition - `requestAnimationFrame()` ensures the browser has painted the `opacity: 0` state before transitioning to `opacity: 1` - Subsequent updates (every 60s) do NOT re-trigger the fade ‚Äî only the first data arrival triggers it - `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` sets `transition-duration: 0ms` for instant display **Silent refresh:** - The `marketStore.subscribe()` callback fires on every store update (every 60s from `MarketDataService`) - No flash animation on subsequent updates ‚Äî just silently updates `textContent` - This matches FR7: "System refreshes supplementary market data periodically without user action" **Typography applied via Tailwind classes (from UX spec):** | Element | Classes | Result | |---------|---------|--------| | Market cap value | `font-price text-text-primary text-[24px] font-medium leading-[1.1]` | JetBrains Mono, #f0f0f0, 24px, weight 500, line-height 1.1 | | Range value | `font-price text-text-primary text-[14px] font-medium leading-[1.1]` | JetBrains Mono, #f0f0f0, 14px, weight 500, line-height 1.1 | | "Market Cap" label | `font-label text-text-secondary text-[12px] font-normal leading-[1.4]` | Inter, #a0a0b0, 12px, weight 400, line-height 1.4 | | "24h Range" label | `font-label text-text-secondary text-[12px] font-normal leading-[1.4]` | Inter, #a0a0b0, 12px, weight 400, line-height 1.4 | **Reserved space (CLS prevention):** - `min-h-[28px]` on market cap value container ‚Äî reserves space for 24px text (line-height 1.1 = ~26px + buffer) - `min-h-[20px]` on range value container ‚Äî reserves space for 14px text - These match the heights established in Story 1.4 ### HTML Structure Compatibility with Story 1.4 Story 1.4 established the empty card structure. This story enhances it by: 1. Adding `data-loading` attribute to value containers (for fade-in) 2. Adding `data-market-cap` and `data-range` attributes to value spans (for JS targeting) 3. Adding the `

input.content:
# Story 4.2: CoinCap Fallback & REST Polling Fallback

Status: ready-for-dev

## Story

As a user,
I want the dashboard to try alternative data sources if the primary one fails,
So that I always see price data regardless of which service is available.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **Given** the ConnectionManager fails to connect or reconnect to Binance **When** Binance WebSocket is unavailable after reconnection attempts **Then** the ConnectionManager attempts to connect to CoinCap WebSocket at `wss://ws.coincap.io/prices?assets=bitcoin` (FR12)
2. **And** CoinCap messages (`{ bitcoin: "68432.17" }`) are normalized to the same `PriceTick` format
3. **And** on successful CoinCap connection, `statusStore` transitions to `'fallback'`
4. **And** fallback from Binance to CoinCap completes without user intervention (NFR14)
5. **And** if both Binance and CoinCap WebSockets fail, the system falls back to REST polling via the MarketDataService for price data (FR16)
6. **And** during REST-only mode, `statusStore` reflects `'stale'` if no update received within 30 seconds
7. **And** the dashboard remains functional displaying last known data when all external APIs are unreachable (NFR15)
8. **And** when a higher-priority source becomes available again, the ConnectionManager reconnects to it
9. **And** `connection-manager.test.ts` covers the full fallback chain: Binance ‚Üí CoinCap ‚Üí REST

## Tasks / Subtasks

- [ ] Task 1: Add fallback constants to `src/lib/services/connection-manager.ts` (AC: #1, #5, #6)
  - [ ] Define `WS_COINCAP_URL = 'wss://ws.coincap.io/prices?assets=bitcoin'`
  - [ ] Define `STALE_THRESHOLD_MS = 30000` (30 seconds)
  - [ ] Define `BINANCE_RETRY_INTERVAL_MS = 60000` (60 seconds ‚Äî how often to attempt Binance reconnection when on fallback)

- [ ] Task 2: Add CoinCap message interface and normalization (AC: #2)
  - [ ] Define `CoinCapPriceMessage` interface: `{ bitcoin: string; [key: string]: string }`
  - [ ] Implement `normalizeCoinCapMessage(raw: CoinCapPriceMessage, previousPrice: number | null): PriceTick`
  - [ ] Parse `raw.bitcoin` string to number via `parseFloat()`
  - [ ] Use `Date.now()` as timestamp (CoinCap does not include a timestamp in price messages)
  - [ ] Compute direction same as Binance normalization: compare to `previousPrice`
  - [ ] Guard: skip update if `isNaN(price)` or `price <= 0`
  - [ ] Export `normalizeCoinCapMessage` for testing

- [ ] Task 3: Add fallback state tracking to ConnectionManager class (AC: #1, #5, #8)
  - [ ] Add private property `activeSource: 'binance' | 'coincap' | 'rest' = 'binance'`
  - [ ] Add private property `binanceRetryTimer: ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> | null = null`
  - [ ] Add private property `staleCheckTimer: ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> | null = null`
  - [ ] Add private property `lastPriceTimestamp: number = 0` (tracks when last price was received, for stale detection)

- [ ] Task 4: Implement `connectCoinCap()` private method (AC: #1, #2, #3, #4)
  - [ ] Call `cleanup()` first ‚Äî close any existing WebSocket
  - [ ] Set `this.activeSource = 'coincap'`
  - [ ] Create WebSocket: `this.ws = new WebSocket(WS_COINCAP_URL)`
  - [ ] Attach `onopen`: set `statusStore` to `'fallback'`, start Binance retry timer, start stale check timer
  - [ ] Attach `onmessage`: parse JSON, call `normalizeCoinCapMessage()`, write to `priceStore`, update `lastPriceTimestamp`
  - [ ] Attach `onerror`: `console.warn()` only, wrapped in try/catch
  - [ ] Attach `onclose`: attempt REST fallback via `enterRestMode()`
  - [ ] Wrap all handlers in try/catch (NFR16)

- [ ] Task 5: Implement `enterRestMode()` private method (AC: #5, #6, #7)
  - [ ] Call `cleanup()` ‚Äî close any existing WebSocket
  - [ ] Set `this.activeSource = 'rest'`
  - [ ] Set `statusStore` to `'stale'` (REST polling is slower, data may be delayed)
  - [ ] Start stale check timer to monitor `lastPriceTimestamp`
  - [ ] Start Binance retry timer to periodically attempt reconnection to higher-priority source
  - [ ] NOTE: this method does NOT start REST polling ‚Äî MarketDataService already polls CoinGecko independently (Story 3.1). The ConnectionManager reads from `priceStore` updates that MarketDataService may provide. In REST-only mode, the ConnectionManager is passive ‚Äî it just monitors staleness and retries WebSocket sources.

- [ ] Task 6: Implement stale detection timer (AC: #6)
  - [ ] Add private method `startStaleCheckTimer(): void`
  - [ ] Use recursive `setTimeout` (not `setInterval`) with 5-second check interval
  - [ ] On each check: if `Date.now() - lastPriceTimestamp > STALE_THRESHOLD_MS` and `statusStore.get() !== 'stale'`, set `statusStore` to `'stale'`
  - [ ] If a new price arrives (detected via `lastPriceTimestamp` update), transition back to appropriate status (`'fallback'` for CoinCap, `'live'` for Binance)
  - [ ] Add private method `clearStaleCheckTimer(): void`
  - [ ] Clear timer in `cleanup()` method

- [ ] Task 7: Implement Binance retry timer for reconnection to higher-priority source (AC: #8)
  - [ ] Add private method `startBinanceRetryTimer(): void`
  - [ ] Use `setTimeout` with `BINANCE_RETRY_INTERVAL_MS` (60s)
  - [ ] On fire: attempt `connect()` (Binance). If it succeeds (`onopen` fires), the fallback state is exited naturally. If it fails, the `onclose` handler routes back to CoinCap or REST.
  - [ ] Add private method `clearBinanceRetryTimer(): void`
  - [ ] Clear timer in `cleanup()` method

- [ ] Task 8: Modify `scheduleReconnect()` to integrate fallback chain (AC: #1, #4)
  - [ ] MODIFY `scheduleReconnect()` from Story 4.1
  - [ ] After backoff exhausts (reaches MAX_BACKOFF_MS and still failing), transition to CoinCap fallback:
    - If `activeSource === 'binance'`, call `connectCoinCap()` instead of retrying Binance again
    - If `activeSource === 'coincap'`, call `enterRestMode()` instead of retrying CoinCap
  - [ ] Track reconnection attempts: add private property `reconnectAttempts: number = 0`
  - [ ] Threshold: after `Math.ceil(Math.log2(MAX_BACKOFF_MS / INITIAL_BACKOFF_MS)) + 1` failed attempts (i.e., 6 attempts: 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s, 30s), escalate to next fallback level
  - [ ] Reset `reconnectAttempts` to 0 when switching to a new source

- [ ] Task 9: Modify `connect()` to track source and update price timestamp (AC: #3, #8)
  - [ ] MODIFY existing `connect()` from Story 2.2
  - [ ] Set `this.activeSource = 'binance'` at the start of `connect()`
  - [ ] Reset `this.reconnectAttempts = 0`

- [ ] Task 10: Modify `onmessage` handler to update `lastPriceTimestamp` (AC: #6)
  - [ ] MODIFY existing Binance `onmessage` handler from Story 2.2
  - [ ] ADD: `this.lastPriceTimestamp = Date.now()` after writing to `priceStore`

- [ ] Task 11: Modify `cleanup()` to clear all fallback timers (AC: #7)
  - [ ] MODIFY existing `cleanup()` from Story 4.1
  - [ ] ADD: `clearBinanceRetryTimer()` call
  - [ ] ADD: `clearStaleCheckTimer()` call
  - [ ] KEEP: existing `clearReconnectTimer()`, WebSocket close/null logic

- [ ] Task 12: Modify `disconnect()` to reset fallback state (AC: #7)
  - [ ] MODIFY existing `disconnect()` from Story 4.1
  - [ ] ADD: `this.activeSource = 'binance'` reset
  - [ ] ADD: `this.reconnectAttempts = 0` reset
  - [ ] ADD: `this.lastPriceTimestamp = 0` reset
  - [ ] KEEP: existing timer clears and cleanup call

- [ ] Task 13: Extend `connection-manager.test.ts` with fallback chain tests (AC: #9)
  - [ ] Test: `normalizeCoinCapMessage` produces correct `PriceTick` from `{ bitcoin: "68432.17" }`
  - [ ] Test: CoinCap normalization handles missing `bitcoin` key gracefully (skip update)
  - [ ] Test: CoinCap normalization handles non-numeric price string (skip update)
  - [ ] Test: after Binance backoff exhausts (6 attempts), transitions to CoinCap
  - [ ] Test: CoinCap `onopen` sets `statusStore` to `'fallback'`
  - [ ] Test: after CoinCap fails, transitions to REST mode with `statusStore` `'stale'`
  - [ ] Test: Binance retry timer fires after 60s in fallback/REST mode
  - [ ] Test: successful Binance reconnection from fallback returns `statusStore` to `'live'`
  - [ ] Test: stale check sets `statusStore` to `'stale'` when no price for 30s
  - [ ] Test: `disconnect()` clears all timers (reconnect, binanceRetry, staleCheck)
  - [ ] Test: full chain test: Binance ‚Üí backoff ‚Üí CoinCap ‚Üí backoff ‚Üí REST ‚Üí Binance retry ‚Üí success

## Dev Notes

### SCOPE BOUNDARY

**This story MODIFIES the ConnectionManager from Stories 2.2 and 4.1.**

- YES: CoinCap WebSocket fallback connection
- YES: CoinCap message normalization
- YES: REST fallback mode (passive ‚Äî relies on existing MarketDataService)
- YES: Fallback chain orchestration: Binance ‚Üí CoinCap ‚Üí REST
- YES: Stale detection (30s threshold) for `statusStore`
- YES: Binance reconnection from fallback mode
- NO status indicator UI ‚Äî that is Story 4.3
- NO stale badge on Price Card ‚Äî that is Story 4.3
- NO changes to `priceStore`, `statusStore`, or `MarketDataService` ‚Äî those exist from earlier stories
- NO changes to `PriceTick` or `ConnectionStatus` types ‚Äî those are from Story 1.1

### File Locations

- **Source (MODIFY):** `src/lib/services/connection-manager.ts`
- **Test (EXTEND):** `src/lib/services/connection-manager.test.ts`

### What Gets ADDED vs MODIFIED

**New constants (ADD):**
```typescript
const WS_COINCAP_URL = 'wss://ws.coincap.io/prices?assets=bitcoin';
const STALE_THRESHOLD_MS = 30000;     // 30 seconds
const BINANCE_RETRY_INTERVAL_MS = 60000; // 60 seconds
const MAX_RECONNECT_ATTEMPTS_BEFORE_FALLBACK = 6; // 1s + 2s + 4s + 8s + 16s + 30s
```

**New interface (ADD):**
```typescript
interface CoinCapPriceMessage {
  bitcoin?: string;
  [key: string]: string | undefined;
}
```

**New private properties (ADD to class):**
```typescript
private activeSource: 'binance' | 'coincap' | 'rest' = 'binance';
private reconnectAttempts: number = 0;
private binanceRetryTimer: ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> | null = null;
private staleCheckTimer: ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> | null = null;
private lastPriceTimestamp: number = 0;
```

**New exported function (ADD):**
```typescript
export function normalizeCoinCapMessage(
  raw: CoinCapPriceMessage,
  previousPrice: number | null
): PriceTick | null;
```

**New private methods (ADD to class):**
```typescript
private connectCoinCap(): void;
private enterRestMode(): void;
private startStaleCheckTimer(): void;
private clearStaleCheckTimer(): void;
private startBinanceRetryTimer(): void;
private clearBinanceRetryTimer(): void;
```

**Modified methods (MODIFY existing):**
- `connect()` ‚Äî ADD `activeSource = 'binance'`, `reconnectAttempts = 0`
- `scheduleReconnect()` ‚Äî ADD fallback escalation after max attempts
- `onmessage` handler (Binance) ‚Äî ADD `lastPriceTimestamp` update
- `cleanup()` ‚Äî ADD `clearBinanceRetryTimer()`, `clearStaleCheckTimer()` calls
- `disconnect()` ‚Äî ADD fallback state resets

### CoinCap WebSocket API Reference

**Endpoint:** `wss://ws.coincap.io/prices?assets=bitcoin`

**Connection:** Standard WebSocket ‚Äî no authentication, no API key required. Uses native browser `WebSocket` (not socket.io).

**Raw CoinCap Message Schema:**

```typescript
// CoinCap sends price updates as simple key-value JSON
// Keys are asset IDs (lowercase), values are price strings
interface CoinCapPriceMessage {
  bitcoin?: string;
  [key: string]: string | undefined;
}
```

**Example raw message:**
```json
{
  "bitcoin": "68432.17"
}
```

**Key differences from Binance:**
- CoinCap sends only price ‚Äî no trade ID, no quantity, no timestamps
- Messages arrive less frequently than Binance (typically every 1-2 seconds vs Binance's real-time per-trade)
- The `bitcoin` key uses the CoinCap asset ID (lowercase)
- Price is a string that may include varying decimal places

**Normalization (CoinCap ‚Üí PriceTick):**

```typescript
export function normalizeCoinCapMessage(
  raw: CoinCapPriceMessage,
  previousPrice: number | null
): PriceTick | null {
  const priceStr = raw.bitcoin;
  if (!priceStr) return null; // No bitcoin data in this message

  const price = parseFloat(priceStr);
  if (isNaN(price) || price <= 0) return null; // Invalid price

  const timestamp = Date.now(); // CoinCap doesn't provide timestamps

  let direction: PriceTick['direction'] = 'neutral';
  if (previousPrice !== null) {
    if (price > previousPrice) direction = 'up';
    else if (price < previousPrice) direction = 'down';
  }

  return { price, timestamp, direction };
}
```

### Complete Fallback Chain Logic

```
                    ‚îå‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îê
                    ‚îÇ     Start: connect() [Binance]   ‚îÇ
                    ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚î¨‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îò
                                   ‚îÇ
                    ‚îå‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚ñº‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îê
                    ‚îÇ   Binance WebSocket Connected    ‚îÇ
                    ‚îÇ   statusStore = 'live'           ‚îÇ
                    ‚îÇ   activeSource = 'binance'       ‚îÇ
                    ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚î¨‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îò
                                   ‚îÇ onclose
                    ‚îå‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚ñº‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îê
                    ‚îÇ   statusStore = 'reconnecting'   ‚îÇ
                    ‚îÇ   Exponential backoff (4.1)       ‚îÇ
                    ‚îÇ   1s ‚Üí 2s ‚Üí 4s ‚Üí 8s ‚Üí 16s ‚Üí 30s ‚îÇ
                    ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚î¨‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îò
                                   ‚îÇ 6 attempts exhausted
                    ‚îå‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚ñº‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îê
                    ‚îÇ   connectCoinCap()               ‚îÇ
                    ‚îÇ   statusStore = 'fallback'       ‚îÇ
                    ‚îÇ   activeSource = 'coincap'       ‚îÇ
                    ‚îÇ   Start Binance retry timer (60s)‚îÇ
                    ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚î¨‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îò
                                   ‚îÇ onclose
                    ‚îå‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚ñº‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îê
                    ‚îÇ   CoinCap backoff (reuse logic)  ‚îÇ
                    ‚îÇ   1s ‚Üí 2s ‚Üí 4s ‚Üí 8s ‚Üí 16s ‚Üí 30s ‚îÇ
                    ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚î¨‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îò
                                   ‚îÇ 6 attempts exhausted
                    ‚îå‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚ñº‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îê
                    ‚îÇ   enterRestMode()                ‚îÇ
                    ‚îÇ   statusStore = 'stale'          ‚îÇ
                    ‚îÇ   activeSource = 'rest'          ‚îÇ
                    ‚îÇ   Start Binance retry timer (60s)‚îÇ
                    ‚îÇ   Start stale check timer (5s)   ‚îÇ
                    ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚î¨‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îò
                                   ‚îÇ Binance retry timer fires
                    ‚îå‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚ñº‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îê
                    ‚îÇ   Retry connect() [Binance]      ‚îÇ
                    ‚îÇ   If success ‚Üí 'live'            ‚îÇ
                    ‚îÇ   If fail ‚Üí back to fallback     ‚îÇ
                    ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îò
```

### Fallback Chain: Modified `scheduleReconnect()`

```typescript
private scheduleReconnect(): void {
  this.clearReconnectTimer();
  this.cleanup();

  this.reconnectAttempts++;

  // Check if we should escalate to next fallback level
  if (this.reconnectAttempts > MAX_RECONNECT_ATTEMPTS_BEFORE_FALLBACK) {
    if (this.activeSource === 'binance') {
      this.connectCoinCap();
      return;
    } else if (this.activeSource === 'coincap') {
      this.enterRestMode();
      return;
    }
    // If activeSource is 'rest', we don't retry WebSocket from here
    return;
  }

  this.reconnectTimer = setTimeout(() => {
    this.reconnectTimer = null;
    if (this.activeSource === 'binance') {
      this.connect();
    } else if (this.activeSource === 'coincap') {
      this.connectCoinCap();
    }
  }, this.currentBackoff);

  this.currentBackoff = Math.min(
    this.currentBackoff * BACKOFF_MULTIPLIER,
    MAX_BACKOFF_MS
  );
}
```

### Status Store Transitions (This Story Adds)

```
'reconnecting' ‚Üí 'fallback'    (NEW: Binance exhausted, CoinCap connected)
'fallback' ‚Üí 'reconnecting'    (NEW: CoinCap dropped, retrying CoinCap)
'reconnecting' ‚Üí 'stale'       (NEW: both WebSockets exhausted, REST-only)
'stale' ‚Üí 'live'               (NEW: Binance retry from REST mode succeeds)
'fallback' ‚Üí 'live'            (NEW: Binance retry from CoinCap mode succeeds)
'stale' ‚Üí 'stale'              (implicit: stays stale during REST-only mode)
```

Complete status transition map (all stories combined):
```
'connecting' ‚Üí 'live'            (Story 2.2: initial Binance connection)
'live' ‚Üí 'reconnecting'          (Story 4.1: Binance drops)
'reconnecting' ‚Üí 'live'          (Story 4.1: Binance reconnects)
'reconnecting' ‚Üí 'fallback'      (Story 4.2: escalate to CoinCap)
'fallback' ‚Üí 'reconnecting'      (Story 4.2: CoinCap drops)
'reconnecting' ‚Üí 'stale'         (Story 4.2: escalate to REST)
'stale' ‚Üí 'live'                 (Story 4.2: Binance retry succeeds)
'fallback' ‚Üí 'live'              (Story 4.2: Binance retry succeeds)
```

### REST Fallback Mode ‚Äî How It Works

The REST fallback is **passive**. The ConnectionManager does NOT implement its own REST polling. Here's why:

- `MarketDataService` (Story 3.1) already polls CoinGecko REST API every 60 seconds
- CoinGecko returns current price data as part of `MarketData`
- In REST-only mode, the ConnectionManager:
  1. Closes all WebSocket connections
  2. Sets `statusStore` to `'stale'`
  3. Monitors `lastPriceTimestamp` for staleness
  4. Periodically retries Binance WebSocket (every 60s via `binanceRetryTimer`)
  5. Relies on `MarketDataService` to keep `marketStore` updated with price data

**Important:** The `priceStore` may not update during REST-only mode unless the Price Card or another component writes to it from `marketStore` data. This is acceptable per NFR15 ‚Äî the dashboard displays last known data.

### Memory Leak Prevention

This story adds three new timers. All must be properly managed:

1. **`binanceRetryTimer`** ‚Äî cleared in `clearBinanceRetryTimer()`, called from `cleanup()`
2. **`staleCheckTimer`** ‚Äî cleared in `clearStaleCheckTimer()`, called from `cleanup()`
3. **`reconnectTimer`** ‚Äî already managed by Story 4.1

All timer clear methods follow the same pattern:
```typescript
private clearBinanceRetryTimer(): void {
  if (this.binanceRetryTimer !== null) {
    clearTimeout(this.binanceRetryTimer);
    this.binanceRetryTimer = null;
  }
}
```

### Testing Strategy

Extend the existing test file. Use `vi.useFakeTimers()` for all timer-based tests.

**CoinCap normalization tests:**
```typescript
describe('normalizeCoinCapMessage', () => {
  it('normalizes a valid CoinCap message', () => {
    const raw = { bitcoin: '68432.17' };
    const result = normalizeCoinCapMessage(raw, null);
    expect(result).not.toBeNull();
    expect(result!.price).toBe(68432.17);
    expect(result!.direction).toBe('neutral');
  });

  it('returns null when bitcoin key is missing', () => {
    const raw = { ethereum: '2400.50' };
    const result = normalizeCoinCapMessage(raw as CoinCapPriceMessage, null);
    expect(result).toBeNull();
  });

  it('returns null for non-numeric price', () => {
    const raw = { bitcoin: 'invalid' };
    const result = normalizeCoinCapMessage(raw, null);
    expect(result).toBeNull();
  });

  it('computes direction based on previous price', () => {
    const raw = { bitcoin: '68500.00' };
    const result = normalizeCoinCapMessage(raw, 68432.17);
    expect(result!.direction).toBe('up');
  });
});
```

**Fallback chain integration test:**
```typescript
it('follows full fallback chain: Binance ‚Üí CoinCap ‚Üí REST', () => {
  const manager = ConnectionManager.getInstance();
  manager.connect();

  // Simulate Binance failure, exhaust backoff (6 attempts)
  for (let i = 0; i < 7; i++) {
    mockWs.onclose?.(new CloseEvent('close'));
    vi.runOnlyPendingTimers();
  }

  // Should now be on CoinCap
  expect(statusStore.get()).toBe('fallback');

  // Simulate CoinCap failure, exhaust backoff (6 attempts)
  for (let i = 0; i < 7; i++) {
    mockWs.onclose?.(new CloseEvent('close'));
    vi.runOnlyPendingTimers();
  }

  // Should now be in REST mode
  expect(statusStore.get()).toBe('stale');
});
```

### Anti-Patterns (DO NOT)

- Do NOT implement REST polling in ConnectionManager ‚Äî MarketDataService handles that (Story 3.1)
- Do NOT create a separate service for CoinCap ‚Äî it's managed by the same ConnectionManager singleton
- Do NOT modify `priceStore` or `statusStore` definitions ‚Äî those are from Story 2.1
- Do NOT modify `PriceTick` or `ConnectionStatus` types ‚Äî those are from Story 1.1
- Do NOT add UI elements ‚Äî status indicator is Story 4.3
- Do NOT use `addEventListener` for WebSocket events ‚Äî use property assignment
- Do NOT use `setInterval` for timers ‚Äî use `setTimeout` / recursive `setTimeout`
- Do NOT throw from any WebSocket or timer callback
- Do NOT add socket.io dependency ‚Äî CoinCap's `wss://ws.coincap.io/prices` endpoint works with native `WebSocket`

### Dependency on Stories 2.2 and 4.1

This story directly modifies `connection-manager.ts` as extended by Story 4.1. The following must exist before starting:

From Story 2.2:
- `ConnectionManager` singleton with `getInstance()`, `connect()`, `cleanup()`, `disconnect()`
- `normalizeBinanceMessage()` function
- `WS_BINANCE_URL` constant
- Store imports: `priceStore`, `statusStore`

From Story 4.1:
- `INITIAL_BACKOFF_MS`, `MAX_BACKOFF_MS`, `BACKOFF_MULTIPLIER` constants
- `reconnectTimer`, `currentBackoff` private properties
- `scheduleReconnect()`, `clearReconnectTimer()` private methods
- `onclose` handler triggers `statusStore.set('reconnecting')` and `scheduleReconnect()`
- `onopen` handler resets backoff

### Previous Story Intelligence

From Story 1.1:
- `ConnectionStatus` type includes `'fallback'` and `'stale'` as valid values
- TypeScript strict mode ‚Äî all properties must be initialized

From Story 2.1:
- `statusStore` is `atom<ConnectionStatus>('connecting')`
- `priceStore` is `atom<PriceTick | null>(null)`

From Story 2.2:
- `cleanup()` nulls all event handlers, calls `ws.close()`, sets `ws = null`
- `normalizeBinanceMessage` is exported for testing ‚Äî follow same pattern for `normalizeCoinCapMessage`

From Story 4.1:
- Backoff sequence: 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s, 30s (6 steps to reach cap)
- `scheduleReconnect()` handles backoff doubling and timer management
- `clearReconnectTimer()` pattern established for timer cleanup

### Constants

```typescript
const WS_COINCAP_URL = 'wss://ws.coincap.io/prices?assets=bitcoin';
const STALE_THRESHOLD_MS = 30000;     // 30 seconds ‚Äî architecture spec
const BINANCE_RETRY_INTERVAL_MS = 60000; // 60 seconds
const MAX_RECONNECT_ATTEMPTS_BEFORE_FALLBACK = 6;
```

### References

- [CoinCap WebSocket API](https://pro.coincap.io/api-docs) ‚Äî endpoint docs, message format
- [CoinCap GitHub](https://github.com/CoinCapDev/CoinCap.io) ‚Äî API documentation and examples
- [Source: architecture.md#API & Communication Patterns] ‚Äî fallback chain: Binance ‚Üí CoinCap, stale threshold 30s
- [Source: architecture.md#Communication Patterns] ‚Äî CoinCap format: `{ bitcoin: "68432.17" }` ‚Üí normalized `PriceTick`
- [Source: architecture.md#Gap Analysis Results] ‚Äî timer reference leak prevention
- [Source: architecture.md#Process Patterns] ‚Äî error handling: silent recovery, no thrown exceptions
- [Source: prd.md#FR12] ‚Äî CoinCap fallback WebSocket

- [Source: prd.md#NFR14] ‚Äî fallback completes without user intervention

- [Source: epics.md#Story 4.2] ‚Äî acceptance criteria

**File B:** `_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/3-3-24h-sparkline-chart-with-hover-tooltip.md`

Market Cap Card island component covering:

### Story B: `/Users/dmytroshamsiiev/Projects/metrics/_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/3-3-24h-sparkline-chart-with-hover-tooltip.md`

Chart Card with lightweight-charts v5 covering:
- Modifies `src/components/ChartCard.astro` to add chart initialization and store subscription
- Uses lightweight-charts v5 API: `import { createChart, AreaSeries } from 'lightweight-charts'` with `chart.addSeries(AreaSeries, options)` (NOT the v4 `addAreaSeries()`)
- All colors sourced from `CHART_COLORS` in `theme-constants.ts`
- `autoSize: true` for built-in resize handling via ResizeObserver
- Crosshair tooltip using `chart.subscribeCrosshairMove()` with `formatChartTooltip()` formatting
- Draw animation via CSS opacity transition (500ms ease-out) on container
- Incremental updates: `setData()` for initial load, `update()` for appending new points
- `ChartPoint.time` conversion from milliseconds to UTCTimestamp (seconds) for lightweight-charts
- Scroll/zoom disabled (`handleScroll: false`, `handleScale: false`) for sparkline behavior
- `aria-hidden="true"` on canvas elements
- Complete lightweight-charts v5 API reference section with exact code examples

content.text:
All 7 remaining stories created and sprint status is clean. Here's the final tally:

| Story | Description | Highlights |
|-------|-------------|------------|
| **2-4** | Tab Title Price Updates | Simple `priceStore.subscribe()` ‚Üí `document.title`, exact code included |
| **3-1** | Market Data Service (CoinGecko) | Two endpoints documented with response formats, recursive `setTimeout`, `AbortController` cleanup |
| **3-2** | Market Cap Card | Island subscribing to `marketStore`, `formatMarketCap()`/`formatRange()`, silent refresh |
| **3-3** | 24h Sparkline Chart | lightweight-charts v5 API (`addSeries(AreaSeries)`), `CHART_COLORS` from `theme-constants.ts`, crosshair tooltip |
| **4-1** | WebSocket Reconnection | Exponential backoff (1s‚Üí30s cap), extends existing ConnectionManager, timer cleanup |
| **4-2** | CoinCap + REST Fallback | Full chain Binance‚ÜíCoinCap‚ÜíREST, CoinCap message format verified, stale detection at 30s |
| **4-3** | Status Indicator + Stale Badge | Pulse animation, 5-state mapping, touches both StatusIndicator AND PriceCard |

**All 12 stories across 4 epics are now `ready-for-dev`.** The entire project backlog is contexted and ready for implementation.
